ingredients.raw: 2 ¾ teaspoons instant or active dry yeast

ingredients.note: Can use all-purpose flour but bagels will be much chewier

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon granulated sugar or packed brown sugar
Nonstick spray or 2 teaspoons olive oil to coat bowl
Egg wash: 1 egg white beaten with 1 Tablespoon water
Everything but the bagel seasoning or other toppings if desired (such as cinnamon sugar)

ingredients.note: In the morning, remove the dough from the refrigerator and let the dough rise for 45 minutes at room temperature

ingredients.raw: You want the bagels to be a dark golden brown

instructions.text: Prepare the dough by whisking the warm water and yeast together in the bowl of your stand mixer fitted with a dough hook attachment
Cover and allow to sit for 5 minutes
Add the flour, brown sugar, and salt and beat on low speed for 2 minutes
Knead the dough for 4-5 minutes
Lightly grease a large bowl with oil or nonstick spray
Place the dough in the bowl, turning it to coat all sides in the oil
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and allow the dough to rise:
Line two large baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats
When the dough is ready, punch it down to release any air bubbles
To shape the dough, divide it into 8 equal pieces, then shape each piece into a ball
Press your index finger through the center of each ball to make a hole about 1.5 – 2 inches in diameter
Loosely cover the shaped bagels with kitchen towel and rest for a few minutes as you prepare the water bath
Preheat oven to 425°F convection
To prepare water bath fill a large, wide pot with 2 quarts of water, then whisk in the honey
Bring water to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-high
Drop bagels in boiling water 2-4 at a time, making sure they have enough room to float around and cook them in the water for 1 minute on each side
Remove bagels from the pot and drain excess water from bagels
Place bagels on lined baking sheet
Using a pastry brush, brush the egg wash on top and around the sides of each bagel
If using, sprinkle on toppings
Bake for 20-25 minutes, rotating the pan halfway through
Remove from the oven and allow bagels to cool on the baking sheets for 20 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely
Cover leftover bagels tightly and store at room temperature for a few days or in the refrigerator for up to 1 week

recipes.title: Bagel Bites Stuffed with Cream Cheese
notes: Store at room temperature for 1-2 days or in the freezer for longer storage

ingredients.raw: 4 cups (482g) King Arthur Unbleached Bread Flour
12 ounces (1 ½ packages) cream cheese, frozen in 1 tablespoon balls

instructions.text: Combine all dough ingredients, and mix and knead (by hand, mixer or bread machine) until a very soft, smooth dough forms, adding more flour or water if needed
Allow the dough to rise, covered, until puffy, about 1 ½ hours
While the dough is rising, prep cream cheese by freezing it into 1 tablespoon balls
Turn the dough out onto a lightly greased work surface and divide it into 20 equal pieces
Round each piece of dough into a ball
Cover the balls lightly and let them rest for 10-15 minutes
Meanwhile, prepare the water bath by heating the water and sugars to a very gentle boil in a large, wide-diameter pan
Preheat the oven to 425°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper
Gently flatten dough balls slightly and place a frozen ball of filling in the center of each
Wrap the edges up around the filling, pinching them together as tightly as you can, and round the parcel into a ball again, then place the bagel bites onto the baking sheet
Transfer the bagel bites, three or four at a time if possible, to the simmering water and cook the bagel bites for 30 seconds, flip them over and cook 30 seconds longer
Using a skimmer or strainer, remove the bagel bites from the water and place them back on the sheet pan, then repeat with the remaining bagel bites
Brush bagel bites with egg wash and top with topping of your choice or leave them plain
Bake the bagel bites for 20-25 minutes, until they’re golden brown
Remove bagel bites from oven and allow them to cool for at least 30 minutes

recipes.title: Baked Eggs with Sausage and Mushrooms

ingredients.raw: 8 ounces pound cremini mushrooms, stemmed and sliced
2 sage leaves, finely chopped or 1/4 teaspoon ground sage

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F
Coat a large saute pan with olive oil over medium heat
Saute the garlic cloves and crushed red pepper
When the garlic has become golden brown and very aromatic, remove it and discard it
Add the sausage and cook until browned
Once nicely browned, add the shallots and mushrooms and season with kosher salt
When the mushrooms have softened, add in the sage and white wine
Cook until the wine has almost been absorbed, then turn off the heat and set aside
Brush 4 individual ramekins with olive oil
Divide the sausage mixture equally among the ramekins and press gently into the bottom
Top each ramekin with a cracked egg
Sprinkle each egg with a couple drops of olive oil and arrange on a baking sheet
Bake for 8 minutes
Sprinkle each egg lightly with Gruyere cheese and bake until the cheese is brown and bubbly and the egg whites are cooked and the yolks are still runny, about 7 minutes
Serve with grilled bread rubbed with garlic, if desired

ingredients.raw: 1 ¾ cups (210g) sifted all purpose flour

ingredients.note: It’s okay to be over, but try not to be under 1 cup

ingredients.raw: 2 minutes at medium speed on electric mixer

instructions.text: Combine flour, baking powder cinnamon and salt in a medium bowl
Beat butter, sugar, vanilla and eggs until creamy
Beat in the sour cream
Gradually add in flour mixture and blend well
Add bananas and mix thoroughly until blended
Mix in chocolate chips
Spray loaf pan and pour batter into pan
Bake in oven at 350°F for 45-50 minutes until brown on top
Let bread partially cool in pan for about 10-20 minutes before removing

recipes.title: Biscuits
notes: Makes 8 small biscuits or 6 larger ones (good for sandwiches)*

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (115g) unsalted butter, frozen and grated
1 cup plus 2 Tablespoons (270ml) cold buttermilk, divided
1 cup shredded cheddar cheese (optional - good to make savory)
optional for spreading: 2 Tablespoons melted butter + 1 Tablespoon honey
The dough will look like shreds and be very crumbly
If you like"crusty" sides, put them about 1 inch apart- these will not rise as high as the biscuits put close together

instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 425°F
Combine the dry ingredients in a bowl, whisk to consolidate and place in the freezer
Grate the frozen butter and put back in the freezer to firm up - about 5 minutes
Combine the butter into the flour mixture and mix by hand, crumbling the butter into the flour mixture until it is coarse and pebble like
Add cheddar cheese if using
Add 1 cup of the buttermilk and the 2 teaspoons of honey and mix JUST until combined
Turn the dough out onto a floured board
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured work surface and gently mold it into a rough looking rectangle using your hands
Fold one side into the center, then the other side, then turn the dough so it’s long horizontally, then gently flatten
Repeat the folding again
Turn the dough so it’s long horizontally once more, then gently flatten
Repeat the folding one more time
Fold the dough about 5 times, gently press the dough down to 1 inch thick
Gently roll the dough out with a rolling pin until it’s about 3/4 inch thick
Cut into 3-inch circles - do not twist the cutter
Re-roll any scraps until you have 9-10 biscuits, then arrange in a 10-inch cast iron skillet or close together on a parchment paper-lined baking sheet (Make sure they’re touching)
Brush the tops with remaining buttermilk
Bake for 15 minutes or until biscuits are golden brown on top
Remove from the oven and, if desired, brush with melted butter and honey mixture

ingredients.raw: 4 ½ - 5 cups (540g to 600g) King Arthur Unbleached Bread Flour
Yellow cornmeal, for coating the pan (optional)
Mix until everything comes together in a rough, shaggy mass of dough
When fully kneaded, the dough will be bouncy and smooth
If the dough doesn’t form a ball that clears the sides of the bowl, sprinkle in just enough of the additional flour to make this happen.
The bowl you started with is fine
Or let rise in the refrigerator overnight up to 12 hours
Gently poke your index finger into the side of one of the loaves; if the indentation remains, your bread is ready to bake
Wear good oven mitts during this process to shield your hands and arms from the steam
The interior temperature of the bread should register at least 190°F on a digital thermometer
This helps keep the crust crisp
It’s best not to cut into the bread until it’s cooled down a bit; cutting into hot bread can negatively affect its texture

instructions.text: Using a sturdy spoon, stir together all of the ingredients (except the cornmeal) in a large bowl, starting with 4 ½ cups of the flour
If you’re kneading the dough by hand, turn it out onto a lightly floured surface, using some of the additional 1/2 cup of flour called for
Fold the far edge of the dough back over on itself towards you, then press it away from you with the heels of your hands, then rotate the dough 90°
Repeat this fold-press-rotate process with a rhythmic, rocking motion for about 6 minutes
If you’re using your stand mixer, use the dough hook and knead the dough at medium speed for about 7 minutes, until it’s smooth, elastic, and feels a bit bouncy
Place the dough in a bowl that’s been lightly greased with vegetable oil or cooking spray
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or another airtight cover, and let the dough rise at room temperature until it's doubled in size, about 1-2 hours
Gently deflate the dough and cut it in half
Pat each half into a rough 6” x 8” oval
Working with one piece of dough at a time, grab a short side and fold the dough like a business letter (one short side into the center, the other short side over it)
Use the heel of your hand to press the open edge of the “letter” closed
Gently pat and roll the dough into a log about 10” long
Repeat with the remaining piece of dough
Place the loaves, seam-side down, on a baking sheet lined with parchment sprinkled with some flour or cornmeal
Let the loaves rise, lightly covered with greased plastic wrap, for 45 minutes
Towards the end of the rising time, preheat the oven to 450°F

For extra-crusty crust and a great rise, add steam to your oven as follows: While the oven is preheating, place an empty cast-iron frying pan on the lowest rack, then bring 2 cups of water to a boil in the microwave or on the stovetop
When your bread is risen, use a sieve to dust the loaves with a thin coat of flour
Then make three or four 1/2” deep diagonal slashes in each loaf; these slashes will help the bread rise evenly as it bakes
Place the bread in the oven and pour the boiling water into the frying pan below, then quickly shut the oven door
Bake the bread for 20-25 minutes, until the crust is golden brown and a loaf sounds hollow to the touch when you tap it on the bottom
Turn the oven off, crack the door open, and allow the bread to remain inside for 5 additional minutes
Remove the bread from the oven and cool it on a rack
Store the bread, well-wrapped, at room temperature for a couple of days

ingredients.raw: 1 pizza crust (Store bought or 1/2 recipe of pizza dough)

instructions.text: Heat oven to 475°F and grease a rimmed baking sheet with 1 tablespoon of the oil
Cook bacon until crisp, crumble and set aside
Combine mozzarella and parmesan cheese in a bowl and set aside
Optional: blend cottage cheese in food processor or using hand blender until smooth
Combine cottage cheese, oregano, 1/4 teaspoon pepper and 1 tablespoon oil in a bowl and set aside
Press and roll dough into a 11x15 inch rectangle onto a lightly floured counter
Transfer dough to prepared baking sheet and stretch out to edges (if possible)
Brush edges with remaining olive oil
Bake dough for about 5 minutes until the top appears dry
Remove crust from oven, and press down any air bubbles
Spread cottage cheese mixture evenly over the top leaving a crust around the edges
Sprinkle bacon over the cottage cheese, then the mozzarella mixture, then add any additional toppings
Create 2 rows of 3 evenly spaced wells in the cheese
Crack each egg into each well and sprinkle with salt and pepper
Return pizza to oven and bake for about 10 minutes for runny yolks or 12 minutes for set yolks
Remove from oven and cool for 5 minutes
Cut into 6 slices and serve

ingredients.raw: 2 ½ teaspoons (7g) instant yeast (1 standard size packet)
1/4 cup (57g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature and cut into 4 pieces
2 ¾ cups (345g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting/ rolling
3 tablespoons (45g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
2 tablespoons milk (can substitute with heavy cream or half and half)
If you do not have a stand-mixer with a hook attachment, knead the dough by hand in this step
Make sure the dough is smooth and evenly thick
If some filling spills out, that’s ok just sprinkle it on top of the rolls

instructions.text: To make the dough: Whisk the flour, sugar, salt, and yeast together into the bowl of a stand electric mixer fitted with the dough hook attachment
Combine the milk and butter together in a heatproof bowl
Microwave or use the stove and heat until the butter is melted and the mixture is warm to touch (about 110°F)
Pour into the dry ingredients, add the egg, and mix on low speed
Once it is all added, beat on medium speed until a soft dough forms
Increase speed to medium-high and beat until the dough is soft and supple, about 6 minutes longer
Transfer the dough to a lightly greased bowl
Cover the dough loosely with plastic wrap, a paper towel, or aluminum foil and let sit in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1-2 hours
Grease the bottom and sides of a 9-inch round cake pan or pie dish and line with parchment paper
Turn the risen dough out onto a lightly floured work surface, punch it down and, using a rolling pin, roll into a 14×8 inch rectangle
To make the filling: Spread the softened butter all over the dough
In a small bowl, toss the cinnamon and sugar together until combined and then sprinkle evenly over the dough
Tightly roll up the dough to form an 14-inch-long log
Cut into 10 even rolls
Arrange them in the prepared pan, cut sides up
Cover the rolls very tightly with aluminum foil (no rolls exposed– this dries out your dough!) and stick in the refrigerator for 8-12 hours
Remove rolls from the refrigerator and let rise just as you did in step 2 until they are puffy, about 1-2 hours
After the rolls have risen, preheat the oven to 375°F
Bake for about 25-30 minutes, until they are golden brown
About halfway through the bake time, cover the rolls loosely with aluminum foil so the tops don’t brown too much
Remove pan from the oven and allow to cool on a wire rack for about 10 minutes as you make the icing
Combine powdered sugar, melted butter, vanilla and milk in a large mixing bowl until smooth
Pour over warm rolls and spread

ingredients.raw: 2 cups (240g) white self-rising flour (if using all purpose flour add 3 teaspoons baking powder and ½ teaspoon salt)
1 ½ cups plain fat free Greek yogurt
Or follow this recipe for another glaze:
ingredients.note: 1/2 cup powdered sugar 2 tablespoons plain fat free Greek yogurt 1/4 teaspoon vanilla extract

ingredients.raw: If dough is too sticky to knead, add more flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, until it is no longer sticky

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Combine flour and powdered sugar in a large bowl
Whisk together yogurt, vanilla and egg in a medium bowl
Add yogurt mixture to flour mixture and stir well with a wooden spoon until just combined and then keep mixing dough in bowl with your hands until smooth (about 2 minutes of kneading)
Place a large piece of parchment paper on a work surface and sprinkle with a little flour
Place dough on parchment paper, and roll into a 12 x 9–inch rectangle
Sprinkle evenly with brown sugar and cinnamon, all the way to the edges
Starting with a long edge, roll up dough tightly (use parchment paper to help roll up dough) to form a 12-inch-long roll
Cut roll into 12 (1-inch) slices
Arrange slices in a 9x9 inch baking dish lined with overlapping parchment paper and coated with cooking spray
Bake until lightly browned, 22-25 minutes
Let stand 10 minutes before topping with glaze
Make icing or combine glaze ingredients stirring until smooth
Drizzle glaze over cinnamon rolls and serve

ingredients.raw: 12 tablespoons (1½ sticks) unsalted butter, at room temperature
1¼ cups sour cream, at room temperature
2½ cups cake flour ( or 2¼ cups all-purpose flour plus ¼ cup cornstarch)
Optional add in - blueberries or chocolate chips
1/4 cup light brown sugar, lightly packed
Optional: 3/4 cup chopped walnuts or pecans

instructions.text: Ingredients (can be halved):
Preheat the oven to 350°F then grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan or a loaf pan if only making half
Cream the butter and sugar in the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with the paddle attachment for 4 to 5 minutes, until light
Add the eggs one at a time, then add the vanilla and sour cream
In a separate bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt
With the mixer on low, add the flour mixture to the batter until just combined
Finish stirring with a spatula to be sure the batter is well mixed
For the streusel, place the brown sugar, flour, cinnamon, salt, and butter in a bowl and pinch together with your fingers until it forms a crumble, then mix in the walnuts
Spoon half the batter into the pan and spread it out with a knife
Sprinkle with 3/4 cup streusel
Spoon the rest of the batter in the pan, spread it out, and scatter the remaining streusel on top
Bake for 45-55 minutes, until a cake tester comes out clean
Allow to cool on a wire rack for 30 minutes
Carefully transfer the cake, streusel-side up, onto a serving plate

recipes.title: Crepes
notes: If you have extra crepes, you can store in sealable plastic bags (again, separating with tin foil) in the refrigerator for several days or in the freezer for up to two months

ingredients.raw: Can let sit in fridge overnight if making the night before
Can use right away if in a time crunch, it is just not ideal
I find the best way to do this is to keep the butter in the wrapper, hold the stick of butter and coat the pan with it
Layer crepes with tin foil or anything in between so they do not stick to one another
Take out the ricotta crepe before others
When using frozen crepes, thaw on a rack before gently peeling apart

instructions.text: Mix all ingredients in a blender for about 10 seconds
Let mix sit for about an hour to get rid of the bubbles so crepes are less likely to tear
Heat a small non-stick pan and add butter to coat
Pour 1/4 cup of batter into the center of the pan and swirl to spread evenly
Cook for about 30 seconds or until the edges are dry and flip
Cook for another 10 seconds and remove from the pan
Lay them out flat so they can cool and continue until all batter is gone
Once you make another crepe, the previous one should have cooled so you can start stacking the crepes
Add whatever filling you want into the crepe
Fold the sides of the crepe in
Place crepes on cookie sheets and in the oven at 250°F for 5-10 minutes

ingredients.note: Should buy ricotta that only has milk, salt and vinegar (no pastes!)

ingredients.raw: Orange zest (about ½ an orange worth)
Sausage gravy (optional - store bought or see recipe for homemade)

instructions.text: Nutella, strawberry & banana
Ricotta chocolate chip
To drain ricotta:
Mix all ingredients together in a bowl
Add all ingredients to center of crepe and fold both sides in
Breakfast crepe
Small dice potatoes, onion, tomato, avocado and bacon
Microwave potatoes for 1 ½ minutes to soften
Add diced onions to the bowl with potatoes
Follow the rest of the instructions in the roasted potato recipe
On one half of the crepe add potatoes and onions, followed by cheese, tomatoes, bacon, avocado, top with some more cheese, fold the other side in, and heat in oven at 250°F
If desired, drizzle sausage gravy over the crepe
Cook egg as desired and place on top of crepe

recipes.title: Challah
notes: place eggs in bowl with warm water to get to this temp if refrigerated

ingredients.raw: 4 1/2- 5 cups (550+ grams) all-purpose flour (or half bread flour), spooned into measuring cup and leveled-off, plus more for dusting
2 teaspoons instant yeast or 1 packet active dry yeast

ingredients.note: 1 egg 1 tablespoon of water 1/4 teaspoon salt

ingredients.raw: The dough may seem too wet, but it’s supposed to be
If the ropes shrink a bit, just work them back into their original length
Note: the loaf will continue to rise significantly in the oven
ingredients.note: This area tends to expand during baking, exposing areas that will turn white unless they are coated with egg wash

instructions.text: If using yeast packet:
If using instant yeast:
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and knead on medium-low speed until you have a sticky dough that clings to the bottom of the bowl, about 5-7 minutes
Dust your hands generously with flour, then scrape the sticky, elastic dough out onto a lightly floured work surface
Dust the top of the dough lightly with flour and knead briefly into a soft, smooth ball
Lightly grease a large bowl with oil (can use spray)
Place the dough in the greased bowl, and flip it over once so the top is lightly oiled
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and set in a warm place to rise.
Let the dough rise for 1-2 hours, or until the dough doubles in size (it may take longer)
Take the dough bowl out and punch it down several times to remove air pockets
Place it back inside oven and let it rise for 1 hour longer, or until dough doubles in size (it may take longer)
If not cooking until the next day, place dough in fridge and keep covered with plastic wrap
If keeping it in the fridge, remove it from the fridge the next day and let it sit out for about an hour to come to room temperature
Flour a smooth surface like a cutting board
Punch the dough down into the bowl a few times, then turn the dough out onto the floured surface
Knead for a few minutes, adding flour as needed to keep the dough from feeling sticky
Cut the dough into four even pieces, and then stretch and roll each piece into a rope about 17-20 inches long
Lay the ropes parallel to one another (vertically)
Pinch them tightly together at the top, and then fan them out
Begin by taking the strand farthest to the right/ back and weave it toward the left/ front through the other strands using this pattern: over, under, over
Take the strand furthest to the right and repeat the weaving pattern again: over, under, over
Continue repeating this pattern, always starting with the strand farthest to the right, until the whole loaf is braided
Tuck the ends under to give the loaf a finished look
Carefully transfer the braided loaf to a parchment-lined 13 x 18-inch baking sheet
Cover the loaf loosely with lightly oiled plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm, draft-free spot until it’s about 1.5 times the size, about 1 hour
Toward the end of the rising time, preheat the oven to 350°F and set an oven rack in the middle position
Prepare your egg wash by beating the egg, salt and water till smooth
Use a pastry brush to brush a thin layer of the mixture onto the visible surface of your challah and set the leftover egg wash aside for later
Place the baking sheet on top of another baking sheet to prevent the bottom crust from browning too much
The challah needs to bake for about 40 minutes total, but to get the best result the baking should be done in stages
Turn the tray around, so the opposite side is facing front, and put the tray back into the oven
Bake for another 15-20 minutes, until the crust is a rich brown color and the internal temperature is between 190-200°F on an instant-read thermometer
Remove the bread from the oven and place it on a rack to cool

recipes.title: Cinnamon Sugar filling for a Cinnamon Swirl Challah

ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour

instructions.text: Follow the recipe for regular challah above through step 17
Combine the ingredients and mix
During Step 18 roll each strip about 2-3 inches wide
Sprinkle filling onto strip and roll each piece to seal the filling inside before braiding and then continue following above recipe

recipes.title: Apple Cinnamon filling for Apple Honey Challah

instructions.text: Follow the recipe for regular challah above through step 17
Combine the ingredients and mix
During Step 18 roll each strip about 2-3 inches wide
Sprinkle filling onto strip and roll each piece to seal the filling inside before braiding and then continue following above recipe
Follow the recipe for regular challah above through step 17 - omit the vanilla
Combine the ingredients and mix
During Step 18 roll each strip about 2-3 inches wide
Brush each strip with melted butter
Sprinkle filling onto strip and roll each piece to seal the filling inside before braiding and then continue following above recipe
Top with Everything but the bagel seasoning after brushing with egg wash

recipes.title: Cheese Latkes
notes: Latkes can be eaten plain or topped with a drizzle of honey
Other toppings include jam or preserves, sour cream, maple syrup, yogurt or agave nectar

ingredients.raw: 1 cup high quality whole milk ricotta cheese
Non-stick cooking oil spray, for frying
Test the first latke for doneness and make sure it’s cooked all the way through
If the latkes are browning faster than they’re cooking, reduce skillet heat
Expect some variation in the shape of the latkes, as they won't form a perfect circle

instructions.text: Combine all ingredients except the non-stick oil in a food processor and process the mixture for about 45 seconds, pausing a couple of times to scrape the sides, until the mixture forms a thick batter
Spray a skillet with nonstick cooking oil and heat over medium

Use a spoon to scoop up about 1-2 tablespoons of the batter, then pour it onto the hot skillet in the size/shape of silver dollar pancakes, then spread it out into a thin circle after it hits the skillet
Fry the latkes for 2-3 minutes on each side until they turn golden brown

recipes.title: Dutch Baby (Apple Pancake)
notes: Haven’t tried yet - Ina Garten’s recipe*
https://www.foodnetwork.com/recipes/ina-garten/dutch-baby-12055315

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F
Peel, core, and slice the apple 1/8 inch thick
Melt 3 tablespoons of the butter in a 9-inch ovenproof crêpe pan or skillet over medium to medium-high heat
Add the apple, 1 tablespoon of the granulated sugar, and the cinnamon and toss well
Sauté the apple for 5-7 minutes until tender, tossing occasionally
Spread the apple out in the pan and keep hot over low heat
Meanwhile, melt the remaining tablespoon of butter in the microwave
In a medium bowl, whisk together the milk, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla
In a small bowl, combine the flour, the remaining tablespoon of granulated sugar, and the salt
Whisk the flour mixture into the milk mixture, whisking until there are no lumps
Pour batter over the cooked apple slices in the hot pan and immediately place in the oven
Bake for 12-15 minutes, until the pancake is puffed and the edges start to get crispy and browned
Dust lightly with sifted confectioners’ sugar, cut in wedges, and serve hot with maple syrup
Prepare taco shells by first spreading a thin layer of pesto followed by smashed avocado on each tortilla
Spray a medium size pan with cooking spray
Sprinkle feta cheese throughout pan
Turn on stove to medium heat and heat feta just until bubbly
Once feta begins to bubble, crack in 2 eggs
Cover pan and cook for about 3 minutes until eggs whites of the egg are cooked
Once eggs are cooked, turn off heat, and place one egg into each taco shell
If desired, drizzle a light layer of the chili onion crunch into each taco shell
Serve and enjoy

ingredients.raw: 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon, plus more to sprinkle

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 200°F
In a large shallow dish, whisk together the eggs, milk, vanilla, and cinnamon
Soak bread slices in mixture, turning once
Heat a griddle or frying pan on medium heat
Spray with no-stick spray or melt butter in a pan
Add the soaked bread, sprinkling the top with a little extra cinnamon, then cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until nicely browned
Place the cooked French toast on a sheet pan and keep it warm in the preheated oven

ingredients.note: If using frozen strawberries, make sure to drain the liquid

instructions.text: Combine cream cheese, sour cream, vanilla and powdered sugar in a bowl and beat with a hand mixer or by hand for about 1-2 minutes until creamy and fluffy
Combine the strawberries and sugar in another bowl and set aside
Start to prepare the French Toast, at least 2 pieces at a time
After cooking the first side and flipping, spread the cream cheese mixture on the cooked side of each slice of bread
Spread 1/3 of the strawberries over one of the slices of bread cream cheese mixture
When the french toast is finished cooking, flip the non-strawberry slice on top of the strawberry covered slice and serve
Sprinkle with powdered sugar and/ or warm strawberry sauce (see recipe)

ingredients.raw: 1 pound loaf of french bread or cinnamon bread

instructions.text: Spray the bottom and sides of a 9×13-inch baking dish with non-stick spray
Cut bread into 1 inch chunks and place in dish
Crack the eggs into a large mixing bowl and whisk to combine
Add the milk, brown sugar, cinnamon, salt and vanilla and mix well
Pour evenly over the bread cubes
Gently toss the bread around a little to make sure the top and bottom pieces all get dipped in the egg mixture
Spread out evenly, cover dish, and refrigerate overnight
To make the topping: Place brown sugar, white sugar, cinnamon and nutmeg in a bowl and stir to combine
Cut cold butter into small chunks and add to the bowl
Use a pastry cutter or your hands to combine the mixture until crumbly
Cover and refrigerate topping until morning
When ready to bake, preheat oven to 350°F
Uncover your french toast pan and sprinkle the topping evenly over top
Bake for 40-50 minutes or until top is golden brown and egg mixture is set at the bottom
Allow french toast to cool for 5-10 minutes before cutting it into squares

ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons dark brown sugar
1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons maple syrup or honey

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 300°F
In a large bowl, combine the oats, nuts, cinnamon and brown sugar
In a separate bowl, combine maple syrup, oil, and salt
Combine both mixtures and pour onto a sheet pan lined with parchment paper
Cook for approximately 30-35 minutes until golden brown
Remove from oven and let cool completely before crumbling and putting into an airtight container

ingredients.raw: Desired fruit (strawberries, bananas, blackberries, blueberries, raspberries, mango, pineapple, etc.)
Any other desired toppings (coconut, agave, almonds, chia seeds, etc.)
Suggest using a food processor because there is little liquid
Could use a pastry bag to drizzle peanut butter and nutella

instructions.text: Blend ingredients for base together
Separate into 4 bowls, approximately 1 cup each
Sprinkle with granola
Add fruit
Top with peanut butter, nutella and/ or any other desired toppings

recipes.title: Crumb topping for Muffins and Breads

instructions.text: Ingredients
Combined the sugars, cinnamon and butter in a bowl
Stir in the flour to a thick and crumbly consistency
Crumble with hands and press lightly onto batter before baking

recipes.title: Apple Muffins
notes: If using all regular flour reduce liquid by 2 teaspoons

ingredients.raw: 8 tablespoons (113g) unsalted butter, at room temperature
1 cup (227g) buttermilk or plain (not Greek-style) yogurt; or 3/4 cup (170g) Greek-style yogurt + 1/4 cup (57g) milk
1 cup (113g) King Arthur White Whole Wheat Flour (can use all regular flour)*
1 cup (120g) King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour

instructions.text: Ingredients
Make the crumb topping and set aside
Preheat the oven to 375°F
Grease and flour a 12-cup muffin pan, or line with papers and grease the insides of the papers
Mix together the butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar, beating until fluffy
Add the egg and mix well, stopping once to scrape the sides and bottom of the bowl
Gently mix in the buttermilk or yogurt
Stir in the flours, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon and pie spice
Fold in the chopped apples
Divide the batter evenly among the prepared muffin cups (about a heaping 1/4 cup)
Press a handful of the crumb topping into the top of each, then crumble it with your hands to make some big chunks
Bake the muffins for 25-30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a muffin comes out clean
Remove the muffins from the oven, cool them for 5 minutes in the pan, then turn them out onto a rack to finish cooling completely

recipes.title: Blueberry Muffins
notes: The total time these muffins take in the oven is about 22-23 minutes, give or take

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
3/4 cup (150g) packed light brown sugar (or granulated)
1/2 cup (120g) sour cream or plain yogurt, at room temperature
1/4 cup milk (any kind), at room temperature
1 ½ cups fresh or frozen blueberries
Scrape down the sides and bottom of the bowl as needed
Scrape down the sides and bottom of the bowl as needed

instructions.text: Ingredients
Preheat oven to 425°F
Spray a 12-count muffin pan with nonstick spray or line with cupcake liners, then set aside
In a large bowl, whisk the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt together, then set aside
In another large bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter on high speed until smooth and creamy, about 1 minute
Add the brown sugar and beat on high until creamed, about 2 full minutes
Add the eggs, sour cream, and vanilla extract and beat on medium speed for 1 minute, then turn up to high speed until the mixture is combined and creamy
Pour the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients and beat on low speed until just about combined
Add the milk and continue to beat on low until combined
Fold in the blueberries
Spoon the batter evenly into each cup or liner, filling each all the way to the top
Bake the muffins for 5 minutes at 425°F then, keeping the muffins in the oven, reduce the oven temperature to 350°F
Bake for an additional 15-18 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean
Allow the muffins to cool for 5 minutes in the muffin pan, then transfer to a wire rack to continue cooling

recipes.title: Overnight Oats
notes: I suggest using a 16 ounce mason jar with a wide mouth*
Can last in refrigerator for up to 4 days*

ingredients.raw: 1 banana, plus another one to top if desired
Mini chocolate chips or Nutella (or both - optional)
Easiest to make this recipe in a larger bowl then pour into mason jar

instructions.text: Mash up 1 banana with a fork
Add ingredients from base listed at the top of the page to mason jar or bowl
Add mashed banana, honey, cinnamon and salt and mix well
If desired, add nutella and mix until combined
Tighten the lid on the jar, place in the fridge and let it sit for at least 4 hours to overnight
When ready to eat, top with slices of banana and sprinkle in some chocolate chips

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon honey or pure maple syrup (whichever you prefer)
1/2 teaspoon pie spice (can substitute with cinnamon if needed)

instructions.text: Add ingredients from base listed at the top of the page to mason jar or bowl
Add honey/ syrup and pie spice and mix well
If desired, add peanut butter and mix until combined
Peel apple, if desired, and dice
Add apples to rest of mix and give it a shake/ mix
Tighten the lid on the jar, place in the fridge and let it sit for at least 4 hours to overnight

ingredients.note: Can substitute for yogurt and sugar

ingredients.raw: 2 medium apples, peeled and coarsely grated

instructions.text: Whisk the eggs with the milk or yogurt and sugar in a large bowl
In a smaller bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, cinnamon and salt
Combine the wet and the dry ingredients and stir in the apples
Heat a thin layer of oil in a skillet over low to medium heat
Drop large spoonful of batter into the pan and flatten it out a little (otherwise, you might have trouble getting them to cook in the center) and cook until golden brown underneath
Flip the pancakes and cook them for an additional two or three minutes
Either dust with powdered sugar and serve immediately, or keep on a tray in a warmed oven until you are ready to serve them

recipes.title: Mini Dutch Baby Pancakes
notes: If using apple mixture from dutch baby recipe, divide apples among the muffin tins prior to adding the batter*

ingredients.raw: 6 tablespoons unsalted butter cut into 12 cubes
Powdered sugar or jam, for serving

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F
Whisk together flour, salt, and cinnamon
Add the milk and whisk smooth
Add the eggs and vanilla and whisk again
Place a cube of butter into each muffin time and then melt the butter in the hot oven, about 2 minutes
When the butter has melted, remove from the oven and pour about 3 tablespoons of the egg mixture into each muffin tin
Bake for 12 minutes, until hugely puffy and slightly browned around the edges
Remove from the oven
The pancakes will fall immediately after removing them from the oven
Use a spoon to scoop out each pancake
Sprinkle with powdered sugar, drizzle with syrup, or top with fruit

ingredients.raw: Optional Add ins: 1 mashed banana, mini chocolate chips

instructions.text: Ingredients
Sift together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt
In a separate bowl, whisk together the sour cream, milk, eggs, and vanilla
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ones, mixing only until combined
Add in optional add ins if using and mix
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet over medium-low heat until it bubbles
Pour ⅓ cup of the pancake batter into the pan
Cook for 2 to 3 minutes, until bubbles appear on top and the underside is nicely browned
Flip the pancakes and then cook for another minute until browned
Repeat with remaining batter
Sift together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt
In a separate bowl, whisk together the ricotta, milk, eggs, and vanilla
Mix in the melted butter, lemon zest and lemon juice
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ones, mixing only until combined
Add in chocolate chips and mix
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet over medium-low heat until it bubbles
Pour ⅓ cup of the pancake batter into the pan
Cook for 2 to 3 minutes, until bubbles appear on top and the underside is nicely browned
Flip pancakes and cook for another minute until browned
Repeat with remaining batter

recipes.title: Peanut Butter Oat Balls
notes: Keep in refrigerator after done making

ingredients.raw: 3/4 cup oat flour (about 1 cup blended oats)
1/2 cup topping of choice (blueberries, chocolate chips, etc.)

instructions.text: Mix all ingredients until well combined except topping of choice
Add and mix in topping of choice
Place in freezer for 30 minutes to set

recipes.title: Pumpkin Bread
notes: Cover and store leftover bread at room temperature for up to 3-4 days or in the refrigerator for up to about 10 days

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar
1 ½ cups (340g) pumpkin puree (canned or fresh)
1/4 cup (50g) packed light or dark brown sugar
Lowering the oven rack prevents the top of your bread from browning too much too soon
There will be a few lumps- do not overmix
The bread is done when a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean with only a few small moist crumbs
This may be before or after 60-65 minutes depending on your oven, so begin checking every 5 minutes at the 55 minute mark or so

instructions.text: Adjust the oven rack to the lower third position and preheat the oven to 350°F
Spray a 9×5 inch loaf pan with non-stick spray, then set aside
In a large bowl, whisk the flour, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, ginger, and salt together until combined
In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs, granulated sugar, and brown sugar together until combined
Whisk in the pumpkin, oil, and orange juice
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and gently mix together using a rubber spatula or a wooden spoon
Gently fold in the chocolate chips
Pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan
To make the crumb topping: Whisk the flour, granulated sugar, brown sugar, and pumpkin pie spice together until combined
Using a fork, lightly mix in the melted butter until crumbs form
Spoon crumbs evenly on top of the batter and gently press them down into the batter so they’re snug
Bake for 60-65 minutes
Allow the bread to cool completely in the pan on a wire rack before removing and slicing

recipes.title: Quiche
notes: Will stay in the refrigerator for up to 4 days if covered

ingredients.raw: 1 prepared unbaked Pie Crust (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
1 cup milk or half and half
2 cups add-ins (spinach, bacon, zucchini, mushrooms, etc)
Easy way to blind bake a crust:
ingredients.note: Place your crust in the pan and flatten its edge Spray the outside of another pie pan (preferably a duplicate of your bottom pan) with non-stick spray, and nestle it into the crust Chill the crust for 30 minutes, to solidify fats and prevent shrinkage Place the pan upside down on a baking sheet, so that the empty pan is on the bottom Bake for 20 minutes in the oven Remove the crust from the oven Use a spatula to carefully turn the pan over, so its crust side is up Prick the bottom of the crust all over with a fork Return the crust to the oven, and bake for an additional 7-10 minutes

ingredients.raw: Most quiche add-ins should be pre-cooked and can still be warm when mixing into the egg filling (pat dry prior to mixing in)
Use a pie crust shield (or tin foil) to prevent pie crust edges from over-browning

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F
Partially blind bake your pie crust
Reduce oven temperature to 350°F
In a large bowl with a handheld mixer, beat the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper together on high speed until completely combined, about 1 minute
Whisk in cheese and add-ins (add up to 2 cups of add-ins including vegetables and meat/seafood)
Bake the quiche until the center is just about set, about 45-55 minutes
Allow to cool for 15 minutes

ingredients.raw: 8 ounces ground breakfast sausage (turkey or pork)
1-1 ½ cups milk or half and half
1/2 teaspoon salt or more to taste
1/4 teaspoon pepper or more to taste

instructions.text: In a small skillet, cook sausage over medium heat until brown
Drain the fat in the skillet
Add butter and heat until melted
Add the flour, salt, pepper, thyme and rosemary, then stir and cook for about 2-3 minutes until blended and slightly browned
Gradually add the milk, stirring constantly
Bring to a boil, cook and stir until thickened, about 2 minutes

ingredients.raw: 2 ¼ teaspoons (7g) instant yeast (1 standard size packet)
1/4 cup (57g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature and cut into 4 pieces
2 ¾ cups (345g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting/ rolling
5 slices bacon cooked until crisp, cut into 1/4-inch pieces
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions.text: Ingredients
To make the dough: Whisk the flour, sugar, salt, and yeast together into the bowl of a stand electric mixer fitted with the dough hook attachment
Combine the milk and butter together in a heatproof bowl
Microwave or use the stove and heat until the butter is melted and the mixture is warm to touch (about 110°F)
Pour into the dry ingredients, add the egg, and mix on low speed
Once it is all added, beat on medium speed until a soft dough forms
Increase speed to medium-high and beat until the dough is soft and supple, about 6 minutes longer
Transfer the dough to a lightly greased bowl
Cover the dough loosely with plastic wrap, a paper towel, or aluminum foil and let sit in a warm place until doubled in size, about 1-2 hours
Turn the dough out of the bowl, and knead briefly to release excess air; reform it into a ball, and return it to the bowl.
Lightly grease a large piece of plastic wrap, and lay it on the dough
Cover the entire bowl tightly with the rest of the plastic, and refrigerate for 4 hours or overnight to let the dough proof
Butter a 9x13-inch baking pan with softened butter and line with parchment paper
Turn the dough onto a floured work surface, and press it flat
Roll it into a 10-by-18-inch rectangle, with a long edge facing you
Spread the softened butter all over the dough leaving a 1-inch border on the side opposite you
In a small bowl, toss the cheese, chives, salt and pepper together and then sprinkle evenly over the dough
Evenly sprinkle the bacon pieces over the dough and lightly press the toppings down onto the dough
Starting from the side facing you, roll the dough up into a tight roll
Lightly brush the 1-inch border with water, and continue rolling to seal the roll
Fold the ends in just a bit, and if any spots look thicker than others, even out the roll with your hands
Cut the roll every 1 ½ inches with a serrated knife, to make 12 buns
Place the buns in the prepared pan, spacing them 1 inch apart
Cover the buns loosely with plastic wrap, and set aside in a warm place to rise until the buns double in size, leaving no gaps in between, 1 ½ hours - 2 hours
For the topping: Stir the butter, chives and Parmesan in a small bowl, then set aside
Position an oven rack in the center of the oven, and preheat to 350°F
Bake the buns until they're golden brown and the tops spring back when pressed lightly, 25-30 minutes
Spread the topping evenly over the buns, and return them to the oven for 5 minutes more
Let them cool in the pan for 10 minutes before serving

recipes.title: Apple Cinnamon Scones
notes: When they're completely cool, wrap in plastic and store at room temperature for up to several days
If not using icing right away, cover and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 days

ingredients.raw: 2 cups (240g) all-purpose flour plus more for hands and work surface

ingredientGroups.name: (I needed to add about ½ cup more flour to the dough)

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (120ml) heavy cream or buttermilk (plus 2 tablespoons for brushing)
1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar
1 heaping cup (125g) peeled and chopped apple (I use Granny Smith)
1/2 cup of cinnamon bits/ chips (optional - store bought or see recipe for homemade)
1 cup confectioners’ sugar, sifted (sift after measuring)
Dough will be sticky, but if it’s too sticky, add a little more flour
If it seems too dry, add 1-2 more Tablespoons heavy cream/ buttermilk
There should be about 1/2" space between them, at their outer edges
When you pull one away from the others, it should look baked all the way through; the edge shouldn't look wet or unbaked
Add another Tablespoon of milk or heavy cream to thin out if necessary
For thicker icing, add a little more confectioners’ sugar

instructions.text: Whisk flour, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt together in a large bowl
Grate the frozen butter using a box grater
Add butter to the flour mixture and combine with a pastry cutter, two forks, or your fingers until the mixture comes together in pea-sized crumbs
Place mixture in the refrigerator or freezer as you mix the wet ingredients together
Whisk 1/2 cup buttermilk or heavy cream, the egg, brown sugar, and vanilla extract together in a small bowl
Drizzle over the flour mixture, add the apples and cinnamon chips, then mix together until everything appears moistened
Line a baking sheet with parchment; if you don't have parchment, just use it without greasing it
Sprinkle a bit of flour atop the parchment or pan
Scrape the dough onto the floured parchment or pan
Gently pat and round dough into an 8-inch circle about 3/4" thick
At this point you can cover and refrigerate overnight or finish
To make the topping, stir together the coarse sugar and cinnamon
Brush circle with milk, and sprinkle with the topping
For best texture and highest rise, place the pan of scones in the freezer for 30 minutes, uncovered
While the scones are chilling, preheat the oven to 425°F
Using a knife or bench knife that you've run under cold water, slice circle into 8 wedges
Carefully pull the wedges away from the center to separate them just a bit
Bake the scones for 18-22 minutes, or until they're golden brown
Remove the scones from the oven, and cool briefly on the pan and serve warm
Whisk the confectioners’ sugar, 2 tablespoons milk or heavy cream, and vanilla extract together
Taste, then add a pinch of salt if desired

recipes.title: Banana Chocolate Chip Scones
notes: When they're completely cool, wrap in plastic and store at room temperature for up to several days

ingredients.raw: 1/3 cup buttermilk or heavy cream, plus more for brushing
2 ¼ ounces semisweet chocolate, chopped (1/2 cup) or mini chocolate chips
Sanding sugar for sprinkling (or turbinado or granulated)
There should be about 1/2 an inch of space between them, at their outer edges
When you pull one away from the others, it should look baked all the way through; the edge shouldn't look wet or unbaked

instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the banana, cream, egg, and vanilla
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, brown sugar, cinnamon, baking powder, and salt
Grate the frozen butter using a box grater, add to the flour mixture and combine with a pastry cutter, two forks, or your fingers until mixture comes together in pea-sized crumbs
Add in the chocolate, drizzle in the banana mixture and stir with a fork until the mixture is crumbly and moistened, but stop before you’ve mixed it into a uniform batter
Line baking sheet with parchment; if you don't have parchment, use it without greasing it
Sprinkle a bit of flour atop the parchment or pan
Scrape the dough onto the floured parchment or pan
Gently pat and round dough into an 8-inch circle about 3/4 inches thick
At this point you can cover and refrigerate overnight or finish
Brush circle with milk, and sprinkle with the coarse sugar
For best texture and highest rise, place pan of scones in freezer for 30 minutes, uncovered
While the scones are chilling, preheat the oven to 425°F
Using a knife or bench knife that you've run under cold water, slice circle into 8 wedges
Carefully pull the wedges away from the center to separate them just a bit
Bake the scones for 18-22 minutes, or until they're golden brown
Remove the scones from the oven, and cool briefly on the pan and serve warm

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (113g) cheddar cheese, very coarsely grated or diced
1/3 cup (about 14g) snipped fresh chives or 1/3 cup (21g) finely diced scallion tops (the green part)
1/2 pound bacon, cooked, cooled, and crumbled (about 1 cup)
3/4 cup + 2 tablespoons (198g) buttermilk or heavy cream plus more if needed
Try squeezing the dough together; if it's crumbly and won't hang together, or if there are crumbs remaining in the bottom of the bowl, add more until the dough comes together
There should be about 1/2 an inch of space between them, at their outer edges
When you pull one away from the others, it should look baked all the way through; the edge shouldn't look wet or unbaked

instructions.text: Ingredients
Lightly grease a baking sheet, or line it with parchment.
Whisk together the flour, salt, baking powder, and sugar
Grate the frozen butter using a box grater, add to the flour mixture and combine with a pastry cutter, two forks, or your fingers until mixture comes together in pea-sized crumbs
Mix in the cheese, chives, and bacon until evenly distributed.
Add 3/4 cup of the buttermilk, stirring to combine
Scrape the dough onto the floured parchment or pan
Gently pat and round dough into an 8-inch circle about 3/4 inches thick
At this point you can cover and refrigerate overnight or finish
Brush circle with some buttermilk
For best texture and highest rise, place pan of scones in freezer for 30 minutes, uncovered
While the scones are chilling, preheat the oven to 425°F with a rack in the middle to upper third
Using a knife or bench knife that you've run under cold water, slice circle into 8 wedges
Carefully pull the wedges away from the center to separate them just a bit
Bake the scones for 20-22 minutes, or until they're golden brown
Remove the scones from the oven, and cool briefly on the pan and serve warm

ingredients.raw: To get eggs to sink into sauce, push a 1/4 measuring cup into the sauce, crack the egg into the cup, and pour out egg into the hole left behind the cup

instructions.text: Heat oven to 375°F
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-low, add onion and bell pepper, and cook gently until very soft, about 20 minutes
Add garlic and cook until tender, about 1-2 minutes
Stir in cumin, paprika and chili powder, and cook for 1 minute
Add tomato paste and stir until softened
Pour in tomatoes and season with salt and pepper; simmer until tomatoes have thickened, about 10 minutes
Gently crack eggs into skillet over tomatoes
Season eggs with salt and pepper
Transfer skillet to oven and bake until eggs are just set, about 8-10 minutes
Sprinkle with feta and serve

ingredients.raw: 4 slices bacon, diced large or 1/4 pound sausage, crumbled
Eggs (1-2 per person - however many you want)

instructions.text: Get roasted potatoes in oven
Cook bacon or sausage according to package instructions, drain and set aside
To saute onions and mushrooms, heat olive oil in a frying pan and add onions, mushrooms, salt and pepper
Saute until soft and until juices evaporate
Using 4 oven safe skillet-type dishes (like a pie plate), divide all ingredients
Top with shredded cheese
Place in oven at 350°F convection to melt cheese
Cook eggs while dishes are in the oven (cook as desired- scrambled, over-easy, etc.)
As eggs finish cooking, remove skillets from oven, top with eggs, and serve

ingredients.raw: 2-3 sliced scallions - white and pale green parts only

instructions.text: Preheat waffle maker and coat with nonstick oil spray
Beat egg whites in a separate bowl until stiff peaks form
Whisk flour, baking powder, baking soda, sugar (if using) salt, and cinnamon together in a large mixing bowl
In a separate bowl, whisk together buttermilk, vegetable oil, egg yolks, and vanilla
Whisk wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, stir in cheese, scallions and chives, then gently fold in egg whites
Pour batter into each waffle mold, then cook according to waffle maker directions
Serve waffles immediately with hot fried chicken, and maple syrup

ingredients.raw: 3-4 Tbsp maple syrup (depending how sweet you like it)
Keep an eye on it as this can happen quick

instructions.text: Prepare a large bowl with an ice bath, and place a smaller bowl on-top of the ice bath, then set aside
In a small pan over medium heat, add butter and melt
Once melted, whisk butter continuously until the butter has browned
Once the butter is brown, immediately transfer to the small bowl over the ice bath
Whisk in maple, cinnamon, salt and vanilla
Whisk butter mixture in the small bowl over the ice bath, constantly, until the butter becomes light in color and has a whipped consistency

ingredients.raw: 2/3 cup plain non-fat Greek yogurt (can also use Vanilla but then don’t need sweetener)
2-4 teaspoons of truvia sugar, depending on desired sweetness
ingredients.note: If using flavored yogurt, you should not have to add sweetener

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup fruit, plus a little more (can be fresh or frozen)
Don’t have to soften it; can eat it frozen if you like

instructions.text: Scoop fruit into a bowl
If you want it softened, heat in the microwave for 30 seconds, then some more if not to desired softness
Take the fruit out of the bowl after microwaving it
Mix together the yogurt and the truvia, if using, in the bowl
Add the fruit back in and mix again

recipes.title: Blooming Onion
notes: Air Fry*

ingredients.raw: Depending on how thick the slices are, turn over the onion, make additional slices down the onion in between each of the original slices to make 16 slices
Longer is better as it gives the onion time to separate into petals on its own
The onion petals will open more fully as it cooks, so spray with more vegetable oil at least twice during the cooking time

instructions.text: Cut off the top ½-inch of the onion, leaving the root end of the onion intact
Use an apple wedge slicer to cut through the onion but DON’T go all the way through
Soak the onion in cold water for 2 hours or more
Take the onion out of the water and shake dry
Gently separate the onion petals that are stuck together
Combine the eggs and milk in a bowl
In a second bowl, combine flour, salt, pepper, cayenne pepper, paprika, and garlic powder
Pre-heat the air fryer to 350ºF and place the onion cut side up into a third empty bowl
Pour the egg mixture all over the onion and let it soak for a minute in the mixture
Carefully remove the onion, tipping it upside down to drain off any excess egg, and transfer it to the empty egg bowl, again cut side up
Sprinkle the flour mixture over the onion, making sure the onion is well coated and all the petals have the seasoned flour mixture on them
Carefully turn the onion over, shake off any excess flour and transfer it to a plate or baking sheet
Spray the onion generously with vegetable oil
Transfer the onion, cut side up to the air fryer basket and air-fry for 25 minutes

recipes.title: Bread Bowl
notes: Cover and store leftover bread bowls at room temperature for a couple days or in the refrigerator for 1 week
You can also freeze the baked bread bowls for up to 3 months, then thaw overnight in the refrigerator and bring to room temperature before serving

ingredients.raw: 6 cups (780g) bread flour, plus more for hands and surface
Egg wash: 1 large egg beaten with 1 tablespoon milk
If you don’t have a stand mixer, use a regular large mixing bowl
The mixture should be frothy and foamy after 5 minutes
The dough should be thick, yet soft and only slightly sticky
It should pull away from the sides of the bowl as it mixes
If it’s too sticky, add more flour, 1 tablespoon at a time, until it pulls away from the sides of the bowl
For this warm environment, I preheat the oven to 200°F then turn the oven off and place the bowl inside the warm-ish oven with the oven door cracked open a bit
The longer you cool, the easier they are to cut open!
Save the center to dunk into soup!

instructions.text: Ingredients
Pour the warm water over yeast in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook attachment
Whisk together, cover the bowl with a towel, and allow to sit for 5 minutes
With the stand mixer running on low speed, add the sugar, salt, olive oil, 4 cups of bread flour, salt and garlic powder
Beat on low speed for 1 minute, then add remaining 2 cups of flour
Beat on low speed for 5-6 minutes
Place into a large greased bowl, turning once to grease the top, cover the bowl with plastic wrap and place it in a warm environment to rise until doubled, about 90 minutes
Once doubled in size, punch down the dough to release any air bubbles
Remove dough from the bowl and turn it out onto a lightly floured surface, then punch down again to release any more air bubbles if needed
Using a sharp knife or dough scraper, cut dough into 6 even pieces and form each into a large ball
Line 2 large baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats and sprinkle with corn meal, then place 3 dough balls onto each
Brush each dough ball with egg wash and, using a sharp knife, score an X into the tops of each
Cover lightly and set aside to rest for 20 minutes as the oven preheats
Preheat oven to 400°F
Bake for 15 minutes, remove from oven, and egg wash again
Put back in oven for 15 minutes or until golden brown
Cool until ready to handle
For serving, cut a large round out of the top of each bread bowl, scoop out the center and fill with soup
Preheat oven to 350°F
Lay slices of a French baguette onto a baking sheet
Brush each slice with some olive oil on both sides
Bake in oven for 5-10 minutes until they are golden brown
Follow directions for one of the following toppings below

ingredients.raw: 28-ounce can Fire Roasted Tomatoes, drained OR 4-5 fresh plum tomatoes
ingredients.note: Can use fresh tomatoes if you have time for bruschetta to sit for at least an hour

ingredients.raw: Red onion (proportion to amount of tomatoes)
If using canned tomatoes, can add in 1 ½ fresh tomatoes

instructions.text: Drain or dice tomatoes and put in bowl
Combine all ingredients, mix and refrigerate until ready to eat
Top each slice of the baguette with bruschetta or serve separately for people to self-serve

ingredients.note: May want to microwave for 5 seconds

instructions.text: Spread goat cheese on each slice of the baguette
Lay a few strawberry slices on each baguette
Drizzle olive oil, balsamic glaze, and season with black pepper and mint
Spread burrata on each slice of the baguette
Lay a small slice of prosciutto each baguette
Drizzle with honey

recipes.title: Beer Cheese
notes: Serve with pretzels (store bought or see recipe for homemade), apples, or bread*
Can cover and store leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 1 week
Heat over stovetop or in the microwave before serving again

ingredients.raw: 2 ½ cups (10 ounces) shredded sharp cheddar cheese
The dip will be thin right off the stove, but will begin to thicken after a few minutes

instructions.text: Shred fresh cheddar cheese, then set aside
Melt the butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat
Add the flour and whisk together until a thick and clumpy paste forms, about 1 minute
In a slow and steady stream, whisk in the milk
After all milk is added, continue whisking until it slightly thickens, about 1 minute
Add in about half the cheese while constantly mixing
Add the beer
Add the remaining cheese and the remaining ingredients and continue to whisk until melted and well combined
Remove the cheese dip from heat and pour into a serving dish
Sprinkle with extra cheese, if desired, and serve warm or at room temperature

ingredients.raw: 1 cup shredded cheddar cheese, plus more to top
8 ounces (1 cup) blue cheese dressing or ranch dressing, plus more to reach desired flavor
1/2 cup Frank’s Red Hot buffalo sauce, plus more to reach desired flavor
Watch carefully so it doesn’t burn!

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F and spray a 9x13-inch baking pan
Bake chicken either in the oven, boil for 20 minutes, or cook in instant pot
Shred chicken into small pieces
Combine cream cheese, cheddar cheese, dressing and hot sauce in a pot and cook until melted/ combined
Add chicken to pot and mix until it is combined with the sauce
Pour dip into baking pan, top with cheddar cheese and bake in oven for 15-20 minutes
Cook for another 2 minutes under the broiler

recipes.title: Cottage Cheese and Onion Chip Dip

ingredients.raw: 2 small yellow onions or 1 large onion grated

instructions.text: Mix all ingredients together and refrigerate overnight so the flavors fuse together

ingredients.raw: At least 1/2 cup shredded Mozzarella Cheese to top
30 Wonton Wrappers or chips of your choice
Make sure not to over-fry the chips, as after you remove them from the pan, the chip will still darken
Rest the wonton chips on a paper towel and season with salt

instructions.text: Mix Crab Meat, Cream Cheese, Japanese Mayo, Sesame oil, Black Pepper, Salt, Soy Sauce, Sugar, and green onions in a bowl, then transfer to a baking pan
Cover with shredded mozzarella cheese and bake at 375°F for 20 minutes
If using wonton wrappers, cut the wonton wrapper diagonally, and heat about 1/2-inch of oil in a pan
Once the oil is hot, add in the wonton wrapper and fry for 30 seconds on each side or until golden brown and crispy

recipes.title: Cranberry and Cream Cheese Dip
notes: Must make night before*

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Combine cranberries and sugar in a baking dish, mixing until sugar is stuck to the cranberries
Bake for 30 minutes, covered
Remove from oven and stir in apricot jam and pecans
Refrigerate overnight
Pour over cream cheese and serve with crackers

recipes.title: Guacamole
notes: If also making pico de gallo, can just take a large spoonful of that and mix in with avocados, then add cumin and more lime juice if needed

instructions.text: Cut avocados in half, remove pits, and scoop the flesh out of their shells into a large bowl
Immediately add the lime juice, onion, cumin, salt, and pepper and mash together
Add the tomatoes, mix well and taste for salt and pepper

ingredients.raw: 1 15-ounce can chickpeas or 1 ½ cups (250 grams) cooked chickpeas
1/4 cup fresh lemon juice (1 large lemon)
A sprinkling of ground paprika to serve on top

instructions.text: Rinse and wash chickpeas
Combine Tahini and lemon juice in food process and process well for about 1 minute
Add garlic, oil, cumin and salt and process for another minute
Add chickpeas in 2 batches, processing well after each batch
Slowly add in water until you reach the consistency you like
Sprinkle with paprika and drizzle with olive oil if desired

ingredients.raw: 1/2 teaspoons cayenne pepper, to your taste

ingredients.note: Can use a whole 16 ounce red bag of frozen roasted corn from TJ’s

ingredients.raw: 1 8-ounce block cream cheese, at room temperature and cut into cubes
2 ½ tablespoons fresh lime juice (about 1 ½ limes)
1 ¼ cup crumbled cotija cheese, divided

instructions.text: In a bowl, combine the chili powder, paprika, cayenne, salt, and pepper
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat
Add the onion to the skillet and cook until soft, about 5 minutes
Add the garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until aromatic
Add the corn and 1 ½ teaspoon of the spice mix, and cook until the corn is softened for about 5 minutes
Reduce the heat to low and mix in the cream cheese until melted and creamy
Stir in the sour cream and cook until warmed throughout
Mix in 1 tablespoon (the remaining) spice mix and stir until combined
In a small bowl, mix together the mayonnaise and lime juice
Pour mayonnaise and lime over the corn mixture in the skillet and mix until combined
Mix in 3/4 cup cotija cheese
Transfer mixture to a serving dish and sprinkle with remaining 1/2 cup cotija cheese

ingredients.raw: 1 large head of cauliflower cut into florets

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 450°F and grease a baking sheet with cooking spray or use airfryer
In large bowl, whisk flour, cornstarch, water, garlic powder, onion powder and paprika in a bowl until batter is smooth
Add cauliflower to batter and toss to coat
Spread cauliflower on the baking sheet in a single layer, and season with salt and pepper
Bake in oven until lightly browned, 20-25 minutes, flip over halfway through to bake evenly
In another small bowl, do one of the following depending on flavor:
c or d. Add all sauce ingredients to a small pot and whisk well while bringing it to a boil
c or d. Reduce to simmer and whisk some more for 2 minutes
c or d. Remove it from the heat and allow it to thicken a little while it cools slightly
Dredge (dip) cauliflower in whichever sauce you made, serve immediately

ingredients.raw: 3/4 pound ground beef (extra lean) or chicken
If they start to stick, gently stir with a wooden spoon to separate

instructions.text: To make the dough: In a food processor bowl, put flour and salt
Process while slowly adding water and eggs until a ball is formed
Wrap in plastic wrap and let rest for 30 minutes or up to overnight
To make the Kreplach: Bring a large pot of water to a boil
Combine the filling ingredients in a bowl and mix well
When ready to make the dumplings roll out the dough to 1/8 inch thickness on a floured surface
Using a 3 inch biscuit cutter, cut the dough into approximately 30 circles
Place a heaping teaspoon (about 3/8 ounces) of filling in the center of each circle
Lightly wet the bottom edges of the dough with a little water and fold dover the top of the dough forming a half circle and pinch the dough closed all around
Carefully drop the kreplach into the boiling water and cook for 20 minutes
Gently remove the cooked kreplach from the water to drain
Serve right away with soup, freeze or saute
To saute: Heat oil over medium flame in 10-inch skillet
Saute boiled kreplach until golden brown on both sides
Serve with sauteed onions or eat plain

recipes.title: Focaccia Bread
notes: Cover leftover focaccia tightly and store at room temperature for 2 days or in the refrigerator for 1 week
To reheat the slices, use the microwave or bake in a 300°F oven for 5 minutes

ingredients.raw: 2 teaspoons instant yeast or 1 packet active dry yeast
1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil for the dough
4 ½ – 5 cups (565g-630g) all-purpose flour or bread flour
1 tablespoon of dried herbs like oregano, italian seasoning, etc and or ¼ cup finely grated parmesan or romano cheese (optional)
5 Tablespoons extra virgin olive oil or more as needed, divided
3-4 tablespoons chopped fresh herbs such as basil, thyme, and rosemary (or 2 Tablespoons dried herbs)
Sprinkle of coarse salt and freshly ground black pepper
Caramelized onions, thin tomato slices, grated parmesan or romano cheese
If the dough is really sticky, slowly add the last of the flour
If the dough is too sticky as you knead, add more flour 1 tablespoon at a time
The dough should still feel a little soft, but shouldn’t stick to your hands too much
Poke it with your finger – if it slowly bounces back, your dough is ready to rise, if not, keep kneading
If you’re in a hurry, you can use your instant pot to proof the dough and make it rise faster - use the yogurt setting and let rise for 30 minutes
The spray keeps the bread from sticking, while the olive oil gives the bottom crust great flavor and a bit of crunch
It’s easier if you oil your hands or wear gloves and spray your hands with olive oil
Be careful not to tear the dough
If it’s shrinking, cover it with a clean towel and let it rest for 5-10 minutes before continuing (this lets the gluten settle and it’s much easier to shape after that)
The longer it rests, the better the flavor but 1 hour is still yummy
Keep it covered, as it may rise a little during this time, but not much
Add a little more olive oil if needed so the dough is completely covered
ingredients.note: This creates the crisp crust!

ingredients.raw: If desired, broil on high for the last minute to really brown the top
You can also freeze the cooled focaccia for up to 3 months, then thaw in the refrigerator or at room temperature

instructions.text: Other toppings ideas:
To prepare the dough: Whisk 1 cup of the water, honey and yeast together in the bowl of your stand mixer fitted with a dough hook or paddle attachment, then cover and allow to rest for 5 minutes
Add the remaining warm water, olive oil, salt, and 1 cup flour
Beat on low speed for 20 seconds, then add 3 ½ cups more flour and beat on low speed for 2 minutes
If using optional herbs or cheese, add it to the dough now as well
Knead the dough for 3-4 minutes on low speed or by hand if too heavy
Lightly grease a large bowl with 1 or 2 teaspoons of oil or some non-stick spray
Place the dough in the bowl, turning it to coat all sides in the oil
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap, or a clean kitchen towel
Allow the dough to rise at room temperature for 2-3 hours or until double in size
To Prepare the pan: Use non-stick vegetable oil spray to lightly grease a 12×17 inch baking pan/ cookie sheet
Drizzle about 2 tablespoons of olive oil atop the spray
When the dough is ready, punch it down to release any air bubbles
Place on the oiled baking pan, then stretch and flatten the dough to fit the pan
Cover the dough tightly and let it rest in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour and up to 24 hours
Remove the dough from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature as you preheat the oven and prepare the toppings
Preheat the oven to 500°F
To prepare the toppings: Whisk the 3 remaining tablespoons of olive oil with the minced garlic and herbs, then set aside
Using your fingers, dimple the dough all over the surface - like you’re playing piano on the dough
Drizzle on the olive oil topping and use your hands or a pastry brush to spread it all over the top
Sprinkle with a little coarse salt and freshly ground black pepper
Place the pan in the oven and immediately lower the temp to 450°F
Bake for 20-23 minutes rotating the pan halfway for even browning or until lightly browned on top
Cut and serve hot or let it come to room temperature before slicing and serving

ingredients.raw: 2 ½ cups very warm water divided
If it is too sticky, add a little more flour
Cover with a lid while you are not kneading
At 40 minutes you will dump the dough out onto a floured surface
Shape the dough by starting at the long rolling and pinching the dough until you have achieved the desired shape
Score the top of the dough with a knife
You may do an egg white wash at this point if desired

instructions.text: Combine 1/2 cup of warm water, the yeast and 1/2 teaspoon sugar
Let the mixture sit for 5-10 minutes until nice and bubbly
Add the bubbly yeast and all other ingredients (2 cups of water, remaining sugar, oil, salt and flour) to the bowl of a stand mixer
Using the dough hook, combine the ingredients
Once dough has formed, turn the mixer's speed up a little and let the mixer knead for 5 minutes
The dough will stick to the sides of the bowl a little but you should be able to roll the dough into a small ball in your hands easily without it sticking
Pour 1 tablespoon of oil into the instant pot and place the dough into the Instant Pot and push the yogurt button, adjust it to the normal setting
Roll the dough in oil so it will not stick, then cover with a regular cover (not the locking lid)
Let the dough rise for 40 minutes and every 10 minutes you will want to come back and knead the dough a few times (so at 10 minutes, 20 minutes and 30 minutes you will knead the dough)
Divide the dough into the amount of loaves you'd like
Use a rolling pin and roll the dough into a large rectangle
Set the oven to 425°F and let the dough rise until double while the oven is preheating
Bake at 425° for 10 minutes
Without taking the bread out, turn the oven down to 375°F and bake for an additional 20 minutes

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ cups dill pickle chips pressed dry with paper towels
Use a rack to make a second layer

instructions.text: Toast the panko crumbs in oven at 350°F convection for about 7 minutes until lightly brown - do not burn
Dry the pickles with paper towels
You need three shallow bowls or deep plates
In the first one combine the flour and seasoning
In the second combine buttermilk and egg, then add 1/4 cup of the flour mixture from the first bowl
In the third combine panko breadcrumbs, remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt and olive oil with a fork until the oil is evenly distributed
Dredge the pickles in the flour mixture, drop them in the buttermilk mixture and then dredge them in the panko mixture until coated evenly
Place pickles in a single layer in the Air Fryer basket
Cook at 400°F convection until golden brown and crispy, about 8-10 minutes, and spray with some cooking spray after about 6 minutes for a more golden finish
Repeat with remaining pickles
Serve with ranch dressing

ingredients.raw: 1 loaf French bread split in half

instructions.text: Heat oven to 350°F
Place bread cut side up on a baking sheet covered with foil
Combine garlic, oil, butter and italian seasoning in a small saucepan over medium low heat for 3 minutes
Brush mixture over bread covering well
Bake for 10 minutes
Top with cheese and bake for another 5-10 minutes until cheese starts to brown
Cut into 2 in wide slices and serve

ingredients.raw: 1 head garlic (about 12 cloves), cloves separated and peeled
1 cup freshly grated Italian Parmesan cheese

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 450°F
Melt the butter in a small saucepan over low heat
Add the garlic, stir to coat with the butter, and cook, covered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the garlic is very tender
Transfer to a small bowl and mash the garlic in the butter with a fork
Stir in the Parmesan, parsley, red pepper flakes, 1 ½ teaspoons kosher salt, and 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
Slice baguette in half lengthwise and place both halves, cut sides up, on a cutting board
Score each half diagonally (don’t cut all the way through) in large serving-size pieces
Spoon all of the garlic mixture generously on the cut sides of the bread
Cut each half of the baguette in half crosswise along a score and place the 4 pieces on a sheet pan lined with parchment paper
Bake for 5-7 minutes, until the topping is bubbly and starting to brown and the bread is crisp

ingredients.raw: 1 can (16.3 ounces) refrigerated big buttermilk biscuits
Mozzarella cheese sticks, cut into 32 cubes

instructions.text: Heat oven to 350ºF
Separate biscuits and cut each into quarters
Place 1 mozzarella cube on the center of each dough piece
Wrap dough around cheese to completely enclose cheese with dough
Mix butter and garlic in large bowl until blended
Add dough pieces and toss to evenly coat dough pieces with butter mixture
Add Parmesan and mix lightly
Place in 9-inch cast-iron skillet
Bake 35 minutes or until golden brown and cool slightly
Serve with marinara sauce or other favorite dipping sauce

ingredients.raw: 4 cups (500g) King Arthur Unbleached Bread Flour

ingredients.note: Use the greater amount in winter or in a dry climate and the lesser amount in summer or a humid climate

ingredients.raw: Plain or herb/spice infused olive oil, to brush on finished pitas
Can also use a cast iron skillet that's flipped upside down or a baking sheet
If you roll the dough about 7" wide, it'll shrink back to about 6"
If they're on a stone, they'll probably puff up like balloons; if they're on a baking sheet, they'll still expand, but not as vigorously

instructions.text: Combine all of the pita ingredients (except the oil for brushing) and mix
Using an electric mixer or by hand, knead to make a soft, smooth dough
Place the dough in a lightly greased bowl or large measuring cup, cover it, and let it rise for 60-90 minutes, until it's just about doubled in size
Towards the end of the rising time, start to preheat your oven to 475°F, and place a pizza stone on the bottom shelf, if you have one, to warm it
Once dough is done rising, gently deflate the dough, and divide it into 8-12 pieces
Shape each piece into a ball, cover them, and let them rest for 10 minutes
Follow the rest of the steps working with one piece of dough at a time
Roll each into a 6" circle
Carefully flop the dough onto the hot pizza stone/ skillet/ ungreased baking sheet
Bake for 4 minutes
Turn the pitas over, and bake for an additional 60 seconds
Remove them from the oven, and brush with your favorite olive oil, plain or flavored
Wrap lightly in a cotton towel, to keep them soft
Repeat with the remaining pieces of dough

ingredients.note: Black pepper Garlic powder Paprika Chili powder

ingredients.raw: This is optional if you're in a hurry, but highly recommended, as it will make the potatoes crispier
Keep an eye on them though- the residual heat can quickly burn them if you aren't careful

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 425°F convection and line baking sheet with parchment paper
Lay slices of potatoes onto baking sheet
Paint olive oil onto each slice of potato
Season with salt and any other desired spices
Bake in the top half of the oven for 15-20 minutes, until golden brown
