Turn OFF the oven and allow to sit in oven while it cools off 15-30 minutes
Set oven to 400°F convection
Scrub potatoes and pierce them 4 times each with a fork to release steam
Rub with olive oil and sprinkle with salt
Bake for about 45 minutes - 1 hour until a fork pierces pretty easily
While baking cook the bacon, either in the oven with the potatoes for about 10 minutes or fry them in a frying pan until crisp
Drain the bacon on paper towels and let cool
Chop or crumble the bacon and set aside
When potatoes are done and have cooled a little, cut them in half and scoop out the insides, leaving a little potato on the skin
Set aside the insides for something else (mashed potatoes or tater tots)
Increase oven temp 425°F
Spray or rub olive oil all over the potato skin (outside and inside) and place on a baking sheet open side down
Cook for 10 minutes and then flip over and cook for another 10 minutes until crispy
Sprinkle the insides of the potatoes with the cheese and bacon and cook for another 2 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly
Serve with sour cream and chives

ingredients.raw: 2 slices of bread of your choice, toasted

instructions.text: Optional: Drain the ricotta cheese by placing ricotta onto 2 layers of paper towel, laying another 2 paper towels over it and pressing down, then repeat one more time
In a small bowl combine ricotta cheese, honey, 1 teaspoon olive oil and lemon juice
On a pan, heat some olive oil and cook garlic for 30 seconds on medium-low
Add tomatoes and cook until they start to soften
Spread ricotta mixture onto toast
Lay tomatoes across toast
Drizzle with balsamic glaze, basil, salt and pepper

ingredients.raw: 2 cups (240g) all-purpose flour, plus extra for dusting work surface
Up to 1/4 cup toasted sesame seed oil
Dipping sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
If dough does not come together and ride around the blade, drizzle in more water a tablespoon at a time until it just comes together

instructions.text: Place flour and salt in the bowl of a food processor and with processor running slowly, drizzle in about 3/4 cup boiling water and process for 15 seconds
Transfer dough to a floured work surface and knead a few times to form a smooth ball
Transfer to a bowl, cover with a damp towel or plastic wrap, and allow to rest for 30 minutes at room temperature, or up to overnight in the fridge
Divide dough into four even pieces and roll each into a smooth ball
Working one ball at a time, roll out into a large thin circle on a lightly oiled surface
Using a pastry brush, paint a very thin layer of sesame oil over the top of the disk
Roll disk up like a tight burrito, then twist roll into a tight spiral, tucking the end underneath
Flatten gently with your hand, then re-roll into an 8-inch disk
Paint with another layer of sesame oil, sprinkle with 1/2 cup scallions, and roll up like a tight burrito again
Twist into a spiral, flatten gently, and re-roll into a 7-inch disk
Repeat steps 6-10 with remaining pancakes
At this point you can wrap each pancake in saran wrap to hold until ready to cook
Heat about 1/2 tablespoon of oil in a small pan over medium-high heat until shimmering and carefully slip pancake into the hot oil
Cook, shaking the pan gently until first side is an even golden brown, about 2 minutes
Carefully flip with a spatula or tongs, and continue to cook, shaking pan gently, until second side is even golden brown, about 2 minutes longer
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain
Cut into 4-6 wedges
Repeat with remaining 3 pancakes
Serve with sauce for dippinng

recipes.title: Stuffed Mushrooms
notes: https://www.foodnetwork.com/recipes/giada-de-laurentiis/stuffed-mushrooms-recipe-1941818

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup pecorino or parmesan cheese (or a combination)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400ºF
Scoop out the stems and some of the insides to create an open cavity in the mushrooms
Using a food processor, or a knife, chop up the insides of the mushrooms/ the stems really small, then add to a mixing bowl
In the bowl with the mushroom stems, add bread crumbs, cheese, minced garlic, salt, and softened cream cheese
In a pan on the stove, add olive oil and cook chopped mushroom stems
Once mushrooms are mostly cooked, add bread crumbs garlic powder, salt and pepper
Stuff the mushrooms, drizzle some olive oil on top and bake in the oven

ingredients.raw: 16 extra-large mushrooms (such as baby bellas), caps and stems separated
1/2 - 3/4 pound sweet Italian sausage, casings removed
1/2 cup minced scallions, white and green parts (3 scallions)
1/2-1/3 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese, plus more to top
May also want to add Italian seasoning, (roasted) garlic powder and more salt and pepper

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 325ºF
Trim/ scoop out the mushroom stems and chop the stems finely, then set aside
Place the mushroom caps in a shallow bowl and toss with 3 tablespoons of the olive oil and the Marsala, then set aside
Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a medium skillet over medium heat
Add the sausage, crumbling it with a wooden spoon and cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring frequently, until it’s completely browned
Add the chopped mushroom stems and cook for 3 more minutes
Stir in the scallions, garlic, salt, and pepper and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes, stirring occasionally
Add the panko crumbs, stirring to combine with the other ingredients
Mix in the mascarpone and continue cooking until the mascarpone has melted and made the mixture creamy
Off the heat, stir in the Parmesan and parsley and season to taste
Fill each mushroom generously with the sausage mixture
Arrange the mushrooms in a baking dish large enough to hold them all in a snug single layer
Drizzle leftover olive oil from when you coated the mushrooms
Sprinkle with a little more parmesan cheese (optional)
Bake for 50 minutes, until the -stuffing is browned and crusty

ingredients.raw: 4 medium-to-large tomatoes (such as cluster, on-the-vine) tomatoes
1/2 cup arborio or another short-grained starchy rice
1/4 cup parmesan cheese, grated, plus more to top
1 tablespoon bread crumbs, plus more to top
You want to have at least 1 ¾ cup’s worth of juice or a mixture of juice and either water or chicken stock if you do not have enough tomato juice

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350ºF
Cut off the tops of the tomatoes and set those aside
Salt the cavities of the tomatoes and turn them upside down on a paper towel to drain
Scoop out the inside of the tomatoes into a bowl
Using an immersion blender or a food processor, blend the insides of the tomatoes until they are smooth and form a puree
Optional: Pour the puree through a mesh strainer to remove the seeds from the mixture
In a medium skillet, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat until shimmering
Add shallot, garlic and red pepper flakes to pan and cook until softened, about 2 minutes
Add rice to pan and toast until it becomes light and translucent, about 3 minutes
Pour tomato puree mixture into pan, raise heat and cook until boiling
Once boiling, reduce heat to low and cover for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until rice is almost cooked through completely
Pour rice mixture into another bowl and mix in basil leaves, 1/4 cup parmesan cheese and 1 tablespoon breadcrumbs
Scoop spoonfuls of mixture into the cavity of each tomato until full
Sprinkle with additional mixture of parmesan cheese and bread crumbs
Drizzle/ spray the top of each tomato with olive oil
Bake in oven for 25-30 minutes until tomato walls are soft

ingredients.raw: 12 tablespoons (1 ½ sticks) cold unsalted butter, 1/2-inch-diced
2 ½ pounds dried beans, for baking the crust (optional)
2 ½ pounds firm medium (2 ½-inch) tomatoes, cored and sliced 1/4 inch thick
1 cup whole fresh parsley leaves, lightly packed
1/2 cup coarsely chopped fresh basil leaves, lightly packed
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
3/4 pound grated Gruyere cheese (1 pound with rind)
Don't worry if it doesn't fit exactly; you want it to cover most of the bottom of the pan but it can be a little rough on the sides
You can also line the pastry with foil and fill it with dried beans
Check the pastry during baking, piercing any spots that bubble up

If there is a little garlic and herb mixture in the bowl, sprinkle it over the tomatoes, but if there is liquid in the bowl, strain it through a very-fine-mesh strainer, discard the liquid, and sprinkle the garlic and herb mixture on the tomatoes

instructions.text: Place the flour and 1 teaspoon of salt in the bowl of a food processor fitted with the steel blade
Add the butter and pulse 12-15 times, until the butter is the size of peas
Add the egg yolks and pulse a few times to combine
With the motor running, add the ice water through the feed tube and pulse until the dough starts to come together
Dump onto a floured board and roll it into a flat disk
Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes
Preheat the oven to 400ºF and line a sheet pan with parchment paper
Place the sliced tomatoes in a large bowl
In a food processor fitted with the steel blade, add the parsley, basil, garlic, thyme, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1 teaspoon pepper and process until finely minced
With the processor running, pour the olive oil down the feed tube and process until combined
Pour the mixture over the tomatoes and toss gently, then set aside
On a well-floured board, roll the dough out to an 11x17-inch rectangle and transfer it to the prepared sheet pan
Place a second sheet pan directly on the pastry and bake for 15 minutes
Remove from oven and remove the top sheet pan (or the beans and foil)
Using a dinner fork, pierce the pastry in many places
Bake for another 8-10 minutes, until lightly browned
Allow the crust to cool for 15 minutes
Lower the oven to 375ºF
Brush the mustard on the crust with a pastry brush
Sprinkle a thick even layer of Gruyere on the pastry, reserving 1/2 cup for the top, and sprinkle with the 1/2 cup of Parmesan
Place overlapping tomatoes in rows on top
Sprinkle the reserved 1/2 cup of Gruyere and the remaining 2 tablespoons of Parmesan on top
Bake for 30 minutes
Cool slightly, cut into squares, and serve warm or at room temperature

ingredients.raw: Trader Joe’s Smoked Ahi Tuna or Sushi Tuna (4 ounce portion), cut into 1/4-inch dice
1/2 cup seedless cucumber, cut into a 1/4 inch dice
2 tablespoons red or vidalia onion finely chopped
1 teaspoon peeled and grated fresh ginger

instructions.text: Crispy Wonton Cups
Preheat oven to 375°F
Whisk the oils together and lightly brush oil on one side of each wonton wrapper with a pastry brush, covering completely, then sprinkle lightly with salt
Place each wonton wrapper, oiled side up, into a mini muffin cup
Gently push the wrapper in to form a cup, the edges will hang over the sides
Bake for 6-8 minutes or until golden brown
Remove from the pan and cool to room temperature
Combine all the Tuna ingredients in a bowl and stir gently to combine
Combine all the Aioli ingredients together and whisk until smooth
Spoon tartare into wonton cups and drizzle with wasabi aioli
In a small food processor, mix ricotta, 1 tablespoon olive oil, and honey
Once blended, transfer to a bowl
Make a well in the center of the bowl and fill with more olive oil
Season with black pepper
Dip bread into ricotta and enjoy

ingredients.raw: 6 strips (uncooked) bacon cut into small pieces
3 Tablespoons butter unsalted or salted will work
2 ½ lbs gold potatoes peeled and diced into pieces no larger than 1” (this was about 6 Large potatoes for me/1.15kg)
¼ - ½ teaspoon ancho chili powder
1 cup mild or sharp cheddar cheese, shredded
Shredded cheddar cheese, chives, and additional sour cream and bacon for topping optional

instructions.text: Ingredients
Place bacon pieces in a large Dutch Oven or soup pot over medium heat and cook until bacon is crisp and browned
Remove bacon pieces and set aside, reserving 1 tablespoon bacon grease in the pot
Add butter and chopped onion and cook over medium heat until onions are tender, about 3-5 minutes
Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds
Sprinkle the flour over the ingredients in the pot and stir until smooth
Add diced potatoes to the pot along with chicken broth, milk, heavy cream, salt, pepper, and ancho chili powder, and stir well
Bring to a boil and cook until potatoes are tender when pierced with a fork, about 10 minutes
Reduce heat to simmer and remove approximately half of the soup to a blender (be careful, it will be hot!) and puree or use an immersion blender until smooth
Return the pureed soup to the pot and add sour cream, shredded cheese and reserved bacon pieces, stir well
Allow soup to simmer for 15 minutes before serving
Top with additional sour cream, bacon, cheddar cheese, or chives
Cook rice according to package directions
In a large pot/ dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium-high heat
Add ground beef, breaking it up and cooking until browned
While ground beef is cooking, season with 1/2 teaspoon salt and some black pepper
Once browned, add garlic and mix for about 30 seconds
Add diced onion and bell peppers, cooking for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are softened and onions are translucent
Season with chili powder and paprika and mix to combine
Add chicken broth and both cans of tomatoes and mix to combine
Bring to a boil then reduce to a simmer
Add parsley, chives, and salt, and mix to combine
Add cooked rice to pot and mix to combine
Reduce heat to low and stir occasionally until ready to serve

recipes.title: Beef Stew
notes: Can serve immediately, or let cool overnight or for up to 5 days and reheat to serve

ingredients.raw: 2-2 ½ pounds good quality chuck beef, cut into 1 ½-inch cubes
4 medium carrots cut into bite-sized pieces
1/2 pound white mushrooms, stems discarded and cut in quarters
1 pound small potatoes, halved or quartered
2 cups or 1 (14 ½-ounce can) chicken stock or broth
2 teaspoons reduced sodium Beef Better than Bouillon

instructions.text: Place the beef in a bowl with red wine, garlic, and bay leaves, then place in the refrigerator and marinate at least 4 hours to overnight
Once done marinating, preheat the oven to 300ºF
Lift the beef out of the marinade with a slotted spoon and discard the garlic, saving the marinade and bay leaves
Combine chicken stock, tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce and better than bouillon in a measuring cup and set aside
Season the cubes of beef with salt and pepper
In a large Dutch oven, heat oil over medium-high heat until shimmering
Brown half the beef over medium heat for 5-7 minutes, turning to brown evenly, then transfer beef to a large bowl and set aside
Continue to brown the remaining beef, adding oil as necessary and set aside
Heat another 2 tablespoons of oil and add the carrots, mushrooms, and potatoes, then cook for 5-6 minutes over medium heat, stirring occasionally
Season with salt and pepper then transfer to a bowl and set aside
Add onions and garlic and cook, stirring with a wooden spoon and scraping the brown bits from bottom of the pan, for about 5 minutes
Add the reserved wine marinade and scrape up any browned bits, and cook until reduced, about 2-3 minutes
Add the chicken stock mixture and and bring to a simmer
Drain the juices from the beef bowl into the Dutch oven and stir to combine
Add flour to the beef and toss to combine
Add beef to the Dutch oven along with bay leaves and rosemary sprigs, then stir to combine and return to a simmer over medium heat
Cover the pot and place it in the oven to bake it for about 2 hours, until the meat becomes tender
Remove stew from oven, and using tongs, fish out and discard the rosemary and bay leaves
Add potatoes, mushroom, and carrots to stew, return to oven, and continue to cook, partially covered, until beef, potatoes, and carrots are tender and broth has thickened, about another hour
Remove stew from oven
If necessary, place over a burner and simmer for up to 15 minutes to reduce to desired consistency
Stir in peas and season to taste with salt and pepper if necessary

ingredients.raw: 1 ¼ pounds ground turkey breast or lean ground beef
1 serrano chile pepper seeded and diced (optional adds heat)
ingredients.note: be careful not to touch your eyes after touching the seeds!

ingredients.raw: 1 habanero pepper seeded and diced (optional adds heat)
ingredients.note: be careful not to touch your eyes after touching the seeds!

ingredients.raw: 2 teaspoons smoked paprika (regular is okay if you don’t have smoked)
1 teaspoon chili powder - more to taste if wanted
1 14.5-ounce can Rotel diced tomatoes and green chilies, mild, undrained
32-ounce canned low sodium red kidney beans (2 cans), drained and rinsed

instructions.text: Over medium-high heat, heat oil in large pot
Add the meat and brown it, draining any excess fat
Reduce heat to medium, and add garlic, onion, carrots, peppers, stirring for about 5 min
Add the spices and mix well
Add the tomatoes and bring to a boil
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes stirring occasionally
Add beans and corn and continue to simmer for another 15 minutes
Serve with shredded cheese and sour cream

ingredients.note: If using fresh clam juice, reduce bottled juice according to how much fresh clam juice you have If using juice from fresh clams, strain sand from juice using cheesecloth

ingredients.raw: 4 cups peeled, diced russet or yukon gold potatoes (about 4 potatoes)
1 pound fresh (or defrosted from frozen) chopped clams (from seafood dept.)

instructions.text: Add chopped bacon to a large pot and cook until crisp, about 10-12 minutes
Remove bacon from pot and drain grease
Melt butter over medium heat and saute onions until soft and translucent, about 10 minutes
Add potatoes, celery, garlic, thyme, bay leaf, salt, and pepper, sauteing for about 5 minutes
Add clam juice to pot and bring to a boil
Once boiling, lower heat to low and simmer for about 15 minutes until potatoes are soft
When the potatoes are done, remove the thyme and bay leaf from the pot
Pour 2 cups of milk into the pot and bring back to a simmer
While simmering, make roux by melting butter in a smaller pot and whisking in flour
Cook roux over very low heat for 3 minutes, whisking constantly
One large pot is simmering, scoop 1 ½ cups of broth from large pot (it’s okay if you get potatoes) and slowly pour into smaller pot with roux, whisking constantly until blended
Add roux to large pot, continuing to whisk
Pour another 1/2 cup or more milk to large pot, depending on desired consistency, continuing to whisk until blended and smooth
Add bacon and clams, continuing to simmer for at least 5 minutes before serving

ingredients.raw: 4 cups chicken stock (about 1 liter, organic or homemade preferred)
1 teaspoon ground ginger or 1 tablespoon fresh
1/2 teaspoon turmeric (Optional, but gives soup that yellow color)

instructions.text: Whisk together stock, soy sauce, salt, pepper, sugar, ginger, garlic powder, and turmeric in a medium saucepan until smooth
Heat over high heat until the stock comes to a simmer, stirring occasionally
Once soup comes to a simmer, mix in the bouillon and stir until dissolved
Next add the cornstarch and water mixture by stirring the soup continuously as you drizzle in the slurry, or you'll get clumps of cooked starch in your soup
Whisk into broth and bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low
Just before serving, add sesame oil and shoaxing wine
Whisk together eggs until homogeneous
Swirl the soup once with a large spoon, then slowly drizzle egg mixture into soup
Allow soup to sit for 15 seconds, the stir gently to break up the egg to desired size
Sprinkle with scallion greens and serve

ingredients.raw: 3 pounds onions (2 pounds yellow and 1 pound sweet (Vidalia)
ingredients.note: okay to go a little over - doesn’t have to be exact

ingredients.raw: 1 quart of low-sodium beef stock (or 1 quart water/ chicken stock mixed with 4 teaspoons of “Better Than Bouillon” beef base)
1 tablespoon of “Better Than Bouillon” beef base (or 3 cubes of bouillon beef cubes)
2-3 sprigs or sticks of thyme or 1/2 teaspoon dry thyme
Loaf of French bread from bakery section (can ask to be sliced at bakery)
2 teaspoons of salt (if not using low-sodium broth, don’t use salt)
If onions start to brown, lower heat to medium
If onions start to stick to pot, add 1 tablespoon of water and scrape the bottom of the pan

instructions.text: Peel outer layer and cut onions in half from top to bottom (pole to pole)
Then cut slices top to bottom (pole to pole) about 1/8 - 1/4 inch thick
Spray the bottom of large pot and melt butter over medium-high heat until bubbling
Add onions and cook for about 2 minutes without stirring to left onions soften a little
Cook for about another 6 minutes or until softened, stirring constantly with wooden flat spoon
Add garlic after cooking onions for about 4 min/ once they’ve cooked down a little
Once onions are softened, lower heat to medium-low for 1-2 hours, stirring occasionally
While waiting for onions to cook, set oven to 400°F
Place pieces of baguette in a single layer on a baking sheet and bake for about 10 minutes until dry and golden, then set aside
Grate the cheese and set aside
Once onions are browned, add salt and pepper and stir
Add sherry wine and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from bottom of pot
Cook for about 3 min, until alcohol smell is mostly gone
Add both stocks, except for about 1 cup of it
Mix the 1 cup of stock with the “better than bouillon” and heat in microwave for 1 minute
Using the tablespoon that you used to measure out the “better than bouillon”, mix it and the stock to dissolve “better than bouillon” and get any remaining off the tablespoon
Add the 1 cup of stock and “better than bouillon” to the pot
Add thyme and bay leaf
Bring heat to medium-high until it reaches a slight boil
Lower it to medium-low and let simmer for 20 minutes
Add cider vinegar and discard thyme sprigs and bay leaf
Let simmer for at least 5 min on low, then can either serve or refrigerate
To serve: put toasted bread in oven safe bowl, fill with soup, top with cheese, and put in oven on broil setting and watch for cheese to melt, get brown and bubbly, about 7-10 minutes

ingredients.raw: 3/4 pound boneless skinless chicken breasts or thighs
2 tablespoons fresh thyme leaves (or 2 teaspoons dried)
1 pinch each kosher salt and black pepper
1 ½ cups canned coconut milk, heavy cream, or whole milk
1 pound fresh or frozen potato gnocchi
If the soup is too thick, add additional broth

instructions.text: In a large pot/ dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium heat
Add onion and cook for 5 minutes, until fragrant
Add the carrots and celery, and cook for another 3 minutes
Stir in the butter and flour, and cook for 1 minute
Add the chicken, garlic, Italian seasoning, paprika, thyme, and a pinch each of chili flakes, salt, and pepper, then toss to coat in spices
Add 4 cups of chicken broth and simmer over medium heat for 20 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through
Pull the chicken out and shred using an electric mixer or 2 forks
Add shredded chicken back to the soup
Stir in the cream/ milk and gnocchi, allowing the gnocchi to cook for 5 minutes if frozen
Stir in the spinach, sun-dried tomatoes, and parmesan, and cook until warmed throughout, for about 5 minutes
Serve the soup topped with parmesan and enjoy warm with a crusty piece of bread

recipes.title: Green Garlic and Ginger Soup (“Swamp Soup”)

ingredients.raw: 1/3 cup dry rice or 1 cup cooked rice
1 pound chicken or 1 ½ cups cooked/ Rotisserie chicken
1-1 ½ ounces (about 2 inches) fresh ginger, peeled
2 stalks of green onions, white and green parts
2 jalapeños, stems and seeds removed, plus more for garnish if desired
ingredients.note: Leave in the seeds from half a jalapeño for more spice

ingredients.raw: 1 cup parsley, packed plus more for garnish
Can also use a slotted spoon to remove the spinach, but this is much easier
If the blender gets stuck, add more water
If your blender is too small, you can do this in batches or use a food processor
DO NOT PUT HOT LIQUID IN YOUR BLENDER, IT WILL EXPLODE
The pungency of raw garlic can vary, and if you’re sensitive to garlic or are prone to acid reflux you might want to hold back a bit

instructions.text: Season chicken with Kosher salt and pepper and cook in oven or Instant Pot, then shred
Bring broth to a boil, add spinach and cook until spinach has wilted and shrunken in size
Place a strainer over a large bowl or measuring cup and pour the broth and spinach through the strainer to separate the spinach
Return the broth to the original pot and add the spinach to a blender (or large measuring glass if using an immersion blender) and let it cool
Add the rice into the broth, bring back to a boil and reduce to a simmer
Let the rice simmer in the broth until liquid has thickened and rice begins to break apart, about 15 minutes
Once spinach has cooled, add garlic, ginger, green onions, jalapeños and parsley into the blender or measuring glass with the spinach
Add 1/2 cup of water and 1 teaspoon Kosher Salt and blend until smooth, about 1 minute
When broth is thickened, fold in the chicken
Start by mixing in about half of the herb puree and tasting it before dumping it all in
Mix to combine, and add up to another teaspoon of Kosher Salt if desired
Finish with a drizzle of soy sauce and mix throughout the soup
Serve and top with fresh parsley, thinly sliced jalapeño, and a wedge of lemon or lime

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ pounds raw, shell on shrimp
1 ½ quart water or low sodium chicken stock
1/2 pound andouille sausage, cut into 1/4-inch pieces and browned
Mixture of 1 ½ tablespoons each of cold water and cornstarch if necessary

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F
Make the roux by placing the vegetable oil and flour into a 5 to 6-quart cast iron Dutch oven and whisk together to combine
Place roux on the middle shelf of the oven, uncovered, and bake for 1 ½ hours, whisking 2 to 3 times until it turns a deep brownish red
While the roux is baking, peel and devein the shrimp, then place in bowl in refrigerator
Place shells in a 4-quart saucepan with water/stock, set over high heat and bring to a boil
Decrease the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour or until the liquid has reduced to 1-quart
Remove from the heat and strain the liquid into a measuring cup discarding the solids
Once the roux is done, carefully remove it from the oven and set over medium-high heat
Gently add the onions, celery, green peppers and garlic and cook, moving constantly for 7 to 8 minutes or until the onions begin to turn translucent
Stir in the sausage, then season with Cajun seasoning, salt, and pepper
Stir in bay leaf and diced tomatoes
Gradually add the shrimp broth while whisking continually and bring to a boil
Reduce heat to low and simmer until thickened, stirring occasionally, about 1 hour
If necessary add some cornstarch/water mixture to thicken
In the last 10 minutes of cooking, add shrimp and cook, then taste and adjust seasonings
Stir in green onions, reserving some for garnish
Serve spooned on top of white rice

recipes.title: Mataconis Gumbo
notes: Can easily half the recipe but lots of onions are still key for flavor*
For best results, cook the day before and reheat day of - it gets better as it sits **

ingredients.raw: 10 oz roux (can find on Amazon or make homemade by mixing oil and flour and cooking until the color of caramel)

ingredients.note: 2 teaspoons Kosher Salt 2 teaspoons Black pepper 1-2 teaspoons Cayenne pepper for the roux (depending on desired spice)

ingredients.raw: 3-4 pounds chicken breasts (about 6 large breasts), cut into cubes

ingredients.note: 2 teaspoons salt 2 teaspoons black pepper 2 teaspoons onion powder 2 teaspoons garlic powder 1 teaspoon Paprika

ingredients.raw: Optional: 1/2 cup parsley (I usually leave out the parsley)
6 cups of white rice, cooked (I usually cook it in 2 batches)
Do not cover after water boils
The longer it’s cooking, the better the taste
Keep tasting to make sure you like the flavor and add more seasoning if needed

instructions.text: Fill a large pot with 32 cups water, cover, and boil (For T&D: 16 cups water)
Once boiling add 10 oz of roux and cook on medium-low for 30 minutes until roux is dissolved stirring continuously (For T&D: 5 oz roux)
Add chopped onions, celery, and 2 teaspoons of each: salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper
Let cook for 2 hours on medium-low, uncovered

While gumbo is cooking cut up 6 large chicken breasts into a little larger than bite size pieces & season to taste with 1-2 teaspoons salt, 2 teaspoons fresh ground black pepper and 1 tablespoon onion powder
Brown both sides of sliced kielbasa sausage and discard grease
Put both chicken and kielbasa sausage in gumbo & cook for at least another 1-2 hours
Cook a batch of white rice while gumbo is cooking and add 1/2 teaspoon salt to rice
Add chopped green onions and parsley, if desired

ingredients.raw: 1 sprig of fresh tarragon or 1 teaspoon dried
2-3 sprigs of thyme or 1 teaspoon dried
1/2 cup heavy cream (or fat free half and half)
Steamed lobsters will be bright red when done

instructions.text: To steam lobsters, bring water and 2 teaspoons salt to a boil over high heat in an 8 to 10 quart stockpot
Using metal tongs or gloves, place the lobsters head first into the pot and cover tightly
When water returns to a heavy boil, turn the heat down to a rolling boil and steam lobsters for 9 minutes for the first pound, and add 4 minutes for each additional pound
Remove with metal tongs or gloves and place in cool water or let cool for 3-5 minutes before removing meat from shell
Reserve the steaming liquid
Twist lobster claws and tails off
Using the side of a kitchen knife or nutcracker, crack the knuckles and claws and take meat out, reserving any liquid that comes out of the shells and on the cutting board
Set the tail on a hard surface and use your hand to press down and crack the shell
Remove the tail meat and slice in half lengthwise, removing the black intestinal vein
Coarsely chop the meat and refrigerate
Break up the shells along with all the lobster remains into smaller pieces put back into the steaming liquid
Bring lobster shells and water back to a boil, reduce heat to medium low and let simmer for 30-45 minutes
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat
Add onion, carrots and celery, tarragon and thyme and cook, stirring occasionally, until very soft and translucent, about 15 minutes
Stir in tomato paste, and cook, stirring often, about 2 minutes
Add lobster shells from stock pot and reserved liquid from when you shelled the lobster and stir to combine
Add sherry, increase heat to high, and bring to a boil to cook off alcohol
Stir in rice, bay leaf, black pepper, and 2 teaspoons salt
Add 4 cups of the lobster stock, and bring to a simmer
Reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer until rice is very soft, about 25 minutes
Remove and discard bay leaves, then remove from heat, and let stand for 15 minutes
Place one-third of the mixture in a blender (yes, including the shells!)
Remove center piece of blender lid (to allow steam to escape), then secure lid on blender, and place a clean towel over opening in lid
Process until very smooth, about 1 minute
Strain the bisque through a fine-mesh sieve pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid out as possible and discard solids
Repeat procedure 2 times with remaining bisque
Strain again through a fine- mesh sieve lined with a cheesecloth and transfer to a large saucepan
Add the cream ad bring to a low simmer
Add the chopped lobster and let it heat through
Season with salt and pepper and serve

ingredients.raw: Freshly ground black pepper, to taste
1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes, omit if sensitive to spice
2 (15-ounce) cans cannellini beans or Great Northern beans rinsed and drained (or 3 cups cooked beans)
6 ounces ditalini, elbow or small shell pasta of choice
Optional garnishes: Additional chopped parsley, black pepper, grated Parmesan cheese or light drizzle of olive oil
Can also use an immersion blender in a heat safe bowl/ measuring cup

instructions.text: Ingredients
In a large Dutch oven or soup pot over medium heat, warm 3 tablespoons of olive oil until shimmering
Add the chopped onion, carrot, 1/2 teaspoon of salt, and about 10 twists of black pepper
Cook, stirring often, until the vegetables have softened and the onions are turning translucent, about 6-10 minutes
Add the garlic and cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant, about 30 seconds
Add the tomatoes, stir, and cook until the tomatoes are bubbling all over
Add the broth, water, bay leaves, thyme, oregano, and red pepper flakes
Raise the heat to medium-high and bring the mixture to a simmer
Continuing at a simmer, cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally
Use a heat-safe measuring cup to transfer about 1 ½ cups of the soup (avoiding the bay leaves and thyme sprigs) to a blender
Add about 3/4 cup of the drained beans to the blender
Securely fasten the lid and blend until completely smooth, being careful to avoid hot steam escaping from the lid
Pour the blended mixture back into the soup
Add the remaining beans, pasta and parsley to the simmering soup
Continue cooking, stirring often to prevent the pasta from sticking to the bottom of the pot, for about 20 minutes
Remove the bay leaves and the tyme spring and add in the spinach
Let simmer for another 5 minutes until the spinach is tender and wilted
Remove the pot from the heat and stir in the lemon juice, the remaining tablespoon of olive oil, and remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt
Taste and season with more salt and pepper as needed

ingredients.raw: 4 pieces of boneless chicken breast or thigh - about 1 pound
1 tablespoon dark soy sauce or double the amount of light soy sauce (below) if you don’t have dark soy sauce
2 tablespoons dark soy sauce or 1 tablespoon light soy sauce + 1 tablespoon water
4-5 cups of good quality chicken stock preferably low sodium
1 ounce dried or 5 fresh shiitake mushrooms trimmed and sliced, or cut in half
12-13 ounces dried ramen noodles or four portions (or four portions of fresh ramen noodles)
2 bunches of spinach or 4 shanghai bok choy or any Asian greens.

instructions.text: Bring a pot of water to a boil
Lower 4 large eggs carefully into the water and cook the eggs for 6-6 ½ minutes
Immediately place the eggs in an ice bath for a few minutes
Gently peel and set aside until ready to serve
Place all the ingredients for the chicken marinade in a ziplock bag
Mix well to coat the chicken
If time permits, allow the chicken to marinate for at least 1 hour
Preheat the oven to 425°F convection and place an oven rack in the top third of your oven
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper
Place the chicken pieces on the baking sheet, smooth side down evenly spaced apart
Place the baking sheet in the preheated oven (upper third)
Let the chicken roast for 10 minutes
While the chicken is cooking, mix the brown sugar and dark soy sauce for the glaze, and start making the ramen
After 10 minutes, flip the chicken pieces over and spoon the glaze over them
Let the chicken cook for another 10-15 minutes
Remove from the oven and let them cool down
Slice the chicken and set aside
Place two saucepans on the stove
In one saucepan (Pot 1), heat the oil over medium heat, until shimmering
Add the garlic and ginger, and cook for a few minutes until softened
Add the soy sauce and mirin, and stir to combine, then cook for another minute
Add the stock, cover, and bring to boil
Lower the heat to medium and let it simmer for 5 minutes
Add the mushrooms
Simmer gently for another 10 minutes, and season to taste
While Pot 1 is simmering, add water to the second pot (Pot 2), and bring it to a boil
Add dried noodles to boiling water in pot 2, and cook 1 minute less than package directions
Drain the noodles and divide into 4 bowls
Divide spinach and scallions into the bowls
Divide the sliced chicken and place into bowls
Evenly divide the hot stock from pot 1 between the four bowls with ramen noodles
Slice eggs in half and place in bowls and add any other toppings you like

recipes.title: Roasted Butternut Squash and Apple Bisque

ingredients.raw: 3 pounds butternut squash, peeled and cubed
1 large carrot, peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F
Mix together the squash, carrot and apple and spread across two baking pans
Roast in oven until well browned, about 40 minutes, then set aside
In a pot, heat oil and add onion and garlic
Lower heat to medium and cook until lightly browned and translucent, about 10 minutes
Add roasted squash, carrot and apple to the saucepan
Pour in cider, vegetable stock, thyme and bay leaf and return to medium-high heat
Simmer until all vegetables are soft
Discard thyme and bay leaves
Puree vegetables (using a blender or an immersion blender), adding ginger, brown sugar, nutmeg and cinnamon
Serve with a sprinkle of glazed pecans and brie if desired

recipes.title: Tomato Soup
notes: In a sealed nonreactive container, tomato soup will keep up to 1 week in the refrigerator or 6 months in the freezer

ingredients.raw: 3 large (28-ounce) cans whole tomatoes, crushed roughly by hand
4 cups homemade or store-bought low-sodium chicken stock
1/4 teaspoon baking soda, plus more as needed
Up to 1 ¼ teaspoons kosher salt
1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, or more to taste
1/8 teaspoon ground cayenne, or more to taste
1/2 ounce fresh basil leaves (1 loosely packed cup) or 1 ½ teaspoon dried, cut into chiffonade
Adjust heat as needed to maintain a gentle simmer

instructions.text: In a 6-quart stainless steel pot, melt butter over medium heat until foaming, then add carrots and onions
Cook, stirring occasionally, until butter starts to brown on the bottom, about 15 minutes
Add tomatoes (including the juice), followed by chicken stock and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until quite thick, about 1 ½ hours
Taste broth: if its flavor is too sharp or acidic, add 1/4 teaspoon baking soda and stir well (it will foam at first), then taste and repeat if needed
Purée soup with an immersion blender until smooth, or cool until no longer steaming before puréeing in a countertop blender
Stir in cream, then season with salt, pepper, and cayenne to taste
Rewarm to serve
Stir in basil just before serving

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon chopped fresh cilantro (1 teaspoon dried)

instructions.text: Place all ingredients in crock pot
Cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4-5 hours
Before serving remove chicken breasts from pot and chop them
Return chicken to pot, serve w/ fresh cilantro, shredded cheese, avocado and tortilla chips

ingredients.raw: 2 large carrots peeled and chopped (about 8 ounces)
1 stalk celery diced (optional - I didn’t use it)
1/4 teaspoon black pepper or to taste
1/2 cup grated Parmigiano Reggiano plus more for serving
2 cups chopped kale stems removed, finely chopped (or baby spinach)
If broth is too thick, you can add more broth or puree some of the sauteed vegetables with the beans

instructions.text: In a blender or measuring cup, puree one can of beans with the liquid, then set aside
Drain and rinse the other 2 cans of beans, discarding the liquid, then set aside
In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat
If using, add the sausage and cook for 1-2 minutes breaking it up into ground pieces
Add the pancetta, and continue to sauté for 4-5 minutes, until both are browned
Add the onions, carrots, celery, and garlic, and cook over medium-low for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender
Add tomato paste and saute until softened and mixed in
Add in the white wine and saute until most of the liquid has evaporated, about 5 minutes
Add the beans, bean puree, chicken broth, bay leaf, rosemary, thyme, oregano, salt and pepper and bring to a boil
Lower the heat and simmer, partially covered, for about 30 minutes, until the beans are tender, stirring occasionally, scraping the bottom of the pot
Discard the bay leaf, rosemary and thyme sprigs, add in the Parmigiano Reggiano and spinach or kale and let simmer for a few minutes to allow the spinach or kale to wilt

ingredients.raw: 1 lb chicken breast (or shredded rotisserie chicken)
ingredients.note: 1 teaspoon cumin 3/4 teaspoon oregano 3/4 teaspoon ancho chili powder 1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika 1/2 teaspoon Kosher Salt

ingredients.raw: 2 jalapeños, diced and seeded except for the seeds from about half a jalapeno

instructions.text: Season chicken with spices listed under chicken in ingredients
Cook chicken in instapot for 10 minutes, with a 7 minute release or in the oven at 400°F for 20-25 minutes, then shred chicken
Dice onion and jalapeno, keeping the seeds from about 1/2 of one jalapeno for some spice
Heat 1 tablespoon avocado oil in a large pot on medium high heat
Add onion and jalapeno to the pan and sauté for 2-3 min
Next add in the beans, corn, green chilis, seasonings, and chicken stock and mix well then bring to a boil
Cover with a lid and bring the temperature down to low
Simmer for about 15 minutes to let the flavors come together
In a small dish mix together 1 tablespoon cornstarch and 1 tablespoon cold water and mix until smooth
During the last 5 minutes of simmering slowly add in the water/ cornstarch mixture stirring constantly, then bring to a boil and keep stirring to slightly thicken the soup
Cut the cream cheese into cubes and add to the soup
Whisk until smooth and well incorporated
Add in the shredded cooked chicken and the juice of 1/2 lime, then give it one more stir
Serve with your toppings of choice
Season chicken with spices listed under chicken in ingredients
Place all ingredients in crock pot, except for avocado oil, lime, and cornstarch
Cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4-5 hours
Before serving remove chicken breasts from pot and shred them
Return chicken to pot, squeeze juice of half a lime over the chili and mix
Serve with your toppings of choice

ingredients.raw: 2 large garlic cloves smashed but whole

instructions.text: Bring all soup ingredients to a boil
Simmer on low for 10 minutes, then remove ginger and garlic cloves
Add in wontons, shrimp and mushrooms and let simmer for 5-10 minutes
Add scallions and bok choy just before serving

ingredients.raw: 1 large lemon, juiced (1 lemon yields ~3 Tbsp or 45 ml)
Pinch of salt and black pepper

instructions.text: Whisk together all ingredients for the dressing and drizzle over salad

ingredients.raw: 1/4 jalapeno pepper, seeded and rough chopped
1/2 medium ripe avocado peeled and pitted

ingredients.note: 1/2 teaspoon salt 3/4 teaspoon ground cumin 1/2 teaspoon oregano 1/2 teaspoon sugar 1/8 teaspoon dark chili powder 1/8 teaspoon black pepper

instructions.text: Add onion, jalapeno pepper, garlic cloves, lime juice and cilantro to blender (don’t blend)
Let it sit for 5 minutes (unblended), add buttermilk, then blend, and add rest of ingredients

recipes.title: Balsamic Honey Dressing
notes: Grandma’s recipe*

instructions.text: In a small jar with a lid, combine all of the ingredients and shake well until combined

ingredients.raw: 1/2 teaspoon freshly crushed black pepper finely ground
1/4 cup balsamic vinegar (or use flavored vinegar)
Continue whisking/ blending until the dressing is fully emulsified
If you have an immersion blender or other small blender of some sort, that is the best way to make sure it’s completely emulsified

instructions.text: Ingredients
In a small mixing bowl, whisk together the honey, balsamic, mustard, salt, pepper and garlic
Slowly add the oil while whisking/ blending thoroughly to combine
Store in a jar with a lid and refrigerate
Shake well before serving

recipes.title: Black Bean, Corn and Avocado Salad

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ cups fresh, canned, or frozen (and thawed) corn kernels
1 can black beans, drained and rinsed
1 small jalapeño pepper, seeded and chopped (optional)
Taste and add more salt/pepper/cayenne as needed

instructions.text: Combine the minced garlic and lime juice while you prepare everything else
Add everything to a large bowl and stir
Cover and let sit in the refrigerator for 1 hour before serving for best flavor
In a large bowl whisk together all ingredients except for the crumbled blue cheese
Fold in the blue cheese gently until combined, but don’t over mix
Refrigerate until ready to serve
Combine the lemon juice, mustard, shallot, garlic and olive oil in a small bowl or measuring cup and whisk to combine
Season with salt and pepper to taste
Pour over sprouts and toss, topping with cheese and pine nuts

ingredients.raw: 3 heirloom or large slicing tomatoes, sliced thin

instructions.text: Line a plate or large baking sheet with paper towel
Slice tomato and lay on paper towels
Season with salt and set aside for at least 10 minutes until paper towels absorb excess moisture on tomatoes
Prepare dressing by combining olive oil, balsamic vinegar, and honey in a small measuring glass
When tomatoes are ready, lay on plates or a platter to serve
Season with a little more salt, pepper, and basil
Dry the burrata with a paper towel and use your hands to break apart the burrata and lay across the tomato slices
If using, sprinkle mozzarella pearls across the tomato slices
Just before drizzling, whisk the dressing well to combine, then immediately pour on tomatoes
If desired, drizzle some balsamic glaze around the tomatoes
Serve and enjoy!

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup of tomatoes (or more if desired)
1/2 slice of red onion, diced large
Dressing of choice, such as pesto or balsamic (see recipe for homemade pesto)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F convection and line a small pan with non-stick aluminum foil
Lay the tomatoes, onions, and garlic on the foil
Drizzle tomatoes with olive oil so they are well coated and season with salt and pepper
Cook in the oven for 15 minutes until the tomatoes are soft
Add arugula to a bowl
Once tomatoes and onions slightly cool, add them to salad bowl along with their juices
Add ball of burrata and drizzle with desired dressing and balsamic glaze

ingredients.note: 1 lime, juiced (about 2 tablespoons) 1/2 tablespoon brown mustard 1/2 tablespoon cumin 1/2 tablespoon honey 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/4 cup avocado oil

instructions.text: Combine thinly sliced red cabbage, red onion, apple, and minced cilantro in a bowl
In a measuring glass, whisk all vinaigrette ingredients except for the olive oil
While whisking, slowly add in the oil to emulsify and make a creamy vinaigrette
Pour vinaigrette over salad and mix to combine, adding more salt and pepper if needed

ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup fresh Meyer lemon juice (or 3 tablespoons fresh lemon juice combined with 1 tablespoon orange juice)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions.text: To roast garlic, fold a piece of aluminum foil into a square, cut bottoms off garlic cloves, put all in foil square, drizzle with a little olive oil, then wrap the cloves in the foil
Bake garlic in the oven (or toaster oven) at 400°F for about 15 min
Place all ingredients, except for olive oil, in blender and blend until smooth
With the motor running, slowly add oil and blend until emulsified

recipes.title: Creamy Caesar Dressing
notes: The dressing will keep well in the fridge for about a week*

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons freshly squeezed lemon juice, from one lemon
1 cup mayonnaise, best quality such as Hellmann's Real

instructions.text: In a medium bowl, whisk together the garlic, anchovy paste, lemon juice, Dijon mustard and Worcestershire sauce
In a large measuring glass, add the mayonnaise, and pour in the above mixture, then whisk well or immersion blend until combined
Add Parmigiano-Reggiano, salt, pepper and whisk or blend again until combined
Taste and adjust to your liking
Slice tomatoes, onion and mozzarella cheese (if not pre-sliced)
Lay slices of tomatoes down on a plate, followed by onion slices, then basil and top with mozzarella
Drizzle balsamic glaze and vinegar
Season with salt and pepper

ingredients.raw: 1 pound chicken breasts, cooked and shredded or chopped
ingredients.note: Seasoned with 1/4 teaspoon:

ingredients.raw: 1 pound cooked rotini pasta or similar
2 tablespoons chopped fresh oregano (or 2 teaspoons dried)

Goes well in a salad with lettuce, slivered almonds and drained mandarin oranges

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper
In a large bowl, evenly drizzle the olive oil over the bread chunks.
Evenly sprinkle the Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt and pepper over the bread, then toss gently until well-combined
Spread the bread cubes out in a single layer on a baking sheet
Bake for 10 minutes
Flip the croutons over and cook for another 10 minutes until golden brown and crispy
Remove from oven, and let cool completely, then serv

ingredients.raw: Kale, coated and massaged with olive oil and salt
1/2 cup dry quinoa, cooked according to package instructions
1 small apple, peeled and sliced thin
Balsamic vinaigrette (store bought or use half of recipe for homemade)
1-1 ¼ pounds butternut squash, peeled and diced small

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F convection, line a baking sheet with foil, and spray with olive oil
Peel butternut squash and dice into small cubes
In a bowl, combine butternut squash, olive oil, and brown sugar and mix until coated
Spread butternut squash on baking sheet and bake in oven for 30 minutes until crispy
Prep kale by pulling it off stalks, ripping it into bite size pieces and rinsing it with water
On a baking sheet, spread the kale out, drizzle with olive oil, and sprinkle with salt
Massage the kale until it softens and is completely coated, then divide into serving bowls
Make balsamic vinaigrette and set aside
Cook quinoa according to package instructions
Line another small baking sheet with foil and lay salmon, skin side down, on the sheet
Massage salmon with a little bit of avocado oil
Season salmon with salt, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper
Once butternut squash is done, lower oven temperature to 375°F bake and cook salmon for 15 minutes until fully cooked
Peel and thinly slice an apple just before ready to serve salad
Begin to build your salad by placing ingredients in portions around the bowl: quinoa, butternut squash, apple slices, dried cranberries, candied pecans
Slide salmon off of baking sheet, removing the skin underneath with a spatula and lay on top of salad
Sprinkle with feta cheese, and drizzle with balsamic dressing

notes: Can serve more people as a side*

Once pasta is cooked, drain and rinse under cold water to cool down
Add pasta to a large bowl
Add tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, cheese, salami, and pepperoni to bowl and mix to combine
In a measuring glass, add all of the ingredients for the dressing and whisk until combined
Drizzle dressing over pasta salad and mix to combine

1/2 large or 1 whole small red or sweet onion, diced large

In another small bowl, whisk lemon juice, olive oil, and mustard until completely emulsified
Add salt and pepper to dressing and mix
Add mint or basil to salad bowl
When ready to serve, drizzle dressing over salad

About 1/3 cup red onion chopped small

1/2 large avocado pitted, peeled, and chopped small

instructions.text: Cook the quinoa and refrigerate until ready to use
Combine ingredients for the dressing and set aside
In a separate bowl, add all of the ingredients for the salad
Pour over the dressing and mix

ingredients.raw: 1 cup uncooked quinoa, rinsed in a fine-mesh colander
3/4 cup chopped red onion (from 1 small red onion)

instructions.text: To cook the quinoa, combine the rinsed quinoa and the water in a medium saucepan
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat, then decrease the heat to maintain a gentle simmer
Cook, uncovered, until the quinoa has absorbed all of the water, about 15 minutes, reducing heat as time goes on to maintain a gentle simmer
Remove from heat, cover, and let the quinoa rest for 5 minutes, to give it time to fluff up
In a large serving bowl, combine the chickpeas, cucumber, bell pepper, onion and parsley, then set aside
In a small bowl, combine the olive oil, lemon juice, vinegar, garlic and salt and whisk until blended, then set aside
Once the quinoa is mostly cool, add it to the serving bowl, and drizzle the dressing on top
Toss until the mixture is thoroughly combined
Season with black pepper, to taste, and add an extra pinch of salt if necessary
For best flavor, let the salad rest for 5 to 10 minutes before serving
This salad keeps well in the refrigerator, covered, for about 4 days

recipes.title: Seared Tuna Salad with a Sesame Vinaigrette

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ tablespoons Finely Grated Ginger Root

instructions.text: Add all ingredients but cilantro to the blender
Blend until smooth
Add cilantro and mix well

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons finely minced shallots, from one shallot
1 (6-7 ounce) bag or carton baby spinach (about 6-7 cups)
2 cups (1 pint) stemmed and sliced strawberries
You may have a bit left over but you'll use most of it

instructions.text: Mix raspberry vinegar, vegetable oil, honey, Dijon mustard, shallots, salt and pepper in a small sealable container and shake vigorously to blend (honey shouldn’t stick to bottom)
Combine vinegar, honey, mustard, shallots, salt and pepper in a separate medium bowl
Whisking the medium bowl constantly, slowly drizzle in oil until completely combined
In a large bowl, combine baby spinach, strawberries, scallions and pecans
Add the dressing little by little and toss until greens are well-coated
Add more salt and pepper, if needed, and sprinkle crumbled goat cheese to top

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons fresh lime juice, from one lime

instructions.text: Combine all of the ingredients except for the cilantro in a blender and process until completely smooth
Add cilantro and blend for a few seconds until the cilantro is finely chopped
Refrigerate until ready to serve

ingredients.raw: 6 6-ounce cans white meat tuna packed in water, drained
1/4 cup + 2 tablespoons minced celery (about 1 celery stick)
1/4 cup + 2 tablespoons diced red onion, soaked in cold water for 5 minutes and drained

instructions.text: Prepare hard boiled eggs, then dice
Drain tuna and add to a large mixing bowl
Mix in celery, red onion, parsley, mayonnaise, mustard, and relish
Season with black pepper and finish with a squeeze of lemon juice

ingredients.note: Can substitute with white wine vinegar

instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine ground mustard, vinegar, olive oil, honey, dry oregano, salt & pepper and whisk

recipes.title: Basic Chicken
notes: Breast: 155-160°F
Everything else: 175-180°F

ingredients.raw: Best to marinade it overnight but can do for a minimum of 1 hour
May need more or less time depending on thickness
Depending on thickness, may need to cook longer for thicker pieces

instructions.text: Coat chicken with whichever sauce you choose (Italian dressing, BBQ sauce, salt & pepper, etc.)
Grill the chicken on one side for about 10 minutes until it starts to brown/ becomes more firm
Flip and cook the other side for about 8 minutes or until chicken reaches proper temperature
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat
Add chicken to skillet and cook for 3-5 minutes until it starts to brown/ becomes more firm
Turn over and cook for about 3-5 minutes, testing chicken with thermometer
Continue cooking until chicken reaches proper temperature
Preheat oven to 400°F convection and spray a baking tin with non-stick spray
Place chicken in oven and bake for 20 minutes
Check temperature of chicken and continue cooking for a few additional minutes at a time until chicken reaches proper temperature

recipes.title: BBQ Pulled Chicken
notes: Goes well with Mac and Cheese or in a sandwich (or in grilled cheese)

ingredients.note: Onion powder Garlic powder

ingredients.raw: Could also just sprinkle directly on without measuring if you don’t have time
A quick way to shred is to use a hand mixer on the chicken to break it up

instructions.text: Sprinkle salt and pepper over chicken
In a small bowl, make the BBQ dry rub by mixing brown sugar, kosher salt, black pepper, onion powder, garlic powder, smoked paprika
Sprinkle the dry rub on all sides of the cut chicken breasts and press the seasoning in so it will stick to the chicken
Pour 1 cup water, Worcestershire sauce, and apple cider vinegar into the Instant Pot, place the trivet over the liquid and put all the seasoned chicken breasts on to the trivet
Set Instant Pot to High pressure for 7 minutes
When cook time is over, let natural release for another 10 minutes, then release any pressure
Drain all but about 1/4 cup of the juices from the Instant Pot and shred chicken
Set Instant Pot to saute an add in the BBQ sauce and mix until bubbly and the chicken is well coated, then serve

recipes.title: Braised Chicken Thighs with Mushrooms and Leeks
notes: https://www.skinnytaste.com/braised-chicken-thighs-with-mushrooms-and-leeks/

ingredients.raw: 2 large or 3 medium leeks, whites only
8 5-ounce (about 40 ounces total) pieces bone-in, skin-off chicken thighs, trimmed
2 tablespoons flour, or gluten-free flour mix

ingredients.note: Can substitute with water mixed with chicken bouillon

ingredients.raw: If using breast cook for less time, about 2-3 minutes

instructions.text: Rinse the leeks well and separate the layers to make sure there is no dirt, then slice into 1/4-inch thick half moons
Preheat the oven to 375°F
Mix together 1 ¼ teaspoons of salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper and the paprika and season all sides of the chicken
Heat a large Dutch oven or oven safe skillet on high heat and spray with olive oil
Once hot, cook the chicken until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes on each side, adjusting the heat as needed
Set aside and repeat with the remaining chicken, then set aside
Reduce the heat to medium-low and add the butter
When butter starts to foam, add the leeks and cook, stirring until soft, about 6-8 minutes
Add the mushrooms, 1/4 teaspoon salt, stir and cook covered for 5 minutes, stirring until they release their juices
Sprinkle flour over them, stir in chicken broth and sherry, and simmer for 2-3 minutes
Return the chicken to the Dutch oven, spooning the mushroom-sherry sauce over them and cook in the oven, covered, for 40-50 minutes, until cooked through and tender

ingredients.raw: 4-5 ounces of boneless, skinless chicken breast, diced small
1 tablespoon hot sauce (or less if want more mild)
Shredded cheese (Mixed blend, cheddar jack, mexican, etc.)

instructions.text: Season diced chicken with garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper
Coat chicken in buffalo sauce and ranch
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a pan on medium
Cook chicken for 3-4 minutes until fully cooked
Transfer chicken to a bowl and cover with foil
Spread butter on one side of the tortilla
Place tortilla, buttered side down, on a griddle or pan
Sprinkle shredded cheese over the bottom of the tortilla
Top half of the tortilla with chicken and bacon
Drizzle ranch and hot sauce over chicken and bacon
Add more cheese, if desired
Turn griddle on to 300°F or stove on medium heat
Cook for 3-4 minutes until cheese begins to melt and outside of tortilla starts to brown
Once tortilla is browning, fold in half
Continue to cook until cheese is melted, flipping quesadilla occasionally to prevent burning

ingredients.raw: 1 ¼ pound boneless skinless chicken breast, sliced thin lengthwise
4-6 slices fresh mozzarella, enough to cover chicken

instructions.text: Season the chicken with the italian seasoning, salt and pepper on both sides
Preheat oven to 425°F
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in an oven safe skillet over medium heat
Add onions and garlic to skillet and saute for about 3 minutes until soft, but not browned, then remove from pan and set aside
Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the skillet and increase heat to medium high
Sear chicken on each side about 4 minutes until brown and cooked through
Add balsamic vinegar to pan with chicken and bring to a simmer
Add back the onions and garlic and add the tomatoes to the pan
Place cheese on top of the chicken and sprinkle with the shredded basil
Transfer skillet to oven and let cook for 3-5 minutes until cheese has melted
Drizzle with balsamic glaze and serve

ingredients.raw: 1 pint cherry or grape tomatoes, halved
4 ounces mozzarella pearls (1 cup) or diced fresh mozzarella
1/4 cup tightly packed fresh basil leaves (thinly sliced)
The total cooking time will vary depending upon the size of your chicken

instructions.text: In a small saucepan, combine 1/2 cup of the balsamic vinegar and 1 tablespoon of the honey
Gently simmer over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until thickened and reduced by a little more than half, about 15 minutes
Remove from the heat and set aside
Place 1 chicken breast in a resealable plastic bag or between two pieces of plastic wrap
Using a meat pounder, rolling pin, or small skillet, pound chicken breast into an even ½- inch thickness, then repeat with remaining 3 breasts
Sprinkle the chicken with 3/4 teaspoon of the salt and 1/4 teaspoon of the pepper, seasoning both sides
In a large, heavy-bottomed skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of the oil over medium-high heat
Once the oil is hot but not smoking, add the chicken breasts top-sides down, being careful of oil that may splatter
Let cook undisturbed for 4 minutes, or until lightly browned
Flip and cook an additional 3 minutes, then check for doneness
If it needs additional time, ﬂip again and continue cooking for a few minutes on each side, until done
Remove to a plate and cover to keep warm
Without wiping out the pan, reduce the skillet heat to medium

Add the remaining tablespoon of oil, swirl to coat the pan, then add the red onion and let cook until slightly softened, about 3 minutes, scraping the browned bits off the bottom of the pan as it cooks
Add the garlic and let cook 30 seconds, just until fragrant

Add the tomatoes, thyme, and remaining 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar, 1 teaspoon honey, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper, then increase the heat back to medium high and cook until the tomatoes begin to soften, about 2 minutes
Return the chicken to the pan, nestling it into the tomatoes, and scatter the mozzarella throughout
Cover the pan and remove from the heat
Let stand for 1 to 2 minutes to allow the mozzarella to become soft and melty
Uncover, drizzle the balsamic reduction over the top, and sprinkle with the fresh basil

ingredients.raw: 1 ¼ - 1 ½ pound boneless skinless chicken breast, sliced thin lengthwise

instructions.text: Season chicken with salt, pepper, and garlic powder
In a shallow dish, mix together eggs and minced garlic
In another shallow dish, add breadcrumbs and parmesan cheese
Dip the chicken into the egg mixture, followed by the breadcrumbs
Heat canola oil in a pan on medium high
Once shimmering, add chicken to pan and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side until cooked through

ingredients.raw: About 1.5 - 2 pounds boneless chicken tenders or breast cut into strips
3/4 cup flour, plus 1 ½ cups flour, divided

ingredients.note: Dried oregano Granulated garlic Onion powder Smoked or regular paprika

ingredients.raw: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions.text: Marinate chicken tenders in buttermilk mixture for at least 1 hour or up to overnight.
Preheat the oven to 425°F convection
Spread the panko bread crumbs on a baking sheet and put in the oven for about 5 minutes until brown (watch so it doesn’t burn!)
Prepare a baking sheet with foil, place a wire rack on top, and spray with cooking spray
For the breading, whisk together in a shallow plate the panko, 1 ½ cups flour, the cornstarch, spices, and 1 teaspoon each salt and black pepper
Place the remaining 3/4 cups flour in a separate shallow plate
Pour buttermilk, garlic, and hot sauce into a medium bowl and crack the eggs into same bowl and whisk
Remove the chicken from the brine and pat dry
Dip the chicken into the flour, then the buttermilk/eggs mixture, then panko mixture lifting the mixture on top of it and pressing it down firmly until it sticks to the chicken
Transfer to the wired cooking rack and repeat with remaining chicken pieces
Spray chicken pieces with olive oil spray - covering the dry flour spots - on both sides
Cook for 25-30 minutes, or until chicken is golden brown and cooked through

ingredients.raw: 1-2 pound boneless skinless chicken breast (as much as needed)
1-2 tablespoons fresh coarsely ground black pepper

instructions.text: Rub each piece of chicken with a tablespoon of the seasoning and a little bit of olive oil
Place in a ziploc bag and refrigerate for 4 hours up to overnight
Grill, bake or saute chicken until done
Slice thin and serve

ingredients.raw: 2-3 cups cooked chicken, chopped (about 1 ¼ pounds chicken)
Can use a rolling pin to roll lattice onto pin and then unroll it onto baking dish

instructions.text: Allow puff pastry to thaw and then gently unfold
Cook chicken and chop
In a large pot, melt butter over medium-high heat
Add chopped onions, celery, carrots, garlic and salt and saute until onions are translucent and vegetables start to get tender
Sprinkle flour over vegetables and cook for 1-2 minutes to cook flour until it starts to brown
Gradually whisk in milk, heavy cream and “Better than Bullion” and bring to a slow boil
Simmer gently over medium heat until sauce begins to thicken, about 5 minutes
Lower heat to low and add chicken, peas and thyme, and mix all together
Preheat oven to 400°F
Pour chicken mixture into a 9x9-inch baking dish
On a clean, floured surface, lay puff pastry down and roll it out slightly so it's flat
Cut the puff pastry into 12 equal strips, using fold lines as a guide
While still on the surface, weave together the puff pastry strips, forming a lattice pattern
Once lattice is created, trim edges so it will just fit the baking dish
Transfer formed lattice from working surface to baking dish
Using a pastry brush, brush egg onto the top of the puff pastry
Bake in oven for 30-35 minutes until the top of the puff pastry is a golden brown color

ingredients.raw: 1 6-ounce box Uncle Ben’s Long Grain Wild Rice

ingredients.note: You can add another can of water for moister rice

ingredients.raw: About 1 ½ pounds chicken (About 6 thighs or 3 breasts)
ingredients.note: However much fits in the pan

ingredients.raw: If using boneless chicken, can probably cut time down to 1 hour

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Grease a 9x13-inch pan
Add the box of rice and seasoning to the bottom of the pan
Add the cans of celery and mushroom soup, and one can of water and mix all together
Arrange the raw chicken on top of the rice mixture and spray skin with olive oil cooking spray
Cover and seal with tin foil
Bake at 350°F for 2 hours
Remove foil and bake another 30 minutes so that the chicken browns on top

ingredients.raw: 3 of the white parts of scallions

instructions.text: Coat chicken with italian dressing
Grill the chicken on one side for 10 minutes
Flip and cook the other side for about 8 minutes or until chicken reaches 155°F (this temp is for breasts only)
Dice grilled chicken, hard boiled egg, celery stick and scallions
Mix in a bowl and add mayonnaise
Add a little salt and pepper for taste

recipes.title: Cider-Braised Chicken With Apples, Onions & Thyme

ingredients.raw: 2 small yellow onions, halved and peeled

ingredientGroups.name: Bread, noodles, or potatoes, for serving

ingredients.raw: You can rotate the pan as needed for even browning, but don’t disturb the chicken or onions
(If not, wait another minute or so

instructions.text: Ingredients

instructionGroups.name: Bread, noodles, or potatoes, for serving

instructions.text: Heat the oven to 400°F
Pat dry the chicken thighs
Heat a large, ovenproof skillet (at least 10-12 inches in diameter) until hot over medium-high heat, then add the oil and swirl to coat
Add the thighs, skin side down, and sprinkle with kosher salt
Sear chicken for 3 minutes, then add the onion halves, cut side down, and sear for another 5 minutes
Flip the onions
Check the chicken and if the skin is golden, flip the chicken
Cook for 3 minutes, then add the apples, cut side down, scooting the onions and chicken as needed
Throw in the thyme sprigs and season everything with salt and pepper
Cook the apples for 4-7 minutes, until they’re evenly browned, then flip them over
Carefully pour in the cider (it will boil vigorously) and let it simmer for 4-8 minutes, until reduced by about half
Add the stock and bring to a boil
Transfer the pan to the oven and roast for 10-15 minutes, until the chicken thighs are cooked
Season to taste with salt and pepper
Optional - thicken the gravy with a mix of 1/2 tablespoon of cold water and cornstarch
Serve with something starchy, like crusty bread, noodles, or potatoes, to soak up all the sauce

ingredients.raw: 3/4 - 1 pound chicken breast, thinly sliced or diced into 1-inch cubes
1 tablespoon cilantro, finely chopped (or 1 teaspoon dried)
1 ½ tablespoons basil, finely chopped (or 1 teaspoon dried)
Veggies of your choice (such as zucchini, onion, peppers, etc.)
If this is too difficult you can also just pierce the skewer with individual 1-inch pieces of chicken
1 ½ cups heavy cream or half and half
1 ½ cups Parmesan cheese, freshly grated, plus extra for garnish
Optional: Freshly chopped parsley, for garnish

instructions.text: Mix all ingredients except the chicken to make a marinade
Set aside 3 tablespoons of marinade for the veggies and refrigerate until ready to cook
Add the majority of the marinade to a ziplock bag with chicken and marinade for at least 1 hour to overnight
When ready to cook, mix reserved marinade with veggies to coat
Preheat oven to 450°F and line a baking sheet with tin foil
Pierce the bottom of each chicken slice onto a skewer and weave the chicken through the skewer, then lay on baking sheet
Bake for about 15 minutes or until chicken is fully cooked, then broil for about 3 minutes
To the same pan, add shallot and garlic and cook until shallot has softened and garlic is fragrant, about 2 minutes
Add the orzo and cook with shallot and garlic for 2 minutes to toast orzo
Add chicken stock, heavy cream, lemon juice, and Dijon mustard and stir to combine
Raise temperature and bring to a boil
Once boiling, reduce head to low so that it is simmering
Cover and cook for 10-12 minutes until liquid is almost absorbed
Stir in parmesan cheese and season with salt and pepper
Add spinach leaves and mix to wilt spinach, then cover and allow to sit for 2 minutes

ingredients.raw: 4 ounces (1 ¼ cups) shredded parmesan cheese
If they are much thicker than 3/4" thick (at the thickest point), pound a bit
DO NOT MOVE chicken, otherwise the cheese will slide off the surface before it adheres!
It adheres once it turns crispy
Lift a corner to take a peek and turn with tongs when it's deep golden brown
Again, do not move until golden
Don't cook longer than 6 minutes in total as it dries out the chicken

instructions.text: Cut each breast in half horizontally to make 4 steaks
Place egg, garlic, pinch of salt and pepper in a bowl and whisk with a fork
Place parmesan in a shallow bowl
Dredge chicken in egg, then place in parmesan (CUT SIDE DOWN), press, then turn
Coat with extra parmesan, press, shake off excess, and repeat with remaining chicken
Melt butter and heat oil in a large skillet over high heat
Place chicken in a non-stick skillet (or a well-seasoned cast iron skillet), press down lightly with spatula, and cook for about 3 minutes
Cook the other side for 3 minutes or until also deep golden brown and crispy and lightly press down with spatula
Serve and enjoy

ingredients.note: Could also try substituting with yogurt

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ tablespoons Everything bagel seasoning, plus more for topping
1/2 cup milk or half and half
1 tablespoon water mixed with 1/2 tablespoons cornstarch, if needed

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 425°F
Cut a pocket through the side of each chicken breast
In a small bowl, mix cream cheese, garlic, everything bagel seasoning and red pepper flakes together
Stuff mixture into chicken breast pockets
Sprinkle everything bagel seasoning on the bottom and top of the chicken
In another bowl, combine ingredients for the sauce, except the cornstarch mixture
Pour sauce into a baking pan
Add the chicken to the sauce
Bake in the oven for about 30 minutes or until chicken is cooked through
Move chicken to a separate plate
If the sauce is too thin, pour sauce into a pot, turn the heat on to medium, and slowly whisk the cornstarch mixture into the sauce until it reaches desired thickness
Remove sauce from heat, pour over chicken and enjoy

ingredients.raw: 1/2 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes, more or less to taste
1 pound mixed baby potatoes, halved if large
1/2 cup dry white wine, such as Pinot Grigio or Sauvignon Blanc
1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 1 tablespoon cold beef broth or water

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F
Melt together the 1 tablespoon each of olive oil and butter in a large oven safe skillet over medium-high heat
Season the chicken with salt and black pepper
Place chicken breasts in pan and cook for 3 minutes per side
Remove chicken from skillet and place on a plate, then cover to keep warm
Add another tablespoon each of the olive oil and butter and melt
Add the onions and season with salt and pepper
Cook 10-15 minutes, stirring frequently, until softened, deep golden in color and caramelized
Add the thyme and red pepper flakes, and continue cooking another 3-5 minutes
Add the potatoes, tossing to coat, then reduce the heat to medium
Pour in the wine, broth and worchestire sauce
Arrange the chicken over the potatoes, skin side facing up, and season with salt and pepper
Transfer to the oven and roast for 30-40 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through, and the potatoes fork tender
Remove skillet from oven and carefully transfer the chicken to a plate
Heat the skillet to medium heat and rizzle the cornstarch mixture into the onion and potatoes and mix well until sauce thickens
Place chicken back into skillet on the potato and onion and sprinkle with the Gruyère cheese
Place skillet under broiler until cheese gets melty, brown, and bubbly

ingredients.raw: About 2 ½ pounds boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs trimmed (can also use bone-in pieces, but will need to cook longer)
3/4 cup flour, plus 1 ½ cups flour, divided

ingredients.note: Dried oregano Granulated garlic Onion powder Smoked or regular paprika Kosher salt Black pepper

instructions.text: If needed, cut the chicken so they measure to be about 4-6 ounces
Place all chicken in a plastic zip-lock bag
Pour buttermilk, hot sauce, garlic, paprika, 1 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper in bag
Seal bag, press out air and use hands to massage marinade into meat until evenly coated
Refrigerate, letting chicken soak for at least 1 hour to overnight
When ready to cook, remove chicken from the bag and place on wire rack to drain
Preheat the oven to 425°F convection
Spread the panko bread crumbs on a baking sheet and put in the oven for about 5 minutes until brown (watch so it doesn’t burn!)
Prepare a baking sheet with foil, place a wire rack on top, and spray with cooking spray
For the breading, whisk together in a shallow plate the panko, 1 ½ cups flour, the cornstarch, and spices
Place the remaining 3/4 cups flour in a separate shallow plate
Pour buttermilk and hot sauce into a medium bowl and crack the eggs into same bowl and whisk
Remove the chicken from the brine and pat dry
Dip the chicken into the flour, then the buttermilk/ eggs mixture, then panko mixture lifting the mixture on top of it and pressing it down firmly until it sticks to the chicken
Transfer to the wired cooking rack and repeat with remaining chicken pieces
Spray chicken pieces with olive oil spray - covering the dry flour spots - on both sides
Cook for 25-30 minutes, or until chicken is golden brown and cooked through

ingredients.raw: About 2 ½ pounds boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs trimmed
Canola oil or other light, neutral oil (avocado, vegetable, etc.)

instructions.text: If needed, cut the chicken so they measure to be about 4-6 ounces
Place all chicken in a plastic zip-lock bag
Pour buttermilk, hot sauce, garlic, paprika, 1 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper in bag
Seal bag, press out air and use hands to massage marinade into meat until evenly coated
Refrigerate, letting chicken soak for at least 1 hour to overnight
When ready to cook, remove chicken from the bag and place on wire rack to drain
For dredging, in a large bowl, whisk together buttermilk, eggs, and hot sauce
In a second large bowl, whisk together flour, cornstarch, salt, and garlic powder
Place a wire rack on a rimmed baking sheet
Dip each chicken thigh first in the flour mixture, then in the buttermilk, then again in the flour mixture, shaking off any excess
Transfer to a wire rack, then repeat until all chicken thighs are coated
Fill a large cast iron or heavy bottom pan with oil until it’s about 1 ½” high
Heat over medium-high until oil reaches ~350°F (chicken should be cooked around ~325°F, but temperature will drop when you add the chicken)

When oil is hot, carefully add the chicken thighs (about 4 at a time, depending on the size of your pan) and cook, turning every 1-2 minutes, until all chicken is a golden brown color and has an internal temperature of 160°F, about 6-7 minutes per batch
Transfer chicken to a clean wire rack, then repeat cooking remaining chicken

ingredients.raw: Fried Chicken from one of recipes above
Nashville Hot Sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
Waffles (store bought or see recipe for homemade; follow additional ingredients for savory waffles)
Syrup (store bought or see recipe for homemade)

instructions.text: Prepare chicken and brine using above recipe
While chicken is soaking/ before doing anything more with the chicken, make the waffle batter and start making waffles, and make the syrup if making homemade
Once waffles are made and chicken is done marinating, follow the rest of the above recipe to make and cook the chicken
When chicken is almost done cooking, or before serving, warm syrup if it cooled and warm waffles in the oven at 200°F

ingredients.raw: Fried Chicken from one of recipes above
Nashville Hot Sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
14-ounce bag of coleslaw mix (or other mixed cabbage blend)
Ranch dressing, blue dressing, spicy mayo, or any other desired sauce
Cut chicken to a size that will fit on a bun; if one piece of chicken is too thin/ isn’t enough, do 2 pieces per person and stack on sandwich

instructions.text: Prepare chicken and brine using above recipe
While chicken is soaking/ before doing anything more with the chicken, make the sandwich slaw by combining all ingredients, except for the slaw, in a large measuring cup, then whisk until combined
Pour over slaw in a large mixing bowl, then use tongs to thoroughly toss
Refrigerate slaw until ready to eat, or for 1 hour if able
If cooking the chicken in the oven, follow the rest of the above recipe to cook the chicken, then make the Nashville Hot Honey Sauce while chicken is cooking
If frying chicken on the stove, first prepare the Nashville Hot Honey Sauce, then turn off heat and leave on stove top, then follow the rest of the above recipe to cook the chicken
When chicken is done cooking, reheat sauce to get a thinner consistency, then use a brush to immediately brush onto both sides of each piece of chicken
To serve, add any desired condiments to brioche buns, then add chicken, and top with slaw and pickles

ingredients.raw: About 1.5 - 2 pounds boneless chicken tenders or breast cut into strips
