Can also cook in the oven by preheating the oven to 425°F, lining a baking sheet with foil, place a wire rack on top, and spray with cooking spray
Can also cook in oven for 20 minutes
Cook in oven for another 8 minutes

instructions.text: Marinate chicken tenders in chicken brine mixture for at least 1 hour or up to overnight.
Preheat the oven to 425°F convection
Optional: Spread the panko bread crumbs on a baking sheet and put in the oven for about 5 minutes until brown (watch so it doesn’t burn!)
Prepare air fryer by spraying with cooking spray
Place 3/4 cups flour in a separate shallow plate
For the breading, whisk together in a shallow plate the panko, parmesan cheese, cornstarch, garlic powder, onion powder, basil, paprika, and salt and black pepper
Pour buttermilk, garlic, and hot sauce into a medium bowl and crack the eggs into same bowl and whisk
Remove the chicken from the brine and pat dry
Dip the chicken into the flour, then the buttermilk/eggs mixture, then panko mixture lifting the mixture on top of it and pressing it down firmly until it sticks to the chicken
Transfer to the wired cooking rack and repeat with remaining chicken pieces
Spray chicken pieces with olive oil spray - covering the dry flour spots - on both sides
Air fry at 400°F for 10 minutes
After 10 minutes, flip chicken and cook for another 8 minutes
Remove from air fryer and serve

ingredients.raw: 1 ¼ pounds boneless chicken breasts or thighs cut into cubes
2/3 cup heavy cream or half and half

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 450°F
Line a baking sheet with tin foil and spray with nonstick spray, then place a rack over the foil, if available
In a bowl, toss together the chicken, smoked paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, Kosher salt, and pepper, then add the flour and parmesan and toss again
Add the olive oil, tossing to coat until all the flour and parmesan sticks to the chicken
Arrange the chicken evenly on rack over the baking sheet
Bake for 10 minutes, then flip chicken pieces and continue cooking for another 7-10 minutes until fully cooked
While the chicken is cooking, begin making the sauce by first heating the butter in a small saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring constantly just until it begins to brown, about 3-5 minutes
Stir in the garlic and then slowly pour in the cream
Whisk in the parmesan and cayenne, then season with Kosher salt and black pepper
Simmer the sauce for 3-5 minutes, whisking until smooth, then keep warm over low heat
Pour the parmesan sauce over the chicken and toss the chicken up in the sauce
Top chicken with a sprinkling of parsley, if desired

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts sliced thin
1 cup Greek yogurt (you can use reduced fat or fat free Greek yogurt if you like)
Kosher salt, generous pinch to taste
Black pepper, generous pinch to taste

instructions.text: Combine the marinade ingredients and mix well
Add the chicken and marinade to a gallon size ziploc and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes or up to 24 hrs (better this way)
When ready to cook, shake off marinade and grill the chicken until done.
Remove chicken from the grill and slice into strips.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a pan on medium heat
Saute the chicken for 3-4 minutes until the edges are browned
Serve in flat pita bread

recipes.title: Honey Hot Wing Style Chicken Thighs

ingredients.raw: 6-8 bone in, skin on chicken thighs
Check for browning and rotate pan if some pieces are browning faster than others

instructions.text: Line a pan (or two) with tin foil and add a wire rack on top of each
To prep the thighs, remove them from the packaging and pat dry with paper towel
Loosen any extra chicken skin folded under the thigh and cut off any excessively long pieces
Generously salt all over both the skin and flesh sides of the chicken
Place the thighs skin side up on the wire rack lined sheet pan skin side up (keep extra skin unfolded on the rack facing up as well)
Transfer to the fridge uncovered for at least 1 hour to overnight
Remove from the fridge and allow the chicken to sit at room temp for about 15 min before cooking
Preheat your oven to 400°F
Pat the chicken dry with paper towel and dust off any excess salt
Add a tablespoon of oil to a large cold pan, preferably stainless steel or cast iron
Place chicken skin side down in a cold pan and the pan over medium low
Let the chicken cook undisturbed for 7-10 minutes until the skin is a deep golden brown
Transfer the chicken back to the foil and wire rack lined sheet pan
Transfer the chicken to the oven and cook for 45 minutes
When the chicken has about 15 minutes left, prepare the sauce by melting butter in a small pot and mixing in hot sauce and honey, stirring until it reaches a low boil and starts to thicken
Once sauce thickens, remove from heat, and set aside
When chicken thighs are done, toss in a bowl with sauce to completely coat chicken
Serve and drizzle with additional sauce, if desired

ingredients.raw: About 1.5 - 2 pounds boneless chicken tenders or breast cut into strips
3/4 cup flour, plus 1 ½ cups flour, divided

ingredients.note: Dried oregano Granulated garlic Onion powder Smoked or regular paprika Kosher salt Black Pepper

ingredients.raw: Can also cook in the oven by preheating the oven to 425°F, lining a baking sheet with foil, place a wire rack on top, and spray with cooking spray
Can also cook in oven for 20 minutes
Cook in oven for another 5 minutes
Cook in oven for another 3 minutes

instructions.text: Marinate chicken tenders in buttermilk mixture for at least 1 hour or up to overnight
Optional: Spread the panko bread crumbs on a baking sheet and put in the oven for about 5 minutes until brown (watch so it doesn’t burn!)
Prepare air fryer by spraying with cooking spray
For the breading, whisk together in a shallow plate the panko, 1 ½ cups flour, the cornstarch, and spices
Place the remaining 3/4 cups flour in a separate shallow plate
Pour buttermilk, garlic, and hot sauce into a medium bowl and crack the eggs into same bowl and whisk
Remove the chicken from the brine and pat dry

Set up your workstation so that you can do the following in order: Dip the chicken into the flour, then the buttermilk/eggs mixture, then panko mixture lifting the mixture on top of it and pressing it down firmly until it sticks to the chicken
Transfer to the air fryers or wired cooking rack and repeat with remaining chicken pieces
Spray chicken pieces with olive oil spray - covering the dry flour spots
Air fry at 400°F for 10 minutes
While chicken is cooking, prepare the sauce by melting butter in a small pot and mixing in hot sauce and honey, stirring until it reaches a low boil and starts to thicken
Once sauce thickens, remove from heat, and set aside
After 10 minutes, flip chicken, spray other side with olive oil spray and cook for another 5 minutes
Add chicken to a bowl and coat in the sauce, mixing carefully to prevent breading from falling off
Air fry at 450°F for another 3 minutes
Remove from air fryer, paint with remaining sauce, and serve

recipes.title: Honey Mustard Chicken and Roasted Potatoes

ingredients.raw: 4 chicken leg quarters or bone-in breasts, skin removed
2 pounds little potatoes cut into 1/2” cubes

ingredients.note: Paprika Kosher salt Pepper

ingredients.raw: Bite size pieces - sometimes may have to cut in quarters

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F
Whisk together honey mustard sauce ingredients in a small bowl, then remove 1/4 cup to coat chicken before it bakes - save the rest of the sauce for after the chicken bakes, then set aside
Spray bottom of a 9x13 baking dish with cooking spray
Cut little potatoes in half
Combine olive oil, seasonings, garlic and rosemary
Combine potatoes with 1 ½ tablespoons of the olive oil mix
Add potatoes to a lightly greased 9x13 baking dish
Combine green beans with the rest 1/2 tablespoon of the olive oil mix and set aside
Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil and all “chicken seasonings” to a bowl, then stir together to make a wet rub
Massage rub onto chicken pieces
Using the 1/4 cup reserved honey mustard sauce, coat both sides of the chicken evenly in sauce
Transfer chicken to baking dish over the potatoes, and spoon over any remaining sauce/spices from the bowl
Cover and bake for 30 minutes, remove pan from oven and remove foil
Remove chicken from pan and add green beans and 2 tablespoons of the honey mustard sauce to the potatoes and mix
Add back chicken and coat with some more honey mustard sauce
Return to oven uncovered and continue to bake an additional 10-15 minutes until chicken is cooked through and registers 175°F
Serve with any remaining honey mustard sauce
Mix all ingredients except the chicken in a measuring cup or bowl
Reserve 1 ⅓ cups of marinade for basting, then cover and refrigerate
Pour the remaining marinade into a gallon sized ziploc bag and add chicken, then refrigerate for at least 8 hours to overnight
Drain chicken, discarding marinade
Grill chicken, on an oiled rack over medium low heat flipping every few minutes and basting with reserved marinade until internal temp reaches 170°F

recipes.title: Not Your Average Joe’s Mustard Crusted Chicken

instructions.text: Whip mustards, oil, lemon juice, chopped garlic, salt and pepper in a clean large mixing bowl
Pour marinade over chicken and marinate overnight
Once marinated, coat each chicken breast liberally with panko bread crumbs
Let breaded chicken sit a few hours so crumbs become absorbed into chicken
Preheat oven to 400°F
Brown chicken breast in a sauté pan until golden brown on one side
Turn over and cook on the other side until golden brown
Transfer saute pan to oven and continue to cook until internal temperature reaches 165°F, approximately 10-15 minutes

ingredients.raw: 2-3 ounces of cashews (depending on the size of breast)
A little bit of flour just to coat it (maybe 2 tablespoons)
2 tablespoons of butter or enough olive oil to coat the bottom of the pan

instructions.text: Crush the cashews
Beat the egg
Mix in salt, pepper, orange juice and zest with egg
Sprinkle flour on chicken just to coat it
Dip the chicken in the egg mix
Lay one side of chicken on the crushed cashews
Coat bottom of frying pan with butter or olive oil
Put chicken in pan with cashew side down
Add rest of cashews to side of chicken facing up
Let it cook on medium for 6 minutes
Flip over chicken and cook for 4 minutes
Check for temperature to be at least 155°F, no more than 165°F

ingredients.raw: 2-3 ounces pistachios (depending on the size of breast)
A little bit of flour just to coat it (maybe 2 tablespoons)
Salt (unless already in pistachios) and pepper
2 tablespoons butter or enough olive oil to coat the bottom of the pan

instructions.text: Crush the pistachios
Beat the egg
Mix in salt, pepper with egg
Sprinkle flour on chicken just to coat it
Dip the chicken in the egg mix
Lay one side of chicken on the crushed pistachios
Coat bottom of frying pan with butter or olive oil
Put chicken in pan with pistachio side down
Add rest of pistachios to side of chicken facing up
Let it cook on medium for 6 minutes
Flip over chicken and cook for 4 minutes
Check for temperature to be at least 155°F, no more than 165°F

recipes.title: Ratatouille Baked Chicken
notes: https://www.skinnytaste.com/ratatouille-baked-chicken

ingredients.raw: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
1 large eggplant, peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes
2 medium zucchini, cut into ½-inch dice
1 yellow bell pepper, cut into ½-inch squares
5 vine-ripened tomatoes, cut into ½-inch dice
1 cup fresh basil leaves, finely chopped
If using breast cook for less time, about 2-3 minutes

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
Add 1/2 tablespoon olive oil to a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and heat until shimmering
Pat the chicken dry with paper towels, season on all sides with salt and pepper, and set the chicken down in the pan
Cook the chicken, without moving, until nicely browned and the meat releases from the pan, about 5 minutes
Flip and continue cooking until the other side begins to brown, about 3 minutes
Remove the chicken to a plate and set aside
Reduce heat to medium and add the remaining olive oil, onion, garlic, and a pinch of salt
Cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables begin to soften, about 3 minutes
Add the eggplant and another pinch of salt and cook, stirring occasionally, until the eggplant begins to soften, about 5 minutes
Add the tomato paste and thyme and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute
Add the zucchini, bell pepper, tomatoes, another pinch of salt, and a twist of black pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until all of the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes
Remove the pan from the heat
Partially submerge the chicken into the vegetables
Transfer the pot to the oven and cook, uncovered, until the thickest portion of the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 160°F, about 20 minutes
Remove from the oven and let stand for 10 minutes
Stir in the basil and serve

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons unsalted butter, softened, or olive oil
1/2 teaspoon minced rosemary plus 2 rosemary sprigs
1/2 teaspoon minced thyme plus 2 thyme sprigs
1 head garlic, cut in half crosswise
Keep it warm over a very low flame while you carve the chicken

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F convection roast or 425°F bake and position a rack in the lower third of the oven
In a bowl, mix the butter with the minced garlic, minced herbs and the lemon zest and season with salt and pepper
Take the chicken out of the packaging, remove any excess packaging and giblets (insides of the chicken)
Rinse the chicken under water, cleaning out the inside
Pat the chicken dry (inside and out) and season the inside with salt and pepper
Stuff the cavity with thyme and rosemary sprigs, both halves of the lemon, and all the garlic
Set the chicken breast-side-up on a rack in a roasting pan
Loosen under the skin on the chicken and rub half of the herb butter under the skin breast-side-up
Flip the chicken over and rub the rest of the herb butter over the skin
Flip the chicken back over, breast-side-up, cross the legs over one another and tie with kitchen string making a knot
Tuck the wing tips under the body of the chicken
Scatter the onion slices around the chicken
Roast the chicken for 1 ½-2 hours, or until the juices run clear when you cut between a leg and thigh
Remove to a platter and cover with aluminum foil while you prepare the gravy
Remove all the fat from the bottom of the pan, reserving 2 tablespoons in a small cup
Add the chicken stock and sherry to the pan and cook on high heat for about 5 minutes, until reduced, scraping the bottom of the pan
Combine the 2 tablespoons of chicken fat with the flour and add to the pan
Boil for a few minutes to cook the flour
Strain the gravy into a small saucepan and season with salt and pepper to taste
Slice the chicken onto a platter and serve immediately with the warm gravy

ingredients.raw: Baste with pan juices every 10 minutes

instructions.text: Whisk together all ingredients, except the chicken, in a medium sized bowl
Add chicken to bowl with marinade, cover and marinate overnight
When ready to cook, preheat the oven to 425°F
Place chicken in a baking dish, and roast, uncovered, for about 30 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through
Turn the oven up to broil, and cook for 5 minutes more to get a nice browning crust on the chicken

ingredients.raw: 1 pound boneless skinless chicken breasts (about 2 medium breasts)

ingredients.note: 1/2 teaspoon cumin 1/2 teaspoon paprika 1 teaspoons Israeli Shawarma Seasoning mix (Neptune or Pereg brand) 1/2 teaspoon Zaatar 1/2 teaspoon salt 1/8 teaspoon black pepper

ingredients.raw: Pita bread (store bought or see recipe for homemade)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F convection
Slice chicken breast into small pieces
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, and the spices
Pour the spice marinade over the chicken pieces and stir with a spoon until all pieces are evenly coated
Spray a baking sheet with nonstick cooking spray and place the chicken pieces on the sheet, evenly spaced
Cook in the oven for 15 minutes until chicken is cooked through
Remove chicken from the oven and let it cool slightly
Using a sharp knife, slice the meat into small thin pieces
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a pan on medium heat
Stir garlic until sauteed and pour half the chicken into the skillet, sauteing for 3-4 minutes until the smallest piece of chicken is crisp and brown
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste, if desired
Serve in pita bread
Preheat the oven to 400° and line a baking sheet with foil
In a medium pan, melt butter over medium heat
Once butter is melted, stir in honey, garlic, salt, and pepper, and bring to a boil
Once boiling, reduce heat and let simmer for 5-6 minutes, then remove from heat and set aside
Pound chicken breasts to even thickness and cut potatoes into quarters or sixths, making each potato about the same size for even cooking
Place chicken breasts on the prepared baking sheet and arrange potatoes around the chicken
Drizzle potatoes and chicken with oil and sprinkle evenly with seasonings
Pour honey glaze over chicken and place in the oven
Bake for 25-30 minutes or until chicken is cooked through and potatoes are tender

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon olive oil or avocado oil

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F convection
Place the chicken breasts on a cutting board and using a sharp knife to cut a pocket into the side of each chicken breast
Add the paprika, 1/2 teaspoon salt, garlic powder, and onion powder to a small bowl and stir to combine
Sprinkle seasonings evenly over both sides of the chicken and in the pockets
Microwave spinach for approximately 2 minutes, let cool a little and squeeze out excess water
Add cream cheese, Parmesan, mayonnaise, spinach, garlic, red pepper and remaining ½ teaspoon of salt to a small mixing bowl and stir well to combine
Spoon the spinach mixture into each chicken breast evenly
Close pockets with 1 or 2 toothpicks
Place the chicken breasts in a 9x13 baking dish
Spray breasts with olive oil
Bake, uncovered, for 25-30 minutes or until chicken is cooked through

ingredients.raw: 2 medium bell peppers any color, diced large
1 teaspoon minced or pressed fresh garlic

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 425°F and grease a 2-quart baking dish
In a small bowl, whisk together cornstarch, ketchup, vinegar, brown sugar, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger
Season chicken with salt and pepper
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat
Sear chicken until browned on each side but not cooked through, about 2-3 minutes per side
Place the onion and peppers on the bottom of the baking dish, pour a little of the sauce over them and mix
Place the chicken on top of the vegetables and pour the rest of the sauce over chicken and vegetables
Bake, uncovered, for 25 minutes
Stir, then return to the oven for 5-10 more minutes, or until chicken is cooked through and the sauce has slightly thickened
If you find that your sauce is too thick when the chicken is done baking, you can add a splash of warm water (as necessary), and stir to thin
Serve over hot cooked rice, if desired

ingredients.raw: Can use broiler to caramelize the glaze if necessary

instructions.text: Set oven rack to middle position and preheat oven to 350°F
Line rimmed baking sheet with heavy-duty aluminum foil, then grease the foil with olive oil or non-stick cooking spray
Make the BBQ glaze: In a medium bowl, whisk together the ketchup, brown sugar, cider vinegar, and Dijon mustard, then set aside
Make the Meatloaf: Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat
Add onion and saute until slightly golden brown, about 5 minutes
Add garlic and saute 30 seconds longer
Meanwhile in a large mixing bowl stir together bread crumbs and chicken broth
Add cooked onion mixture to bread crumb mixture and stir
Transfer to fridge to let cool completely about 15-20 minutes
To the cooled mixture add everything other than the turkey, and stir mixture to blend well
Add ground turkey and using your hands, toss and mix until well combined
Shape mixture into a 9x4-inch loaf on prepared baking sheet
Brush with olive oil
Bake in preheated oven for 30 minutes
Remove meatloaf and brush with remaining glaze
Return to oven and bake until center registers 165°F
Remove from oven and let rest for 5 minutes
Scrape any fat away from the sides of the loaves, then slice and serve

ingredients.raw: About 2 pounds skinless chicken breasts or boneless skinless chicken thighs
2 tablespoons unsalted butter or olive oil
4 ounces (half a package) light cream cheese, cut into cubes and softened
If you need to, brown chicken in 2 batches using the same cooking times
Chicken Thighs: High Pressure 5 minutes + Natural Release 10 minutes
Chicken Breasts: High Pressure 4 minutes + Natural Release 10 minutes
It will seem like soup at first but as it rests and cools it will thicken right up

instructions.text: Ingredients
Pat dry chicken and season with salt and black pepper
Heat up Instant Pot using Sauté More function and wait until it says “HOT”
Add unsalted butter or olive oil to pot and heat until melted
Add the chicken and leaving the pot uncovered, brown each side of chicken for about 2 minutes
Set the chicken aside
Add minced shallots, garlic and mushrooms, season with salt and black pepper, then saute for about a minute
Add Italian seasoning, then saute for another minute
Mix soy sauce and chicken stock together, then pour in Instant Pot and deglaze by scrubbing all the flavorful brown bits off the bottom, then give it a quick mix
Add chicken and juices back into pot
Pressure cook chicken at:
After 10 minutes, release remaining pressure
Remove chicken and set aside
Push the Saute button and bring the liquid mixture back to a boil
Mix in cream cheese and grated Parmesan cheese
Add in spinach and sun-dried tomatoes, give them a quick stir, then cook for 30 seconds
Thicken Tuscan Chicken Sauce & Seasoning: Mix cornstarch and cold water together in a small mixing bowl
Add the cornstarch mixture one third at a time to thicken the Tuscan Chicken sauce until desired thickness
Taste and season with more salt, cheese, soy sauce if necessary
Place chicken back in Instant Pot and give it a quick mix and serve
Replace the 3/4 cup chicken stock with one 32-ounce carton of low sodium chicken stock
Add in 12 ounces of short pasta - bowties, rotini, etc
Cut the chicken into bite size pieces
Omit cornstarch mixture - the pasta starch will thicken the sauce
After the time is up select "cancel" then use the quick release method to release pressure and do not wait 10 minutes
Once it stops sputtering, open lid and immediately stir noodles to separate then stir in light cream cheese and parmesan until melted
Add in spinach and sun-dried tomatoes and stir
Toss well and let rest for about 5-10 minutes to thicken

ingredients.raw: About 3/4-1 pound skinless chicken breasts or boneless skinless chicken thighs
1 tablespoon unsalted butter or olive oil
2 ounces (half a package) light cream cheese, cut into cubes and softened
6 ounces of pasta of your choice (such as penne)

instructions.text: Cut chicken into bite sized pieces
Season with salt and pepper
Add unsalted butter or olive oil to pot and heat until melted
Heat up Instant Pot using Sauté More function and wait until it says HOT
Add chicken, minced shallots, garlic, and mushrooms, and all seasonings, then saute for about a minute or two mixing it all together
Add soy sauce and chicken stock in Instant Pot and deglaze by scrubbing all the flavorful brown bits off the bottom, then give it a quick mix
Add pasta to Instantpot and mix it all together, making sure all of the pasta is submerged in the chicken stock
Hit cancel then hit pressure cook and set to 5 minutes, then close the lid
Hit cancel again and push down the quick release
Open lid, stir noodles to separate them
Add cream cheese and parmesan cheese, and stir it until it melts
Add spinach and tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes
To thicken sauce if needed: Mix cornstarch and cold water together in a small bowl
Add cornstarch mixture one third at a time to sauce until desired thickness letting it reach a simmer and mixing each time before adding more
Taste and season with more salt, cheese, soy sauce if necessary, and serve

ingredients.raw: 3 strips of bacon, sliced into small strips
3 sprigs scallion cut on the bias

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Season chicken cutlets with salt and pepper on both sides
Put flour on cookie sheet and season with salt and pepper, mix together and spread
Dredge the chicken through the flour
In a large skillet over medium hot heat, add canola oil
Pan fry chicken cutlets until golden brown on each side
Place in an oven-proof baking dish
Cook bacon in the same pan and leave on until crispy while making the sauce
In a bowl, mix ranch dressing, mustard and soy sauce
Pour dressing over chicken and top with shredded cheese
Bake in oven for 20 minutes or until golden brown with internal temperature at 165°F
Garnish with bacon and scallion and serve

ingredients.raw: 4 (1 1/2- to 2-inch thick) beef tenderloin steaks, 6 to 8 ounces each (can use sirloin tips instead)
2 cups homemade or store-bought low-sodium beef stock (or use chicken stock and 2 teaspoons better than bouillon)
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
12 ounces button or cremini mushrooms, quartered
8 ounces peeled pearl onions (frozen is fine)
1 cup sour cream, plus more for serving

instructions.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
Place sour cream in a medium bowl and set aside to come to room temperature.
Combine 1 tablespoon salt, 1 teaspoon pepper, and half of paprika in a small bowl
Season steaks generously on all sides with mixture (you may not need all the mixture) and press to stick
Heat half of vegetable oil in a large skillet or sauté pan over high heat until lightly smoking
Add meat and cook, turning occasionally, until well browned on both sides and center of steaks register 115°F at the thickest part for rare or 125°F for medium rare
Remove from pan, transfer to a plate, and set aside
Add remaining vegetable oil to the same pan and add the mushrooms
Return to medium heat and cook, stirring and tossing frequently, until mushrooms have released their liquid and are just starting to brown, about 8 minutes
Add 1 tablespoon butter and pearl onions and continue to cook, stirring, until onions and mushrooms are softened and well browned, about 6 minutes longer
Add shallots, thyme, and remaining paprika, and cook, stirring, until fragrant
Add soy sauce, Worcestershire sauce and mustard and cook, stirring, until mostly evaporated, about 30 seconds
Add flour and stir for about 1 minute
Add any collected juices from the plate with the meat, followed by white wine
Cook until reduced to just a few tablespoons
Add broth and bring to a heavy simmer
Carefully remove about ½ cup of the broth into a liquid measuring cup
Slowly pour hot liquid over sour cream whisking constantly until fully homogenous
Add sour cream mixture to pan along with remaining 2 tablespoons butter
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and season to taste with salt and pepper
Remove thyme sprigs
Cook noodles according to package directions
When cooked, drain and return to pot, reserving 1 cup of pasta cooking water
Just before the pasta is done cooking, return steaks to pan until just warmed through, about 1 minute, then remove from heat
Transfer steaks to a cutting board and pour the sauce, mushrooms, and onions over the noodles
Stir to coat pasta, adding pasta cooking water as necessary until it reaches a loose, creamy consistency
Stir in half of minced parsley
Thinly slice steaks
To serve, divide pasta and cream sauce evenly between 4 hot serving bowls
Top with sliced steak, spooning a little extra sauce over them and sprinly with remaining parsley

ingredients.raw: 2 pounds of center cut beef tenderloin, trimmed
Demi Glace (optional - see recipe for homemade)
Start checking the temperature at 1 hour

instructions.text: Line a rimmed baking sheet with tin foil and place a wire rack on the sheet
Using butcher's twine, tie tenderloin at 1-1 ½ inch intervals
Transfer to a wire rack on baking sheet, season generously with salt and pepper, and refrigerate uncovered at least overnight and up to 48 hours
When ready to cook, adjust oven rack to center position and preheat oven to 250°F
Mix garlic and rosemary and smear all over roast and season with more salt and pepper
Place baking sheet with rack and tenderloin in oven and roast until internal temperature registers 120 to 125°F on an instant-read thermometer, about 1 hour 15 minutes
Remove from oven and set aside at room temperature for 10 minutes
Cut and remove twine
In a pan or cast iron skillet, heat butter over high heat until melted and foamy
Add tenderloin to skillet and sear for 30 seconds to 1 minute on each side (including the ends) until it forms a nice crust
Remove from pan, cut into 1/2-1 inch slices, and serve with desired sauce
Adjust rack to 6 inches from broiler element and preheat broiler to high
Heat butter, swirling, in a medium skillet over high heat until foaming subsides and butter turns a light nutty brown
Add thyme and shallots and stir until crackling stops
Pour butter mixture over tenderloin and spread with a spoon until all surfaces are coated
Remove shallots from top surface of meat and spread to sides
Place pan with tenderloin under broiler and broil, turning every 30 seconds, until meat is well browned on all sides
Remove from rack, cut into 1/2-1 inch slices, and serve with desired sauce

ingredients.note: 1 large yellow onion, chopped 4 stalks celery, chopped 3 large carrots, chopped

ingredients.raw: 2 cups of low-sodium beef stock (or 2 cups water/ chicken stock mixed with 2 teaspoons of “Better Than Bouillon” beef base)
The liquid should almost cover the short ribs completely, so add more stock or water if needed
Optional: for a silkier sauce, whisk in a few tablespoons of cold butter

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 275°F
Season short ribs generously with salt and black pepper on all sides
Heat a large dutch oven medium high heat
Once heated, drizzle 1 tablespoon of avocado oil on the bottom of the dutch oven
Sear the short ribs in batches, for about 1 ½ minutes on each side, adding more avocado oil as needed, then set aside
Pour off excess fat, leaving about 1-2 tablespoons
Add the chopped vegetables, smashed garlic, and 1/2 teaspoon salt, and begin scraping up any browned beef bits off the bottom of the pot
Cook until vegetables start to soften, about 5-10 minutes
Add the tomato paste and stir to evenly distribute, cooking until the tomato paste has started to caramelize and stick to the pot, about 3 minutes
Add red wine, bringing to a boil while scraping the bottom of the pot, to reduce the wine about 2/3 until the alcohol smell is gone
Stir in beef stock and return short ribs to the pot
Add thyme sprigs, a few sprigs of parsley and a bay leaf to pot
Bring everything to a boil, cover tightly with a lid, and transfer to your preheated oven for 4 hours
When the short ribs are done cooking, remove them from the pot and set aside
Strain the braising liquid and discard the solids
Return the strained liquid to the pot and reduce by half, then taste and adjust seasoning as needed
To serve, ladle sauce over short ribs and top with fresh chopped parsley and lemon zest, if desired

ingredients.raw: 3 pounds steak roast (tri tip, flank, etc)

instructions.text: Combine steak and marinade ingredients in a 1 gallon size ziploc bag
Marinate at least 3 hours, preferably overnight
When ready to cook heat grill on high
Remove steak from marinade and pat dry
Reserve marinade and set aside
Lower heat on one side of grill to low or turn off
Place steak on high heat grill and sear one side about 3 minutes
Turn steak over and sear for another 3 minutes
Flip and move steak to cooler side of grill and cook for about 10-20 minutes checking temp to see when it's ready
When steak is done, let rest on cutting board covered with foil for about 10 minutes
While the steak is resting, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a saucepan on medium
Add onion to saucepan and saute for about 2-3 minutes
Add reserved marinade to saucepan increase heat to medium high and bring to a boil
Lower temp to low and add tomatoes and basil and let simmer 1 minute then set aside
Slice steak against the grain and top with bruschetta mix

recipes.title: Brisket (Sous Vide-Que)
notes: 24-72 hours*

ingredients.raw: Use about 1/2 teaspoon of kosher salt per pound of meat
You may have to cut it in half and put in 2 bags
Liquid from bags can be added to your favorite barbecue sauce and simmered down to provide extra flavor
ingredients.note: If your oven has a convection setting, turn it on and adjust heat to 225°F if you can Remember that the interior of the brisket will already be perfectly cooked from the sous vide step, the goal is to simply reheat and smoke the brisket without overcooking it

instructions.text: To prep, trim off most of the fat cap but leave about 1/4-inch
If using a whole packer separate the point from the flat
Season the brisket with kosher salt
If you can, refrigerate the brisket uncovered for 12-24 hours to give the salt time to be absorbed
Combine the Brisket Rub ingredients into a bowl
When ready to start cooking, rub 2/3 of the brisket seasoning generously all over the brisket
Reserve the rest of the seasoning for later
Place brisket in a vacuum bag
Set sous vide machine to 135°F for brisket with a tender, steak-like texture, or 155°F for more traditionally textured brisket that falls apart when you pull at it
Add brisket to water bath and cover it with a lid or aluminum foil to prevent evaporation
Cook for 24-36 hours at 155°F or 36-72 hours at 135°F
Once the brisket has finished cooking, remove the bag from the water and submerge in a large container filled with a 50/50 mix of ice and water until chilled, at least 30 minutes
The brisket can be refrigerated until you are ready to grill (2-3 days ahead of time)
Remove brisket from bags and carefully blot dry with paper towels
Rub reserved brisket seasoning onto surface of brisket
To finish in the oven:
To Finish on the grill:
Remove the brisket from the oven, smoker or grill, slice it against the grain, and serve immediately
Take the point and cut some or all of it into 1” cubes
Place the cubes in an aluminum pan
Season and toss the cubes with more rub
Cover the cubes with the BBQ sauce mixed with the brisket juices
Mix the cubes thoroughly to ensure they are completely covered
Return the pan to the smoker and cook for another 1-2 hours or until all liquid has reduced and the sauce has caramelized

ingredients.raw: freshly ground black pepper, to taste
1 tablespoon avocado oil, or other high-heat, neutral oil of choice

ingredients.note: Rare 125°F Medium Rare 130°F Medium 135°F

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 415˚F
Pat steaks dry with paper towels, then rub with avocado oil, and season liberally with salt, pepper and garlic powder
Heat a medium, oven-safe stainless steel or cast iron skillet over high heat for 5-10 minutes
Add the filet mignon to the pan
Cook without moving for 2-3 minutes, until a crust has formed
Use tongs to flip the steak over, cook for another 2 minutes
Top each with 1 tablespoon of butter
Add rosemary, and garlic to the pan if using
Transfer the pan to the oven for 5-6 minutes for a medium rare steak
Remove filets from the skillet and set on a plate, lightly cover with tin foil and let sit for 5 minutes before serving
Fill sous vide bucket with warm water and heat water to 130˚F for medium rare, 140˚F for medium, 150˚F for medium well
Remove lamb rack from package and pat dry
Generously season with kosher salt, black pepper, and garlic powder on all sides
Add lamb rack to sous vide bag, then lay thyme sprigs, rosemary sprigs, and sliced shallots across the top of the lamb rack
Vacuum seal the lamb rack
Once the water comes to temperature, add the lamb rack in the bag to the water and cook for at least 2 hours, but no more than 4 hours
When lamb is almost done in the sous vide, prepare the butter spread by combining all the ingredients together to form a paste
After 2-4 hours, remove lamb from sous vide, open the bag, discard the herbs and shallots, and pat the rack dry
Depending on size of cast iron skillet, cut rack in half if needed to fit and cook on skillet
Place a cast iron skillet on the stove and raise the temperature to medium-high
Spread butter mixture all over the meaty part of the rack of lamb - front, back, and sides
Once cast iron is hot, lay the rack of lamb, meatiest portion down first, onto the skillet until a nice crust forms, about 1 minute
After crust forms, turn rack onto other 3 sides for 30 seconds each, then remove from pan
Repeat above steps for however many racks you have
When ready to serve, stand lamb chops on a cutting board, with the meaties parts down and the bones up, and slice into lollipop pieces by guiding your knife down each bone
Serve as is, with garlic aioli, or other dipping sauce of choice and enjoy

ingredients.raw: 2 racks of lamb (1 ½ pounds each), trimmed and frenched
4 ounces creamy goat cheese, such as Montrachet

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 450°F and line a sheet pan with aluminum foil
In a medium bowl, crumble the goat cheese with a fork
Add the panko, garlic, rosemary, thyme, 1 teaspoon Kosher salt, and 1 teaspoon pepper
Drizzle with 3 tablespoons olive oil and stir to moisten the crumbs, then set aside for a few minutes or cover and refrigerate
Remove the racks of lamb from the refrigerator 30 minutes before you plan to cook them
Place the lamb, fat side up, on the prepared pan
Sprinkle with 2 teaspoons Kosher salt and 1 teaspoon pepper
Roast for 12 minutes exactly
Remove from the oven and, working quickly, use a knife to spread the mustard on the top of the lamb
Spread the crumb mixture evenly on the mustard, pressing gently to help the mixture adhere

Return the lamb to the oven right away and continue roasting for another 12-18 minutes (depending on the size of the lamb), until the crumbs are golden brown and the meat is 120°F for rare or 125°F for medium rare
Cover loosely with aluminum foil and allow the lamb to rest for 8 minutes
Cut the racks into single or double chops and serve hot

ingredients.raw: 3 tablespoons apple cider or red wine vinegar
1-3 pounds skirt steak (can also use tips, flank or hanger)

instructions.text: Whisk all of the ingredients together and pour into a large ziploc bag.
Add skirt steak to ziploc bag with marinade and allow to marinate minimum 2 hours or up to overnight.
Remove from marinade and grill over high heat 2-3 minutes per side until charred
Allow to rest 5-10 minutes before slicing on a cutting board against the grain.

recipes.title: Meatballs - Makes
notes: Can be cooked two different ways: baked in the oven or fried in the pan first then finished either in the oven or in the sauce

ingredients.raw: Half large onion, chopped small (can use food processor)
1/2 pound ground pork (if not using pork, add 1/2 pound ground beef)
1/2 pound of ground veal (if not using veal, add another 1/2 pound ground beef)
1 cup italian seasoned breadcrumbs (or plain bread crumbs and 1 tablespoon italian seasoning)
The onions should be very soft and aromatic but have no color
Works well to squish the mixture with your hands

ingredientGroups.name: Baked on a Baking Sheet in Oven Method

ingredients.raw: This will allow the drippings from the meatballs to add more flavor to the sauce
This should take about 10 minutes for each batch- don't crowd the meatballs
ingredients.note: Such as when having with spaghetti Such as when having meatball subs or freezing meatballs for a later time

ingredients.raw: If the meatballs and sauce are ready a while before you’re ready to eat, add the meatballs to the sauce, turn off the stove, and 15 minutes before ready to eat, turn the stove back on to reheat the sauce and meatballs

instructions.text: Heat a tablespoon of olive oil in a saute pan, add onions and bring to medium-high heat
Season the onions with 1 teaspoon salt and cook for about 5-7 minutes
Add garlic and crushed red pepper and saute for another 1-2 minutes
Turn off heat and allow to cool
In a large bowl combine the meat, eggs, Parmigiano, bread crumbs, salt, pepper, parsley, and onion mixture
Add the milk or water and do 1 final really good squish

instructionGroups.name: Baked on a Baking Sheet in Oven Method

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F convection and line a baking sheet with parchment paper
Using your hands, lightly form the mixture into 2-inch meatballs
Place meatballs on baking sheet and bake them in the preheated oven for about 20 minutes or until the meatballs are cooked all the way through
If making a sauce, add the meatballs to the sauce, cover, and simmer on the lowest heat for at least 10 minutes so flavors can blend
Preheat oven to 400°F convection
Using your hands, lightly form the mixture into 2-inch meatballs
Lightly coat an oven-safe, wire rack large enough to sit on top of a 9x13-inch baking dish without falling in with cooking spray or olive oil
Place the meatballs on the rack so that they are not touching
Place the rack of meatballs on top of a baking dish filled with your marinara sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade) and carefully transfer to the oven
Bake until the meatballs are browned and cooked through or and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of a meatball registers 165°F, 25-30 minutes
Remove from oven, mix up the sauce, and add meatballs to sauce
Place back in oven for another 5-10 minutes, turn off oven, and leave in until ready to eat
Coat a large saute pan with olive oil and bring to a medium-high heat
Very carefully, in batches, place the meatballs in the oil and brown them well on all sides over medium-low heat, turning carefully with a spatula or a fork
After browning in pan, there are two ways to finish cooking meatballs:
After you make the sauce, add the meatballs to the sauce, cover, and simmer on the lowest heat for at least 10 minutes, until the meatballs are cooked through

ingredients.raw: Marinara sauce (1 jar store bought or 1 recipe for homemade)
6 slices of mozzarella cheese cut in half
1/2 cup pecorino or parmesan cheese (or a combination)

instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine all the filling ingredients
Scoop out 12 equal portions of the filling, roll into a ball and place on a parchment paper lined plate, then freeze for 30 minutes - 1 hour
Preheat oven to 400°F and spray a 9x13-inch baking dish with cooking spray
Prepare the meatballs as directed in the above recipe (steps 1-6)
Separate the meatball mix into 12 equal portions
Take one meatball portion and place 1 frozen ricotta ball in the middle of the meatball, completely covering the ricotta with the meatball mix so there aren’t any openings
Place each stuffed meatball on baking dish and bake for 30 minutes, turning over halfway
Pour sauce into baking dish covering meatballs and the bottom of the dish
Top each meatball with a slice of mozzarella cheese
Put back in oven and bake for another 15-20 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly

ingredients.raw: 1/3 cup canned chicken stock or broth
1/2 pound ground pork (can use all beef)
1/2 pound ground veal (can use all beef)
Don't mash or the meat loaf will be dense

instructions.text: Ingredients
Preheat the oven to 350°F
Heat the olive oil in a medium saute pan
Add the onions, thyme, salt, and pepper and cook over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, for 8-10 minutes, until the onions are translucent but not brown
Off the heat, add the Worcestershire sauce, chicken stock, and tomato paste, and allow to cool slightly
In a large bowl, combine the ground meat, onion mixture, bread crumbs, and eggs, and mix lightly with a fork
Shape the mixture into a rectangular loaf on a sheet pan covered with parchment paper
Bake for 30 minutes
While the meatloaf is cooking add the brown sugar, ketchup and Worcestershire Sauce in a small bowl and mix
Spread the glaze evenly on top
Bake an additional 30 minutes or until the internal temperature is 160°F and the meatloaf is cooked through
Rest the meat for 10 minutes before slicing

ingredients.raw: 3-4 pound store bought uncooked corned beef brisket
1-2 tablespoons fresh coarsely ground black pepper
If you can, change the water once or twice
Note that the pastrami is already perfectly cooked from the sous vide step, the goal is to add smoke to the meat while reheating it to a temperature that is pleasant when served

instructions.text: Take the corned beef out of the packaging and save the seasoning packet
Put the corned beef in a pot slightly larger than the meat and cover it with cold water
Refrigerate for 8-24 hours in order to remove excess salt from the meat
Prepare a sous vide immersion circulator and set the water temperature for 140°F
Remove the desalinated corned beef from the pot and season with 2-3 tablespoons of Pastrami Rub and saved corned beef seasoning and place in a vacuum bag
Add to water bath and cover it with a lid or aluminum foil to prevent evaporation
Once bag is submerged, cook the corned beef for 40-48 hours
Remove beef from container and bag and let cool until easy to handle.
Pat the surface dry with paper towels and season generously all over with the dry rub mixture
Wrap in tin foil and place in refrigerator 12 hours or up to 2 days until ready to smoke
Fire up your smoker to 225°F or prepare a grill for 2-zone cooking and get the indirect side to about 225°F

If you are using a gas grill, simply turn off the burners on one half of the grill for indirect cooking, adjust the grill temperature to approximately 225°F, then add a smoker box or smoking wood pouch according to manufacturer's instructions for the full smoke flavor
Place the meat in your smoker or on the cooler side of the grill as far from the heat source as possible
Allow the meat to smoke until it reaches an internal temperature of 125°F, about 1 hour
Remove the pastrami from the grill, wrap it in a double layer of foil, and allow it to sit for 1 hour before unwrapping and slicing against the natural grain of the meat

ingredients.raw: 1 standing rib roast (prime rib) or boneless prime rib roast 5 ½ pounds
ingredients.note: Can be larger or smaller than this, but the following measurements are based off this size

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon fresh minced Rosemary or thyme
Leave in Probe thermometer (if you have)
This will take approximately 4 ½ - 5 hours, but should first check after 3 ½ hours in case it cooks quicker

instructions.text: At least 2 days before you are ready to cook, season roast generously on all sides with salt
Place roast, with fat cap up, on a V-rack set in a large roasting pan, or on a wire rack set in a rimmed baking sheet and place in the refrigerator uncovered
One hour before you are ready to cook season roast generously with pepper
Mix olive oil, garlic, and rosemary and smear mixture all over the roast, adding any extra to the front and 2 sides
Preheat the oven to 180°F and insert probe thermometer (if you have one) into thickest part of the roast
Place roast in oven and cook until center of roast registers 120-125°F for rare, 130°F for medium-rare
Remove roast from oven and tent loosely with aluminum foil
Place in a warm spot in the kitchen and allow to rest for at least 30 minutes and up to 1 ½ hours
Meanwhile, preheat the oven to the highest possible temperature setting, 500-550°F convection if possible
10 minutes before guests are ready to be served, remove foil, place roast back in the hot oven, and cook until well browned and crisp on the exterior, about 6-10 minutes
Remove from the oven, carve, and serve immediately

recipes.title: Reverse Sear Steak (no grill)
notes: Cooking time can vary dramatically depending on many factors, so check often*

ingredients.raw: Thick-cut beef steaks, at least 1 ½ to 2 inches thick, such as ribeye, strip, porterhouse, T-bone, etc.
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
High heat oil - such as Avocado
Optional: 1 tablespoon unsalted butter (if finishing on the stovetop)

instructions.text: Generously season steaks all over with salt and pepper
Set steak on a wire rack set in a rimmed baking sheet and refrigerate, uncovered, for at least 1 hour up to overnight
Preheat oven to 225°F or 250°F
Place steaks in the oven and cook until an instant-read thermometer registers to desired temperature below:
Just before steaks come out of the oven, add 1 tablespoon of oil to a cast iron or heavy stainless steel skillet and heat over high heat until smoking
Add steaks and butter to the skillet and cook until each side is well browned, about 45 seconds per side
Using tongs, hold steak(s) sideways to sear edges
Serve right away as there's no need to let reverse-seared steaks rest

ingredients.raw: 1 rack of St Louis style or baby back ribs
Rub/ marinade (store bought or see one of the below recipes)
Work your fingers around under it to loosen it so you can get a grip on it
I like to use a paper towel to help me grip it
ingredients.note: Can do this step the day/ night before the rest of the cooking Cook over indirect heat - Turn on one side of the grill and get grill to about 250°F, then place the ribs on the other side of the grill and cook for about 2 hours After 2 hours, wrap ribs in tin foil and put back on grill for another 2 hours When ribs are tender, remove from foil and brush with reserved marinade Place ribs on hot side of grill and cook for a few minutes until glaze starts to look sticky and a little charred Preheat oven to 250°F Place ribs on a rack on a baking sheet and cover with foil Bake for about 3-4 hours until tender Remove foil and glaze the ribs with the reserved sauce Place ribs under broiler and broiled for about 5 minutes until glaze is sticky and a little charred

instructions.text: Place and mix together all ingredients for the rub/ marinade in a measuring cup or bowl and set aside
To prepare the ribs, remove the membrane on the back of the ribs
Turn the slab to the bone side up and insert a butter knife between the membrane and the meat at one end
Gently begin peeling it off, trying not to rip it, pull off the membrane and throw it away
If using a marinade/ sauce:
If using a salt-free rub:
To cook the ribs:

recipes.title: Memphis Dust Dry Rub
notes: Put the rest in a jar/ bag and it will stay good for a long time

ingredients.raw: 3/4 cup firmly packed dark brown sugar
1 teaspoon liquid smoke, separated from rest of the above ingredients (optional)

recipes.title: Chinese Spare Ribs Marinade
notes: Makes enough for 1 rack of ribs

instructions.text: Heat the oil in a heavy large skillet over medium heat
Add the sausages and cook until brown on all sides, about 7-10 minutes
Remove from the pan and drain
Keeping the pan over medium heat, add the peppers, onions, salt, and pepper and cook until golden brown, about 5 minutes
Add the oregano, basil, and garlic and cook for 2 more minutes
Add the tomato paste and stir
Add the Marsala wine, tomatoes, and chili flakes, if using, then stir to combine, scraping the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon to release all the browned bits
Bring to a simmer
Cut the sausages into bite-sized pieces, about 1-inch cubes
Add the sausage back to the pan and stir to combine
Cook until the sauce has thickened, about 20 minutes
a) Serve in bowls

ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup Marsala wine (or white wine if don’t have)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F
Cut potatoes in half and add to a measuring cup with 1 tablespoon olive oil, mixing until well coated
Heat potatoes in microwave for 1 minute
Add sliced pepper, onion, and potatoes to a large bowl
Season veggies with 1 tablespoon olive oil, salt, pepper, oregano, basil, red pepper flakes, and garlic and mix until well coated
Spray a medium-sized glass baking dish with cooking spray
Pour veggies into baking dish
Combine marsala wine and chicken broth and pour over veggies
Lay sausage links over the veggies
Cook in oven for 20 minutes, then flip sausage over, and cook for another 20 minutes until browned and fully cooked
Serve in a bowl on its own or in a sandwich

recipes.title: Steak/ Steak Tips
notes: Medium Rare: 130-135°F
Medium: 140-145°F
Medium well: 150-155°F
Well done: 160-175°F

ingredients.raw: Marinate for a minimum of one hour but doing so overnight is the best
Could also paint sauce on as you’re cooking if you don’t have time to marinate
Thinner pieces won’t need to cook as long
Thinner pieces won’t need to cook as long
Thinner pieces won’t need to cook as long

instructions.text: Add meat to a ziplock bag and pour Teriyaki or BBQ sauce over to marinate (enough that everything is coated)
Close the ziplock bag, pressing all of the air out of it, massage sauce into meat, and flip it over
Grill on medium high for crisp on outside but kind of pink on inside
Grill for about 5 minutes on each side (this is for thicker pieces - at least 1 inch thick)
Check temperature after 3 minutes on the other side and continue cooking until meat is at desired temperature
Take off grill and let it rest on plate for a few minutes so the juices stay in the meat
Coat a pan with oil and heat on medium-high until shimmering
Add steak and cook for about 5 minutes on each side (this is for thicker pieces - at least 1 inch thick)
Check temperature after 3 minutes on the other side and continue cooking until meat is at desired temperature
Line bottom of broiler pan with tin foil, and place broiler rack on top
Place steak on rack in oven using broiler setting and cook for about 5 minutes on each side (this is for thicker pieces - at least 1 inch thick)
Continue cooking until meat is at desired temperature

ingredients.raw: 1/2 an 18-ounce jar of Smuckers Apricot Preserves
1 packet of Lipton Onion Soup mix

ingredients.note: 1 tablespoon cornstarch 1 tablespoon cold water

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Place the chicken pieces in a 4 quart casserole dish
Mix the soup mix, dressing, and jam together
Pour mix over the chicken
Bake for 1 hour in oven
When chicken is almost done, make the pasta to go with the chicken
To thicken the sauce, if needed, whisk cornstarch with cold water and slowly pour into sauce, mixing until it is as thick as desired

ingredients.raw: 1/2 pound chicken breasts sliced in half to 1/2" thickness
1 ½ cups milk or half and half
2 tablespoons sun dried tomatoes finely chopped

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 425ºF
Cook the tortellini to al dente according to package instructions, then drain and set aside
Prep chicken by slicing pieces in half the long way (to make thin)
Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper on both sides to taste
Add chicken to skillet and cook for 3-5 minutes, until brown (depending on thickness)
Turn over and cook for about 3-5 minutes
Take chicken off pan and set aside on a plate, allow them to rest before slicing them into thin, bite-sized pieces
Melt butter in the same pan used for chicken over medium heat
Add garlic, nutmeg and red pepper flakes and cook for 30 seconds, stirring frequently
Add in flour and whisk until brown
Slowly pour in some milk stirring with a whisk until it thickens
Once mixture is smooth, add the rest of the milk, raise the temperature and bring to a boil
Lower the temperature to medium, and stir for about 3 minutes or until mixture starts to thicken
Add cream cheese, parmesan cheese and sun dried tomatoes and whisk until cheese melts and mixture is smooth
Combine the sauce, tortellini, and sliced chicken and place in an oven-safe baking dish
Place dollops of ricotta cheese on the top of the tortellini bake, then sprinkle the mozzarella cheese on top
Place the pan in the middle rack of the oven and bake for 18-20 minutes, until the mozzarella cheese is melted and browned in spots

ingredients.raw: Shredded white Italian cheese (such as mozzarella cheese)

instructions.text: Cook ziti according to box instructions
After cooked, combine ziti with ricotta cheese, marinara sauce, and shredded cheese (leaving out about 1/2 cup of shredded cheese)
Pour into a casserole dish
Sprinkle with remaining 1/2 cup of shredded cheese
Bake at 350°F for about a half hour

ingredients.raw: 1 pound ground meat (beef, turkey or chicken)
1 large carrot (or 4 baby carrots) finely chopped
About 1 tablespoon Extra-virgin olive oil for the pan
1 cup dry red wine, plus another 1/4 cup, divided
1 can (28 ounces) crushed tomatoes, preferably Pastene
1 ½ teaspoons freshly ground black pepper
1/4 cup chopped fresh basil leaves, lightly packed or 1 tablespoon of dried basil
I let simmer for about 30 minutes and then removed from heat until about a half hour before dinner when I put it back on low to warm

instructions.text: Heat the oil in a heavy large frying pan over medium heat
Add the onion and saute until translucent and soft, about 5 minutes
Add the carrot, celery and garlic and saute until vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes
Raise heat to medium high and add the ground meat and cook, crumbling the meat with a wooden spoon
Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the meat has lost its pink color and has started to color from white to brown
Stir in the oregano, hot red pepper flakes, and Italian seasoning and mix for one minute
Pour 1 cup of wine into the skillet and stir to scrape up any browned bits
Cook until the wine has reduced to half, about 4-5 minutes
After wine is reduced, add the tomato paste and mix until well combined
Add the crushed tomatoes, salt and pepper, and stir and bring to a boil
Lower the heat to low, and simmer for at least 20 minutes to a few hours
10 minutes before serving add nutmeg and basil to sauce and simmer for 8-10 minutes
Also 10 minutes before serving, add the remaining 1/4 cup of wine, if necessary to make enough sauce/ to thin the sauce a bit

ingredients.note: Could also make with spaghetti squash

ingredients.raw: 2 cloves garlic, whole (use more if small cloves)
4 fresh basil leaves, diced small or 1/4 teaspoon dried
Parmesan cheese for serving, if desired
Optional: 1 ¼ pounds chicken breast, diced into bite-sized pieces
ingredients.note: 1/4 teaspoon Garlic Powder 1/4 teaspoon Onion Powder 1/4 teaspoon Kosher salt 1/8 teaspoon Black Pepper

ingredients.raw: Before straining the pasta, set aside some pasta water in a small measuring cup in case needed for the sauce

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Spray glass baking dish with cooking spray
Add Boursin cheese to the middle of the baking dish
Add cherry tomatoes and garlic around boursin and drizzle with 2-3 tablespoons olive oil
Season with basil, oregano, salt, and pepper
Bake in the oven for 20-30 minutes until tomatoes burst and cheese is softened

After adding the baking dish to the oven, fill and cover a pot of water and heat on stove at high heat until it comes to a boil, then add 1/4 teaspoon Kosher salt, then lower heat to low until time to cook the pasta
About 10 minutes before tomatoes/ cheese are done, season chicken with garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper
Heat a pan with 1 tablespoon of olive oil to cook your chicken
Add chicken to pan and cook for about 2-3 minutes on one side, then mix it around for another 3 minutes until fully cooked on all sides
Once the chicken is fully cooked, set aside in a bowl and cover with foil to keep warm
About 10 minutes before you’re ready to eat, raise heat on pot of water to bring back to a boil and add pasta and cook according to package directions
When tomatoes are ready/ bursting, remove baking dish from oven and mix tomatoes and cheese together until fully combined
Add ingredients to a measuring cup and blend with immersion blender until smooth
Add sauce to a pot and heat on low heat until pasta is ready
Add the pasta you cooked earlier and some of the pasta water to loosen the sauce if needed

recipes.title: Butternut Squash and Maple Chicken Risotto (Pressure Cooker)
notes: Can easily be cut in half*

ingredients.raw: 3 ½ pounds butternut squash, peeled and seeded
1 chicken breast, sliced thin lengthwise and seasoned with salt & pepper
4 medium cloves garlic, 3 crushed and 1 minced, divided
2 sprigs sage plus 8 sage leaves, divided
1/2 apple (I used Granny smith), peeled, cored, and cut into chunks
4 tablespoons maple syrup, plus more for serving
2 cups (400g) risotto rice, such as arborio
1/2 cup grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese, plus extra for serving
If you have room on one of the trays of squash you can just cook the chicken on the same tray at the same time
It should begin to develop a creamy consistency
If risotto is too soupy, cook for a few minutes longer, stirring, until it begins to thicken more; it should look like a smooth, creamy sauce

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F convection
Cut about half the squash into 1-inch chunks, to yield 4 cups
Using the flat side of a knife, crush three garlic cloves to flatten garlic

In a large mixing bowl, toss large squash chunks with 2 tablespoons olive oil, crushed garlic, apple, red pepper flakes, sage sprigs, and a large pinch of salt until evenly coated, then spread in an even layer on a baking sheet
Cut remaining squash into 1/4-inch dice
In a separate bowl, toss small diced squash with 2 tablespoons olive oil, 2 tablespoons of maple syrup, and a pinch of salt, then spread in an even layer on a second baking sheet
Bake both trays of squash, stirring once or twice during baking, until large chunks are very tender, for 45 minutes, and small diced squash is browned in spots, for 30 minutes
Discard sage sprigs from large squash chunks
Spray a baking sheet with non-stick spray or oil and place chicken on sheet
Brush chicken with remaining maple syrup and cook for 15-20 minutes turning once and brushing with more syrup
Remove chicken from oven, cover and set aside
Transfer large squash chunks and apple to a food processor, blender, or the container of a stick blender
Add miso and 1/4 cup broth and blend until a smooth purée forms, then season with salt
Heat remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil in a pressure cooker over medium-high heat until shimmering
Add shallots, chopped sage, and remaining 1 minced garlic clove and cook, stirring, until translucent but not browned, about 4 minutes
Add rice and cook, stirring, until rice is evenly coated in oil and toasted but not browned, 3 to 4 minutes
Add wine and cook, stirring, until raw alcohol smell has cooked off and wine has almost fully evaporated, about 2 minutes
Stir in remaining 3 ¾ cups broth and scrape any grains of rice or pieces of onion from side of pressure cooker so that they are fully submerged
Close and lock the lid of the pressure cooker
While rice is cooking, in a small saucepan, melt butter over medium-high heat until foaming subsides, 2 to 3 minutes
Add remaining 8 sage leaves and fry, gently swirling, until milk solids in butter turn a hazelnut-brown color, 1 to 2 minutes longer, then remove from heat
Using a slotted spoon, transfer sage leaves to a paper towel–lined plate
Dice chicken into bite sized pieces
When time is up, open the cooker by releasing the pressure through the valve
Open pressure cooker and stir to combine rice and cooking liquid
Stir in puréed squash mixture, then stir in brown butter
Stir in the Parmesan cheese and nutmeg
Mix in the maple-roasted diced squash and chicken and season with salt
Spoon risotto onto plates, top with fried sage leaves and more grated cheese, a drizzle of maple syrup and serve

ingredients.raw: 3 cups diced butternut squash about 1 pound of squash
¼ cup broth or apple cider
1 pound mezzi rigatoni or any small shaped pasta such as elbow macaroni or orecchiette
If the sauce is too thick, add in some of the hot starchy pasta water to thin it out
It will thicken as the pasta absorbs the sauce

instructions.text: Ingredients
Preheat oven to 400°F convection
Using the flat side of a knife, crush three garlic cloves to flatten garlic
In a large mixing bowl, toss squash with 2 tablespoons olive oil and crushed garlic, then spread in an even layer on a baking sheet
Bake stirring once or twice during baking, until squash very tender and browned in spots, for 45 minutes
Bring a large pot of generously-salted water to a boil.
Transfer squash chunks and garlic to a food processor, blender, or the container of a stick blender
Add milk and broth and blend until a smooth purée forms, then season with nutmeg salt, pepper and tabasco
Add pasta to the boiling water and cook according to package instructions until al dente
Reserve 1 cup of the starchy pasta water for later, then drain the pasta
While the pasta is cooking, transfer the butternut squash mixture back to a deep sauté pan or saucepan over medium-high heat and bring to a simmer
Add in the shredded cheese, and stir until it has melted into the sauce
Taste and season the sauce with salt and pepper as needed
Then once the pasta is drained, return it to the stockpot
Pour the butternut cheese sauce on top of the pasta, and toss to combine
Serve warm

ingredients.raw: 1 butternut squash (about 4 cups), peeled and diced into cubes
1 ounce parmesan cheese, grated, plus more to top

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 400°F and line baking sheet with parchment paper
In a bowl, add the diced squash, 1 tablespoon olive oil, 1/2 teaspoon Kosher salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes and mix until squash is coated
Pour butternut squash onto baking sheet and spread out into a single layer
In the same mixing bowl, add garlic cloves, 5 sprigs of thyme, 1 sprig of rosemary, 1/2 tablespoon olive oil, and mix to combine, then place on top of butternut squash
Cook butternut squash in oven for 20 minutes
After 20 minutes, remove thyme and rosemary, add the boursin cheese to the center of the sheet, and cook for another 20-30 minutes until squash has softened
While squash is cooking, bring large pot of water to a boil
Once squash is cooked, blend squash and garlic in a blender or add to measuring cup and blend with immersion blender
In a dutch oven, add pancetta and cook on medium heat for about 5 minutes
While pancetta cooks, add pasta to boiling water and cook according to package
Once pancetta is almost cooked, add diced shallot and cook until shallot is softened
Add 2 tablespoons butter to pancetta and shallots
Dice an additional handful of rosemary and sage, and add to pancetta and mix to combine
Add butternut squash mixture, gouda and parmesan cheese to pot and mix to combine
Add spoonfuls of pasta water to sauce until sauce reaches desired consistency
Once pasta is cooked, strain, and serve with sauce, and an additional sprinkling of parmesan cheese

ingredients.raw: 1 ounce freshly grated Parmesan, plus some for serving
1 ounce freshly grated pecorino romano, plus some for serving
1/4 cup chopped fresh Italian parsley leaves
Before draining, reserve about 3/4 cup pasta water to fortify the sauce

instructions.text: Ingredients
Cook the pasta until al dente, adding the peas one minute before the pasta is finished
While the pasta is cooking, in a medium saute pan over medium heat, cook the pancetta and garlic cloves until the pancetta is tender-crisp
Remove to a paper towel-lined plate and set aside - discard the garlic cloves, reserving the pan with the drippings
Season the chicken breast with the salt, pepper and Italian seasoning
In the same pan saute the chicken breast for 4 minutes on each side until well browned and cooked through, then set aside on a plate
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, cream if using, pancetta, the cheeses and black pepper
Cut the chicken into bite sized pieces
When the pasta is done, add the chicken, pasta and peas back into the saute pan
Add the egg mixture into the pasta and toss well with tongs for about 1 minute
Add the reserved pasta water to the bowl in 1/4 cup increments, combining and adding until desired sauce consistency is reached
Add the parsley and gently combine and place into a serving dish
Top with additional cheese

ingredients.raw: While doing that, prep everything else
Be careful not to burn it!

instructions.text: Boil water and cook cavatelli according to package instructions
Cut up broccoli into smaller pieces (florets)
Grate the parmesan cheese
In a big saute pan pour olive oil
Crush garlic into pan and stir constantly over medium heat for 30 seconds
When there are 30 seconds left in cooking the cavatelli, add the broccoli to the boiling water
Strain the cavatelli and broccoli and reserve some of the boiling water
Dump the cavatelli and broccoli into the pan and mix all together still at medium heat
Add cheese and mix until it’s a little melted
If need to make a little more saucy, add some of the reserved pasta water
Turn off and put cover on to keep warm until ready to be served

ingredients.raw: 1 pound boneless, skinless chicken breast, sliced thin lengthwise
8 ounces broccoli cut into florets (about 2 ounces per person)
8-10 ounces pasta (2-2 ½ ounces per servings, depending on how much people want)
1 ⅔ cups skim milk or fat free half and half
1 cup (about 3 ¾ ounces) grated fresh Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese
Additional about 1/4 cup fresh Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese for sprinkling on top
2 ounces or 4 tablespoons whipped cream cheese

instructions.text: Prep chicken by slicing pieces in half the long way (to make thin)
Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper on both sides, to taste, and set aside on plate
In a large pot, boil salted water
Once pasta water is boiling, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat
Add chicken to skillet and cook for 3-5 minutes, until brown (depending on thickness)
Turn over and cook for about 3-5 minutes, testing chicken with thermometer (155-160°F)
Take chicken off pan and set aside on a plate, and make sure pasta water is boiling
Add pasta to boiling water and cook according to package instructions
Once the pasta is cooking, start making the sauce
Melt butter in the same saucepan used for chicken over medium heat
Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds, stirring frequently (don’t let it burn!)
Add in flour and whisk until brown
Slowly pour in some milk stirring with a whisk until it thickens
Once mixture is smooth, add the rest of the milk, raise the temp and bring to a boil
Once boiling, lower temp to medium, and stir for about 4 minutes or until mixture starts to thicken
Add cream cheese and whisk until incorporated
Add 1 cup parmesan cheese and whisk until cheese melts and mixture is smooth
If the sauce is too thick, add some milk to thin it
Lower temp to low
Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces and add to sauce, along with juices from chicken
Toss sauce with hot pasta and broccoli
Add some pasta water if needed to thin the sauce

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ pounds boneless, skinless chicken cutlets, sliced thin and each pounded about 1/2 to ¼ inch thick
1 ½ cups sweet marsala wine (not cooking wine!)
1 packet unflavored gelatin, such as Knox (2 ½ teaspoons, about 10g)
About 1 cup all-purpose flour for dredging
2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil (can use more if needed)
10 ounces cremini mushrooms (baby bellas), stemmed and thinly sliced
1 teaspoon minced fresh thyme leaves (can use dried but only use ⅓ teaspoon)
1 tablespoon cold unsalted butter, cut into cubes
1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 2 tablespoons chicken broth
Working in batches, and adding more oil for each cutlet, if necessary

instructions.text: Combine marsala wine and stock in a large measuring cup and sprinkle gelatin all over the surface, then set aside
Season chicken cutlets all over with salt, pepper, and a little bit italian seasoning
Pour about 1 cup of flour into a wide, shallow bowl to cover the bottom
Lay both sides of each cutlet in flour (dredge), tap off excess, and transfer to clean plate
Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering
Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides, about 3-5 minutes per side
Transfer chicken to a new plate and keep warm
Add mushrooms to skillet (do not drain remaining oil) and cook for about 10 minutes, stirring frequently, until mushrooms have released their juices and browned well
Add shallots, garlic, and thyme and cook for about 2 minutes, stirring, until shallots are translucent (add more oil if pan seems too dry at any point)
Pour marsala wine and broth mixture into pan
Bring to a boil, whisking and scraping up any browned bits from bottom of pan
Once boiling, continue whisking for about 5 min until liquids are reduced by about half
Add butter and soy sauce and whisk constantly until creamed together
Make cornstarch mixture, add to pan + whisk until sauce has consistency of heavy cream
Season with salt and pepper
Put chicken cutlets in pan and cover with sauce

ingredients.raw: About 3 ½ - 4 pounds boneless skinless chicken breast
1/2 cup flour, plus 3/4 cup flour, divided

ingredients.note: 1 teaspoon dried oregano 1 teaspoon granulated garlic 1 teaspoon onion powder 1 tablespoon Italian seasoning

ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup freshly grated Parmesan, plus some more for garnish
1 quart marinara sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade - must make a double batch for this recipe)
Enough Mozzarella cheese to cover chicken pieces
ingredients.note: 1 package of fresh dry sliced mozzarella or 1 ½ packages shredded mozzarella

ingredients.raw: Can do for minimum of an hour if necessary
Oven temperature should be at least 300°F for bread crumbs
Do not stack - use another dish if running out of room and add more marinara sauce to bottom

instructions.text: Working one piece at a time, place inside a plastic zip-lock bag and pound with a meat pounder or bottom of a skillet to a 1/4-1/2 inch thickness, then cut each piece to serving size pieces, then transfer to a large ziplock bag
Add the buttermilk and minced garlic to bowl
Season milk and garlic with 1 tablespoon kosher salt and 1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Pour into a ziplock bag with chicken, press out the air, and seal
Turn chicken with hands until buttermilk mixture is evenly coated on the chicken
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or up to overnight
Before doing anything more with the chicken, make marinara sauce if using homemade
Preheat the oven to 425°F convection
Spread the panko bread crumbs on a baking sheet and put in the oven for about 5 minutes until brown (watch so it doesn’t burn!)
Line a baking sheet with foil, place a wire rack on top, and spray with cooking spray
For the breading, whisk together in a shallow plate the panko, 1/2 cup flour, 1/4 cup of the parmesan cheese, the cornstarch, spices, and 1 teaspoon each salt and black pepper
Place the remaining 3/4 cups flour in a separate shallow plate
Pour buttermilk into a medium bowl and crack the eggs into same bowl and whisk
Place another wire rack in the sink, remove the chicken from the brine, place on the rack, pat dry, then season with salt and pepper
Dip the chicken into the flour, then the buttermilk/ eggs mixture, then panko mixture lifting the mixture on top of it and pressing it down firmly until it sticks to the chicken
Transfer to the sprayed wired cooking rack and repeat with remaining chicken pieces
Spray chicken pieces with olive oil spray
Cook for 20-25 minutes, or until chicken is golden brown and almost cooked through
Begin boiling water for pasta and cook the pasta
Spray the bottom of baking dish and spoon 1 ½ cups of sauce onto the bottom and place chicken in sauce
Spoon additional sauce on top of each piece of chicken
Top with mozzarella and bake until the cheese is melty, about 5-10 minutes
If desired, broil until cheese is golden, 3 minutes

ingredients.raw: Salt and pepper to season the chicken

ingredients.note: 1/4 teaspoon dried oregano 1/4 teaspoon granulated garlic 1/4 teaspoon onion powder 3/4 teaspoon Italian seasoning

ingredients.raw: 1 or 2 slices of mozzarella or enough parmesan cheese to top the chicken
Could also use a small broiler pan with rack to cook 1 piece

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 425°F convection
Line a baking sheet with foil, place a wire rack on top, and spray with cooking spray
For the breading, whisk together in a shallow plate the bread crumbs, 1 tablespoon of the parmesan cheese, and spices
Crack the egg into a medium bowl and whisk
Season chicken with salt and pepper
Dip the chicken into the eggs bowl, then bread crumb mixture lifting the mixture on top of it and pressing it down firmly until it sticks to the chicken
Transfer to the sprayed wired cooking rack
Spray chicken pieces with olive oil spray
Cook for 15-20 minutes, or until chicken is golden brown and almost cooked through
Begin boiling water for pasta and cook the pasta
When chicken is done, remove rack from pan, spray foil with cooking spray and spoon sauce onto the bottom and place chicken in sauce
Spoon additional sauce on top of piece of chicken
Top with mozzarella or parmesan cheese and bake until the cheese is melty, about 5 minutes
If desired or needed, broil for 3 minutes, until cheese is golden

ingredients.raw: 2-3 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts, butterflied and pounded thin
3/4 cup Italian dressing (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
4 large tomatoes (preferably heirlooms), grated on a box grater yielding about 4 cups of sauce
Optional: handful of cherry tomatoes, sliced in half
2 cloves of garlic, finely grated on a microplane
2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil, plus more for serving
Parmesan cheese, freshly grated for serving

instructions.text: Combine the chicken and the Italian Vinaigrette in a large zip top bag and refrigerate for at least 1 hour to overnight
When ready to begin cooking, bring a pot of water to a boil and cook pasta according to package directions
Prepare tomato sauce by combining grated tomato with 2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil, 1/2 teaspoon of sea salt or kosher salt, and the finely grated garlic, then mix to combine
Add tomato sauce to a medium saucepan over high heat, bring to a boil and reduce to a medium simmer for about 5 minutes until the sauce has slightly thickened
While sauce is simmering, prepare the panko by heating a large skillet over medium heat and add the olive oil and butter
When the butter has melted, stir in the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds
Add the panko and Italian seasoning and toss to coat, toasting until the breadcrumbs are golden, stirring occasionally, about 5-7 minutes
Turn off the heat, add a pinch of salt, and transfer the breadcrumbs to a bowl, then set aside
After sauce has simmered, add basil, taste, and add additional salt/ pepper as needed, then cover and keep warm on low
Optional: In a medium sized pan, heat olive oil and add sliced tomatoes, cooking for 3-4 minutes until softened, then move to a bowl and set aside
Remove chicken from marinade and pat dry
Cook chicken either on the stove or grill for 3-5 minutes on each side until fully cooked
To assemble, ladle the tomato sauce over each butterflied cutlet and add some sliced tomatoes around cutlets

Top with freshly grated parmesan cheese, a few dollops of burrata/ mozzarella/ stracciatella cheese, flaky salt, freshly ground black pepper, toasted panko, fresh basil, and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil
Serve with spaghetti and ladle additional sauce over pasta

ingredients.raw: 1 pound chicken breast cut into cubes

instructions.text: Ingredients
Mix together flour, salt, pepper, paprika and garlic
Dredge chicken in flour mixture
In a Skillet, heat the olive oil and butter on medium heat
Cook chicken 4-5 minutes on each side until golden brown and cooked through
Take the chicken out of the skillet and set aside keeping warm
