You’ll pour the pank mixture from one bowl into the other each time to coat an onion ring
Place smaller rings inside bigger rings if necessary, just make sure there is space between them
Spray with some cooking spray after about 6 minutes
Don't need to turn the onion rings

instructions.text: Using 4 shallow bowls or deep plates:
Dip the onion rings one at a time into the egg/ buttermilk mixture, then the flour mixture and shake it around to coat
Then dip it back into the egg/ buttermilk mixture and then drop it into the smaller panko mixture and pour the rest of the panko on top and shake it around to coat well
Spray Air Fryer Basket with oil spray
Place onion rings in a single layer in the Air Fryer basket
Cook at 400°F until golden brown and crispy, about 11-15 minutes
Carefully lift out the onion rings with a cookie spatula or fork and serve

ingredients.raw: 1 small plum or Campari tomato diced
Kosher salt and fresh pepper to taste
1/4 cup fresh grated Parmesan or Pecorino

instructions.text: Cook pasta in large pot of salted water as directed for al dente
Reserve about 1/2 cup liquid before draining
Drain orzo in a colander and add the oil to the pot
Saute garlic and shallot for 1 minute, until fragrant
Add the zucchini, peppers and tomatoes, season with salt and pepper and mix well, cooking until tender, 3-4 minutes
Add the cooked orzo and stir to combine all
Add some of the reserved liquid as needed so pasta isn't dry
Add freshly grated cheese and stir
In a large saucepan or skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium high heat
Add shallots, crushed garlic, and salt and pepper to taste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant
Add the mushrooms, oregano and thyme, and cook for 4-5 minutes
Add couscous and the broth and bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer
Cook covered for about 12-15 minutes until broth is mostly absorbed

ingredients.raw: 1 red onion, thinly sliced (use a mandoline if you have one)

instructions.text: Slice the red onions as thin as you can (use a mandoline if you have one)
Stuff all the red onions in the jar of your choice; a bowl will work too
Heat vinegar, water, sugar, and salt in a small skillet over high heat, whisking until sugar is dissolved and bring to a boil
Pour this pickling mixture over sliced onions and make sure they are immersed in the liquid
Allow to sit until onions are softened and liquid has cooled for about 25 minutes
Peel ginger to completely remove the outer layer
Using a knife or mandolin, thinly slice the ginger
Place ginger in a saucepan with the water and salt, bring to a boil and continue cooking for an additional 3 minutes
Drain the ginger and transfer to a glass jar/ bowl/ container
Place vinegar and sugar in a saucepan and bring to a boil, whisking constantly until sugar dissolves
Once boiling and sugar has dissolved, pour over ginger
Allow pickled ginger to cool
Place a rimmed baking sheet in the oven and preheat to 425°F
Combine water, vinegar and 1 teaspoon salt in a large pot and bring to a boil over high heat
Cut potatoes into 1/2-inch-thick sticks
Add to the pot, reduce heat to medium and cook just until starting to become tender, 6 minutes
Carefully remove the potatoes to a clean kitchen towel with a slotted spoon
Gently pat dry, then transfer to a medium bowl and toss with oil
Carefully spread the potatoes in a single layer on the hot baking sheet
Bake, flipping halfway through, until golden and crispy, about 30 minutes
Transfer the fries to a paper-towel-lined bowl and sprinkle the fries with the remaining 1/4 teaspoon of salt

ingredients.raw: 2 ½ pounds potatoes, peeled and quartered (about 3 large Yukon gold potatoes)
1/2 cups milk or half and half, warm
1/2 stick cold butter, cut into 4 pieces
Test by sticking a fork in them and it should cut easily/ go right through

instructions.text: Place the peeled/ quartered potatoes and garlic in a large saucepan
Fill pot with cold water, just enough to cover potatoes, and season generously with salt
Bring water to a boil, cover, and cook until potatoes are fork-tender, about 20-25 minutes
Place butter at the bottom of the pan
Drain potatoes and garlic well and pass both through food-mill, ricer, or use an electric mixer back into pot
Microwave milk or cream for 30 seconds to 1 minute
While the potatoes are still hot add half of the cream and stir into the potatoes
Repeat this process until all of the cream and butter has incorporated
Add sour cream and stir
Taste for seasoning and add more salt, milk and/or sour cream, if needed, then serve

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons butter (or 2 tablespoons olive oil, for vegan)

instructions.text: Wash the potatoes
Cut them into thin slices, about 1/4” inch thick
Heat the butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat
Add the potatoes and cook them for 10-12 minutes until browned, flipping occasionally until all sides are browned
Sprinkle with seasoning and serve hot

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ pounds baby potatoes, halved and scored (tip - use the large dicing disc from the chopper and press down halfway)
1/2 teaspoon garlic powder (or sub with onion powder)

instructions.text: Ingredients
Preheat oven to 400°F
Line a 9 x13 baking pan or sheet with parchment paper
Drizzle olive oil on parchment paper
Mix Parmesan Mixture in a bowl
Use a spoon to scatter Parmesan Mixture over the base, spreading as evenly as you can
Place potatoes cut side down, pressing firmly
Spray the potatoes with some additional olive oil or some melted butter
Bake potatoes for 35 - 40 minutes or until they are soft and the parmesan crust is deep golden

ingredients.raw: 1 yellow onion, halved and thinly sliced crosswise
1 pound russet potatoes, peeled (2 large potatoes)
1 cups plus 2 tablespoons heavy cream or half and half, divided
1 ¼ cups (4 ounces) grated Gruyère cheese, divided
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F and butter an 8x8-inch square baking dish
Put the olive oil and butter in a medium (10-inch) sauté pan and sauté the onions over medium-low heat for 10 minutes, until tender
Thinly slice the potatoes by hand or with a mandoline
Place them in a large bowl with the 1 cup of cream, 1 cup of Gruyère, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper
Add the onion mixture and mix well
Pour the potato mixture into the prepared baking dish and press down lightly to smooth the top
Combine the remaining 2 tablespoons of cream and 1/2 cup of Gruyère and sprinkle evenly on the top
Bake for between 1 ¼ hours, until the potatoes are very tender and the top is browned and bubbly
Allow to cool for 10 minutes and serve hot

ingredients.raw: 4 tablespoons butter plus some for prepping the baking dish
3-4 sprigs thyme, oregano or rosemary (leaves only, finely chopped)
This should take around 15 minutes until they are softened and slightly golden

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 375°F convection
Peel and slice the onions and garlic
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a pan and gently saute the onions along with the garlic and thyme on medium to low heat
While sautéing the onions, peel and slice the potatoes, using a mandolin or the slicer attachment on your food processor, if available, to slice the potatoes
Brush an oven-safe 8 or 9 inch baking dish with soft butter and start building the potatoes
Nicely arrange about 1/3 of the potatoes on the bottom of the dish and sprinkle on half of the onions
Season with salt and pepper then arrange another 1/3 of the potatoes
Sprinkle the rest of the onions, season, and nicely arrange the rest of the potatoes
Add seasoning, stock, and scatter small cubes of the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter on top of the potatoes
Cover the dish with a lid or tin foil and cook in preheated oven for 45 minutes
Remove the lid or tin foil and cook for another 30 minutes until the stock is absorbed by the potatoes and the top layer is crisp

ingredients.raw: 3 pounds medium white potatoes or Yukon golds, skinned and diced into 1-inch cubes

instructions.text: Stovetop method:
Instant pot method:
Transfer the potatoes to a large mixing bowl and sprinkle with the white vinegar
Toss the potatoes with the vinegar and set the potatoes aside to cool, about 15-20 minutes
Add the celery and the green onions to the potato mixture
Chop the hard boiled eggs and add to the potato mixture
In a medium bowl, mix the mayonnaise, yellow mustard, celery seed and salt and pepper
Fold into the potato mixture and season with more salt and pepper to taste
Sprinkle with paprika if desired and serve or chill before serving

ingredients.raw: 4 cups of little potatoes (fresh creamer), cut up

ingredients.note: Paprika Onion powder Garlic powder Kosher salt Pepper

ingredients.raw: Enough to coat it, shouldn’t have a lot at the bottom/ shouldn’t be swimming in it
Can do it at a lower temperature if needed, it will just need to cook for a little longer

instructions.text: Cut little potatoes in half (bite size pieces); sometimes may have to cut in quarters
Coat potatoes with olive oil and mix
Microwave for a minute in a bowl
Mix in paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, kosher salt
Mix in enough pepper “to taste it”
Place a layer of tin foil on baking sheet and add potatoes to sheet
Stick in oven at 425°F convection for about 20 minutes or until crisp
Preheat oven to 425°F convection
Place potatoes in pot and fill with water, covering about an inch over the potatoes
Cover with lid, bring to a boil
Once boiling, lower the temperature and simmer for 15 minutes
Drain water and let potatoes cool
Once cooled, mix with olive oil, garlic, salt, pepper and thyme
Spray a baking sheet with olive oil, pour potatoes onto sheet and spread them out
Using a potato masher, flatten each potato on the pan
Spray another layer of olive oil on the potatoes
Place in oven for 20 minutes
Sprinkle with parmesan cheese and place back in oven for another 10 minutes

ingredients.raw: 4 medium/large russet potatoes (cleaned and cut into thin wedges)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 425°F convection
Whisk together olive oil, paprika, garlic powder, chili powder and onion powder
Using a gallon sized ziplock bag add potato wedges and olive oil mixture and shake them all up until they are evenly coated
Cover a baking sheet in foil and spread coated potato wedges onto it
Bake for 30-40 minutes or until crispy golden and cooked through

ingredients.raw: See steps 13-15 for air fryer method
Use your own judgement, cooking longer or shorter, if needed, keeping an eye on the fries to make sure they don't burn

instructions.text: Slice your sweet potatoes into long, thin strips, about 1/4-inch wide
Add the fries to a large bowl of cold water and soak for at least 30 minutes
Preheat the oven to 425°F and line a large baking sheet (or two medium baking sheets) with parchment paper, then set aside
Drain the fries, then blot dry the fries with a clean towel
Place the fries in a gallon size ziploc bag
In a separate small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, garlic and onion powder, smoked paprika and black pepper until combined
Add the mixture to the bag of fries, then toss until the fries are evenly coated
Add the cornstarch to the bag and toss until the cornstarch has soaked into the oil
Arrange fries in a single layer and don’t overcrowd, otherwise they will never crisp up
Bake for 20 minutes, then flip the fries so they can cook on all sides
Rearrange so that the fries are evenly spaced and not overlapping again, then bake for 10-15 more minutes, or until the fries are crispy and have begun to brown a bit on the tips
Remove from oven, sprinkle with desired amount of salt, then let fries rest for 5 minutes
Preheat your air fryer to 380°F
Transfer fry mixture to the air fryer, and cook until tender and crispy (about 20 minutes)
Shake the basket a few times to rearrange the fries, and use a flipper to flip them

ingredients.note: (or the scooped out insides from/ following the potato skins recipe)

ingredients.raw: The mixture should be workable but dry
ingredients.note: Helps to use two forks to wiggle any tots that are more stuck to the foil than others

instructions.text: If starting with fresh potatoes:
If using the already cooked potatoes, dice the potatoes very small
Transfer potatoes to a large bowl
Stir in cornstarch, garlic powder, onion powder,salt and pepper
If baking:
If frying:
Preheat oven to 400°F
Pierce potatoes 4-5 times with a fork
Rub potatoes with canola oil and place on a baking sheet
Bake potatoes in preheated oven for 1 hour
When potatoes are done allow them to cool for 10 minutes
Lower oven temp to 350°F
Slice potatoes in half lengthwise and scoop the flesh into a large bowl and mash
Place the skins back on the baking sheet
To the potato flesh add sour cream, milk, butter, salt, pepper, 1/2 cup cheese 1/2 the bacon and 1/2 the scallions and mix until well blended and creamy
Spoon the mixture into the potato skins and top each with remaining cheese, green onions and bacon
Bake for another 15 minutes

ingredients.raw: 1/8-1/4 teaspoon chili garlic sauce or sriracha

instructions.text: Cook noodles al dente according to package instructions
As the pasta is cooking, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, ground ginger, chili garlic sauce, garlic cloves and black pepper together in a bowl until combined
Once the pasta is ready, drain it and immediately toss the pasta with the sauce and green onions until combined
Serve warm or cold, sprinkled with sesame seeds and extra green onions

ingredients.raw: A lot of spinach (3/5 a box)
ingredients.note: Can pretty much have however much you want Shrinks a lot

ingredients.raw: A sprinkling of grated parmesan cheese, if desired
Be careful! Garlic burns and does not taste good burned

instructions.text: Melt butter in the frying pan on low
Mince garlic and mix in with the butter on low
Pour spinach in pan and mix around a lot so the butter and garlic coat all of it on medium
Once it is almost done being cooked, add salt and pepper and parmesan cheese
Leave in pan on low until ready to be served

ingredients.raw: Trader Joe’s Cornbread Stuffing Mix (or other brands if out of season)
14.5 ounce container of Mirepoix (celery, carrots, onion)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F and lightly grease a rectangle baking dish
Add stuffing mix to a large bowl and set aside
Melt butter in a saucepan
Add mirepoix, thyme, sage, and cook for 8-10 minutes until softened and onions are transluscent
Add garlic and cook for 1 minute
Add seasoning pouch and mix everything together
Add chicken stock and simmer for about 3 minutes
Carefully pour mixture over stuffing mix and gently toss
Transfer to the casserole dish
Cover and bake for 40-45 minutes
Uncover and bake an additional 5 minutes if you like the top to be crispier

ingredients.raw: 8 ounces linguine rice noodles or regular pasta
1/8 - 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes to taste

instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook noodles until al dente, about 6 minutes
Meanwhile, prep your veggies/ garnishes: In a small bowl mix soy sauce, hoisin, honey, chili garlic paste, and red pepper flakes
Before the noodles are done, in a large saute pan, heat sesame oil over medium heat
Add garlic and cook for 30-60 seconds, then add sauce and stir
When noodles are done, drain and add immediately add to pan with sauce and stir until the noodles are well coated
Mix in carrots, peppers, peanuts, green onion, and Sriracha

ingredients.raw: 1/2-3/4 cup Mayonnaise (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
2 tablespoons pesto (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
For a smoother consistency, use an immersion blender

instructions.text: In a jar, combine and whisk together mayonnaise and pesto
If desired, add more of either to taste

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup Mayonnaise (store bought or see recipe for homemade)

instructions.text: In a small bowl, add mayonnaise and stir to remove any lumps
Add lemon zest, lemon juice and garlic and stir to combine

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup Mayonnaise (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
Additional ingredients to test if needed:
ingredients.note: 1 small garlic clove, fresh 1/2-3/4 teaspoon dijon mustard

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F
Set the garlic on a piece of aluminum foil, drizzle olive oil and sprinkle with some coarse sea salt
Wrap up in the aluminum foil, making sure it is tight
Place the aluminum foil wrapped garlic directly in the oven, no need for a baking sheet and roast for 35-40 minutes
After they cool, move the garlic to a small bowl and mash all the cloves up with a fork until a paste forms
In the same bowl with the roasted garlic, add lemon juice, worcestershire sauce, sugar, and balsamic vinegar
Mix until smooth, and taste
If not getting the desired garlic flavor, mince once small clove into the bowl and mix/ blend to combine
If still not getting desired taste, add dijon mustard and mix/ blend
Chill in refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup Mayonnaise (store bought or see recipe for homemade)

instructions.text: Combine all ingredients together and whisk until smooth

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup Mayonnaise (store bought or see recipe for homemade)

instructions.text: Combine all ingredients together and whisk until smooth
Heat soy sauce, sugar, and mirin in a small saucepan over medium-high heat
Cook and stir until liquid reaches a boil
Once boiling, reduce heat to medium low so sauce is simmering and continue to mix for 10-15 more minutes until sauce becomes a caramel color
Remove from heat and pour into bowl to cool
As sauce cools, it should become very thick
Serve with sushi or any other desired dish

ingredients.raw: 1/2 teaspoon chili garlic sauce, or more, to taste

instructions.text: Whisk together all ingredients and set aside until ready to use

recipes.title: Peanut Dipping Sauce
notes: Note that this sauce is thicker than the peanut sauce above and meant for dipping*

instructions.text: Whisk together all ingredients and set aside until ready to use

recipes.title: Potsticker Dipping Sauce
notes: Sauce can be refrigerated in an air-tight container for several days

instructions.text: Bring soy sauce, vinegar, sugar and 1/4 cup water to boil in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring until sugar dissolves
Pour mixture into a bowl and stir in scallion, ginger and oil
Whisk together ingredients, then taste and add more mayo or sriracha to reach desired level of spice

ingredients.raw: 2 ½ teaspoons cornstarch mixed with 1 tablespoon cold water
Or any pot that is preferably not nonstick
Can use a cast iron or stainless steel pot
Include beef, onions, and everything else when measuring

instructions.text: In a dutch oven melt 1 tablespoon butter over medium-high heat
Add the beef and let it cook, without mixing/ moving it for about 3 minutes until a nice charred crust forms
Once crust forms, flip it over and repeat on the other side, continuing to do so until all sides have a nice charred crust
Once beef is browned, add the tomato paste and stir for 2 minutes
Pour red wine and beef broth in pot and scrape up brown bits from the bottom of the pot
Add the sliced shallot, soy sauce, sugar, and thyme sprigs and stir
Raise temperature to high and bring to a boil
Once boiling, continue to cook at just a slightly lower heat so that is doesn’t boil over for 15 minutes until it reduces to about 4 cups
After sauce has reduced, pour into a large measuring cup to make sure it is about 4 cups
Once there are 4 cups of sauce left, strain the sauce to remove all solids
Pour pure sauce back into sauce pan and raise heat to medium-high and bring to a boil
While waiting for sauce to boil, in a small cup, mix together cornstarch and water
Once sauce is boiling, add cornstarch mixture to pot and mix for about 30 seconds while sauce is boiling until it has slightly thickened
Reduce heat to low and add the 4 remaining tablespoons butter, one tablespoon at a time, whisking in between each
Season with salt and pepper
Combine all ingredients and mix well
Cover and place in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving

ingredients.raw: The syrup will reduce a little and the alcohol will burn off, leaving nothing but flavor behind
The mixture will cool down slowly and should thicken/caramelize
If not eating right away, heat on medium-high and bring to boil to warm again before serving

instructions.text: In a small pot, pour in all ingredients in a pot
Bring mix to a boil over medium-high heat, while stirring to dissolve the brown sugar
Once it starts boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low and allow mixture to simmer for about 10 minutes
Turn off the heat but leave the pot on the stove, covered
While it’s still warm, if eating right away, pour over your waffles and enjoy
Combine all ingredients in a small bowl and stir to combine

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoons Barbecue Sauce, plus 1/4 tablespoon more as needed

recipes.title: Cranberry Orange Sauce
notes: Cranberry sauce will keep for 10 days in a covered container in the refrigerator
Can also be frozen for up to two months

ingredients.raw: 1 (12-ounce bag cranberries, fresh or frozen (not dried)
You may need to mash them a bit with a spoon
Thaw overnight in the refrigerator before using

instructions.text: In a medium saucepan over high heat, bring the orange juice, water and sugar to a boil
Add the cranberries, orange zest, and salt and return to a boil
Reduce the heat to medium and boil gently for 10-12 minutes, until most of the cranberries have burst open
Transfer sauce to a serving bowl, cover and chill until ready to serve

recipes.title: Blueberry Sauce
notes: Good for cheesecake, pancakes, french toast*
Makes enough to fill one pie

instructions.text: Mix all ingredients together until well combined

recipes.title: Caramel Sauce
notes: Cover tightly and store for up to 1 month in refrigerator and reheat when ready for use

ingredients.raw: 6 Tablespoons (90g) butter, room temperature cut up into 6 pieces
1/2 cup (120ml) heavy cream, at room temperature
1/2 teaspoon Kosher salt (Use 1 teaspoon to make salted caramel)
Sugar will form clumps and eventually melt into a thick brown, amber-colored liquid as you continue to stir
Be careful in this step because caramel will bubble rapidly when butter is added

If you notice the butter separating or if the sugar clumps up, remove from heat and vigorously whisk to combine it again until it comes back together, even if it takes 3-4 minutes, then return to heat when it’s combined again
Since the heavy cream is colder than the hot caramel, the mixture will rapidly bubble when added and rise in the pan as it boils

instructions.text: Heat sugar in a medium heavy-duty saucepan over medium heat, stirring constantly with a high heat resistant rubber spatula or wooden spoon
Once sugar is completely melted, immediately stir in butter until melted and combined
Once butter melts and combines with sugar, cook for 2 minutes without stirring
Very slowly stir in all the heavy cream, then stop stirring and allow to boil for 1 minute
Remove from heat and stir in the salt
Allow to slightly cool down before using, as caramel will thicken as it cools

ingredients.raw: 8 ounces dark chocolate, chopped into small pieces

ingredients.note: 3 tablespoons butter, melted 2 ounces light corn syrup

instructions.text: Melt chocolate in a bowl set over a pot of water, taking care not to allow the water to touch the chocolate
Warm cream, butter, and salt (use the microwave) until butter is melting and cream feels warm to the touch
Pour cream mixture over chocolate in the double boiler and stir until chocolate is completely melted
Stir in corn syrup until glaze is smooth and shiny, with no visible lumps

recipes.title: Cream Cheese Icing
notes: Good for cinnamon sugar pretzels, red velvet cookies*

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon milk or half and half (if necessary)

instructions.text: Mix softened butter and softened cream cheese in a bowl
Add powdered sugar and vanilla extract and mix with electric mixer (preferably) until smooth
Add milk to thin if necessary

recipes.title: Raspberry Sauce
notes: Haven’t tried yet - Ina Garten’s recipe*
https://www.foodnetwork.com/recipes/ina-garten/panna-cotta-with-fresh-raspberry-sauce-12049031
The sauce can be refrigerated for up to one week

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (12 ounces) seedless raspberry jam, such as Tiptree

instructions.text: Place the raspberries, sugar, and 1/4 cup water in a small saucepan over medium heat
Bring to a boil, lower the heat, and simmer for 4 minutes
Pour the cooked raspberries, the jam, and the framboise into the bowl of a food processor fitted with the steel blade and process until smooth
Pour into a container and chill

recipes.title: Strawberry Sauce
notes: Good for cheesecake, pancakes, french toast*

ingredients.raw: If strawberries aren’t very juicy, you may need to add 1-2 tablespoons of water as needed

instructions.text: In a saucepan over medium heat, add strawberries, sugar, lemon juice, and cornstarch, and stir to combine
Bring to a boil and reduce heat to medium-low
Cook for 6-8 min or until mixture has thickened, and remove from heat and allow to cool

ingredients.raw: 1 large red onion, halved and sliced about 1/4-inch thick
4 large garlic cloves, peeled and halved
About 2 teaspoons Kosher salt (depending on the saltiness of the chicken stock)
1 cup dry white wine, such as Pinot Grigio

instructions.text: Melt the butter in a large saucepan over medium heat
Add the onion and garlic and sauté, stirring often, for 15-20 minutes, until the onion becomes browned and starts to caramelize
Sprinkle on the flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 ½ minutes
Stir in the chicken stock, Cognac, sage leaves, bay leaves, salt, and pepper
Bring to a boil, lower the heat, and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally
Set aside at room temperature for 1 hour and strain, pressing the solids lightly and then discarding them
Refrigerate until ready to use
After the turkey is cooked, remove it to a carving board to rest while you finish the gravy
Place the roasting pan on the stovetop over medium heat and add the wine
Bring to a boil, lower the heat, and simmer for 2 minutes, stirring and scraping up all the bits clinging to the bottom of the pan
Slowly whisk the gravy base into the pan
Simmer for about 5 minutes, until the gravy is smooth and slightly thickened
Taste for seasonings and add more salt or pepper if needed
Strain gravy and serve
Mix all the ingredients together in a bowl and chill before serving

recipes.title: Mayonnaise
notes: Refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks

ingredients.raw: 1/2 tablespoon lemon juice, plus more to taste if desired
1/2 tablespoon white wine vinegar, plus more to taste if desired
1 small clove garlic, minced (optional, but adds more flavor)

ingredients.note: Can substitute vegetable/ canola oil

ingredients.raw: The egg/ lemon juice mixture must reach the blades for this to work
If the mixture does not reach the blades, double the recipe before attempting
Do not pulse or move the head

instructions.text: Place egg, lemon juice, white wine vinegar, mustard, garlic, and salt in the bottom of cup or jar that just fits the head of an immersion blender
Pour oil on top and allow to settle for 15 seconds
Place head of immersion blender at bottom of cup and turn it on high speed
As mayonnaise forms, slowly tilt and lift the head of the immersion blender until all oil is emulsified
Blend in food processor or blender until combined and smooth, or whisk by hand until smooth

ingredients.raw: 5 whole Plum (Roma) Tomatoes, seeded and diced small
1/2 whole Large (or 1 Small) Red Onion, diced small
1/2 Jalapeno Peppers seeded and small dice

instructions.text: Mix all ingredients together in a bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 min

ingredients.raw: 1/2 jalapeno pepper, seeded, deviend and finely chopped

instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients
Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour or until chilled

ingredients.raw: 3 tablespoons Hot Honey (or sub with regular honey if don’t have)

ingredients.note: Or up 1 ½ - 2 teaspoons cayenne pepper if not using hot honey

instructions.text: Combine all ingredients in a saucepan, then heat over medium-high until butter has melted and spices become fragrant
Use a brush to coat each piece of chicken with hot sauce
Ingredients
Mix all the ingredients together in a bowl and chill before serving
Ingredients
In a bowl/ jar, combine all ingredients
In a bowl/ jar, combine all ingredients

ingredients.raw: 2 large apples, peeled, cored and thinly sliced
1-2 tablespoons white sugar to sprinkle on top of the pastry braid before baking
You should get around 8 to 10 strips depending on the length of your pastry braid

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F
In a medium bowl, combine brown sugar and cinnamon
Add the thinly sliced apples and toss to coat, then set aside
In a mixing bowl, combine softened cream cheese, white sugar, and vanilla, then set aside
Carefully unfold the sheet of puff pastry and place it on top of a sheet of parchment paper, then roll lightly with a floured rolling pin so it is flat
Transfer the puff pastry with the parchment paper to a baking tray
Add the cream cheese mixture down the center of the puff pastry only
Arrange apple slices on top of the cream cheese mixture, overlapping
Using a sharp knife, slice diagonal strips down both sides of the puff pastry, being careful to leave a 1/2-inch border around the cream cheese and apple slices
Starting at one end, begin folding the strips over the apples, alternating sides to "braid" the dough, continuing until the apples are covered
Tuck the end strips under the puff pastry
Brush the pastry braid with the beaten egg white and sprinkle the top with white sugar
Bake in the oven for 20-25 minutes or until golden brown
Allow to cool slightly, then drizzle caramel sauce on top
Slice and serve

recipes.title: Apple Crisp
notes: Can cover leftovers and store in the refrigerator for up to 5 days

ingredients.raw: 7–8 medium apples, peeled, cut into 1-inch chunks (about 10 cups of chunks)
1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar
1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg or pie spice
3/4 cup (150g) packed light or dark brown sugar
1/2 cup (1 stick; 115g) unsalted butter, cold and cubed or grated
Optional: Salted caramel and vanilla ice cream for serving

instructions.text: Ingredients filling:
Preheat oven to 350°F and lightly grease a 9×13-inch baking pan
Mix all of the filling ingredients together in a large bowl, then spread into the baking pan
To make the topping whisk the flour, brown sugar, and cinnamon together in a medium bowl
Cut the butter using a pastry cutter or fork until the mixture becomes super crumbly
Stir in the oats and sprinkle over the filling
Bake for 45 minutes or until the topping is golden brown and the fruit juices are bubbling around the edges
Remove from the oven, place on a wire rack, and allow to cool for at least 5 minutes before serving
Serve plain or with salted caramel and/ or vanilla ice cream

ingredients.raw: 1 pound apples (3 medium or 2 large), peeled if you wish, cored, cut into 1/2-inch wedges
1/3 cup (65 grams) light or dark brown sugar
It’s going to be very thick
There may be a few slices left over
For bigger crumbs, squeeze the crumbs into small fistfuls and break these up into a couple bigger chunks over the cake

instructions.text: Heat oven to 325°F and lightly coat an 8-inch square or 9-inch cake pan with butter or nonstick spray, then line it with parchment paper and spray paper
To prepare apples, toss apples with lemon juice, then cinnamon and sugar and set aside
To make crumbs, whisk butter, sugars, cinnamon, and salt together until evenly mixed
Add flour and mix until it disappears, then set aside
To make cake, beat butter with sugar until lightened and fluffy
Add egg, sour cream, and vanilla and beat until combined
Sprinkle surface of batter with with baking powder and salt, and beat well to combine
Add flour and mix only until it disappears
To assemble, scrape batter into prepared cake pan and smooth it flat
Arrange apples on cake, slightly overlapped
Pour any cinnamon-apple juices from bottom of bowl over apples
Sprinkle crumbs over apple slices
Bake the cake for about 50-55 minutes until a toothpick inserted into the apples doesn’t hit any crisp spots and if you look closely, you’ll see juices bubbling around some apples

recipes.title: Babka
notes: Store leftover babka at room temperature for up to 3 days or freeze

ingredients.raw: This may take a couple minutes
You’ll need to scrape the bowl down a few times
Dough will not fully double, so don’t fret if it doesn’t look like it grew by more than half
If you want this to be a single-day process, let it rise at room temperature for about 3 hours then once the dough is done rising, put it in the fridge for 30 minutes before rolling it out
Using a pizza cutter works best
Don’t worry if this step makes a mess, just transfer the twist as best as you can into the prepared loaf pan
If it browns too quickly, you can cover it with foil
When fully baked, you’ll feel almost no resistance when you push down on top, temp should be between 185 and 200
It will seem like too much but it’s not, it makes the bread glossy and moist

instructions.text: To make the dough: Combine the flour, sugar, yeast and zest in the bottom of the bowl of a stand mixer
Add eggs, milk and vanilla, mixing with the dough hook until it comes together
With the mixer on low, add the salt, then the butter, a spoonful at a time, mixing until it’s incorporated into the dough
Mix on medium speed for 8-10 minutes until dough is completely smooth
Grease a large bowl and place dough inside, cover with plastic and refrigerate in fridge for at least half a day, preferably overnight
To assemble loaves: Coat a 9x4-inch (2 ¼ or 1 kg) loaf pan with oil or butter, and line the bottom of each with a rectangle of parchment paper

Take dough out of the fridge, roll out on a well-floured counter to about a 10-inch width (the side closest to you) and as long in length (away from you) as you can when rolling it thin about ¼ inch thick, likely 10-12 inches
If using chocolate filling: In a medium saucepan over medium heat, combine sugar, cream and salt
Simmer, stirring occasionally, until sugar completely dissolves, about five minutes
Scrape mixture into a bowl
Stir in chocolate, butter,vanilla, cocoa and cinnamon until smooth, then let cool to room temperature
Spread the chocolate mixture evenly over the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border at the furthest long end
If using a cinnamon filling: Combine all ingredients in a mixing bowl until smooth
Evenly spread the cinnamon filling over the dough leaving a 1/2 inch border at the furthest long end
Brush the end farthest away from you with water, roll the dough up with the filling into a long, tight cigar, seal the dampened end onto the log
Slice log down the middle the LONG way into 2 long ropes
Pinch the top ends gently together, lift one side over the next, forming a twist and trying to keep the cut sides facing out
Cover with plastic wrap and leave to rise for another 1 ½-2 hours at room temperature
Bake the proofed loaf at 375°F for roughly 30 minutes or until golden brown
While babka is baking, to make syrup: Bring sugar and water to a simmer until sugar dissolves
Remove from heat and set aside to cool down
As soon as the babka leaves the oven, brush it generously with the syrup
Let cool in the pan for 20 minutes, then transfer to a cooling rack
Rolling from long side and cutting into 1 ½ - inch slices, flatten slightly on parchment lined baking sheets prior to rising, and bake for about 15-18 minutes, glaze with syrup and cool

ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup banana liqueur (if you don’t have this you can replace with rum)
4 bananas-cut in half lengthwise, then halved

instructions.text: Combine the butter, sugar and cinnamon in a flambé pan or skillet
Place the pan over low heat either on an alcohol burner or on top of the stove, and cook, stirring, until the sugar dissolves
Stir in the banana liqueur, then place the bananas in the pan
When the banana sections soften and begin to brown, carefully add the rum
Continue to cook the sauce until the rum is hot, then tip the pan slightly to ignite the rum, or ignite with a long match
When the flames subside, lift the bananas out of the pan and place four pieces over each portion of ice cream
Generously spoon warm sauce over the top of the ice cream and serve immediately

ingredients.raw: 2 ½ teaspoons (1 package) active dry yeast
1/4 cup, plus a pinch of granulated sugar
3 ½ - 4 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for rolling
3 cup powdered sugar (for dusting the baking sheet and topping)

instructions.text: In the bowl of a stand-up mixer, whisk together the water, yeast, and a pinch of sugar, then let it rest for 5 minutes, until foamy
Add the browned butter, the warmed milk, 1/4 cup sugar, and egg to the bowl of the stand-up mixer
Add 2 cups of flour, the salt, and baking powder, then beat on low speed, adding more flour (up to 1 ½ - 2 cups more) until you have a soft, slightly sticky dough
Scrape down the sides of the bowl, sprinkle with a bit more flour, cover with plastic wrap, and let rest at a warm room temperature for 1 hour and 30 minutes
Punch down the dough and dump it onto a lightly floured work surface
Roll the dough out into a roughly 16x12-inch rectangle and, using a pizza cutter, cut it into 2-inch squares, then cover lightly with plastic wrap
In a medium saucepan fitted with a deep-fry thermometer and set over medium heat, heat about 2 inches of oil to 375°F
Spread a generous amount of powdered sugar onto a rimmed baking sheet
Working in batches of about four, gently lower the beignets into the hot oil and cook for 2-3 minutes total, until golden brown on each side
Using a slotted spoon, transfer them directly to the powdered sugar and then dust them with them with even more powdered sugar immediately
Let the oil return to 375°F before each batch and repeat with the remaining beignets

ingredients.raw: 10 tablespoons (1¼ sticks) unsalted butter, at room temperature
2 ½ ounces/70 grams bittersweet chocolate, melted and cooled
2 ½ tablespoons unsweetened Dutch-process cocoa powder
Too thick is preferable to too thin
You will have vanilla frosting left over
You can smooth it out with an offset spatula, if you need to

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees and arrange two racks evenly spaced in the oven
Put the butter and granulated sugar in the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with the paddle attachment and beat on medium-high speed for 2-3 minutes, until light and fluffy
With the mixer on low, add the egg and vanilla and mix well, scraping down the sides with a rubber spatula
Sift the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a bowl
With the mixer on low, alternately add the flour mixture and sour cream in thirds and mix just until combined, then stir with a rubber spatula
With a standard (2¼-inch) ice cream scoop, place 5 level scoops of batter 2 inches apart on each of two sheet pans lined with parchment paper
Bake for 10 minutes, then rotate and switch the pans from the top to bottom rack to bake the cookies evenly
Bake for another 6-8 minutes, until the edges are lightly browned and a cake tester comes out clean
Cool on the pans for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack set over a sheet pan to cool completely
Place the confectioners’ sugar in a medium bowl and whisk in 3 tablespoons boiling water, the corn syrup, vanilla and salt
Continue to whisk, adding more boiling water as needed, until you have a thick yet spreadable frosting that is the texture of hot fudge sauce
Flip each cookie over and spoon glaze over half of its flat side, spreading to edges with the back of the spoon
Place on wire rack to set
Whisk melted chocolate into vanilla frosting, then whisk in cocoa and enough room temperature water to make a thick yet spreadable glaze
Glaze the bare half of each cookie
Let glaze set for at least 1 to 2 hours before serving.
Allow to set for 30 minutes
Serve at room temperature

recipes.title: Blondies
notes: Blondies stay fresh covered at room temperature for up to 1 week

ingredients.raw: 1 ¼ cups (250g) packed dark (or light) brown sugar
1 tablespoon pure vanilla bean paste or extract
1/4 cup bourbon (optional - if not using decrease flour by ⅛ cup)
Or spray pan generously with nonstick spray
Pay attention because once it starts to brown, it browns very quickly

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F and line an 8-inch metal baking pan with aluminum foil or parchment paper, leaving an overhang on the sides to lift the finished blondies out
Melt the butter in a medium, light-colored saucepan set on low heat
Once the butter is melted, turn the heat to medium and use a heat proof spatula to stir the butter and scrape the bottom of the pan as the butter foams and spatters
Once the butter quiets down, it will begin to smell nutty and will develop brown (not black) bits at the bottom of the pan, about 2-4 minutes
Pour butter into a small heat-proof bowl, making sure to scrape in the brown bits, and allow to cool while you whip the egg
Add eggs to bowl of a stand mixer fitted with whisk attachment (or use hand held mixer)
Add in both sugars, salt and vanilla, and whip on high speed until the mix turns from runny and dark, to light, thickened and pale in color, from 7-10 minutes
Reduce speed to low and drizzle in the brown butter and bourbon until incorporated
If using a stand mixer, switch to the paddle attachment and add the flour all at once and continue mixing on low until roughly combined
Stir in the add-ins with a spatula until they are evenly distributed, but don’t over-mix
Spread evenly into prepared baking pan and smooth it with back of a spoon or spatula
Bake for 25-30 minutes, until the edges are very light brown, but the center looks a little under-baked (Temperature should be about 210°F - it will firm up quite a bit as it cools)
Let the blondies cool in the pan, then lift out onto a cutting board and cut squares into your desired size

ingredients.raw: 1 21-ounce can Lucky Leaf Premium Blueberry Fruit Filling
4 tablespoons unsalted butter, at room temperature

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F and grease a 9x13-inch baking pan with nonstick cooking spray
Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, cinnamon and salt together in medium bowl until combined
Add eggs, vanilla, milk and oil and whisk until smooth
Pour batter into prepared baking dish
Dollop Lucky Leaf Premium Blueberry Fruit Filling on top of batter and gently swirl the filling and batter together with a knife or rubber spatula
To prepare the topping, combine brown sugar, flour, cinnamon and salt
Once combined, add butter and mix until crumbly
Sprinkle topping evenly over batter
Bake until topping is light golden-brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 40-45 minutes
Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature, about 30 minutes
Slice and serve with ice cream if desired

ingredients.raw: 6 ounces cream cheese, at room temperature

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F and spray an 8-by-8-inch baking pan with cooking spray
Line the pan with parchment paper so that it hangs over on two sides, then spray the parchment
For the crust: In the bowl of a food processor, combine the butter, sugar, flour, cinnamon and salt
Process until the mixture starts to come together and clump, about 1 minute
Remove 3/4 cup and reserve; press the remaining crust mixture evenly into the prepared baking pan, then set aside
For the filling: In a medium bowl, add cream cheese, 1/4 cup of sugar and 1 egg and mix until smooth
Spread the filling mixture over the crust evenly
Spread the pie filling over the cream cheese mixture
Take the reserved crust and sprinkle it over the top of the berries, squeezing the mixture in your hands to encourage large lumps
Bake for 1 hour, then let it cool
Remove the bars from the pan and cut into 9 pieces
Refrigerate until ready to serve

ingredients.note: Can substitute 1 ⅔ cup evaporated milk for milk and cream

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup heavy cream or half and half
The mixture will thicken within a few minutes
Keep whisking the custard while it's cooking, do not walk away or it will be nothing but lumps
This thickening process should take between 1 and 3 minutes
There will be some odd little scrambled eggs bits in the sieve and that's what you want to exclude
Scrape sides and bottom of the bowl and mix just until combined
Don't make it too tight and don't press
You might have a little bit of custard left over

instructions.text: To make custard: Vigorously whisk together the egg yolks and sugar until completely combined and lightened
Add flour and cornstarch, whisk very well and set aside while heating the milk
Heat milk, heavy cream, butter and salt until simmering
Slowly add heated milk mixture into the egg yolk mixture stirring constantly
Return the mixture into the pot and bring to simmer stirring constantly
As the mixture just begins to resist the whisk, drop the heat to low and keep whisking until the mixture turns thick and just begins to bubble

When the mixture just starts to bubble, you'll know it's as thick as it's going to get, so quickly remove the pan from the heat and pour the mixture through the sieve into the empty bowl, helping it through with a rubber spatula
Whisk in the vanilla
Cover the pastry cream with plastic wrap, making sure to get the wrap right down on the surface, preventing a skin from forming
To make the cake: Preheat oven to 375°F and line 17x11x1 pan with parchment paper and spray with cooking oil spray (make sure to get the sides too)
Beat eggs with electric mixer on high speed for 5 minutes until thick and light yellow
Slowly beat in granulated sugar
Lower speed to low and add water and vanilla extract
Still on low, slowly add flour, baking powder and salt
Beat just until batter is smooth
Pour batter into prepared pan and spread evenly
Bake for 8 minutes and touch the center of the cake to make sure it's done
Run a spatula around the edges of the pan to loosen the cake, then let it rest for a couple of minutes
Generously sprinkle a clean kitchen towel with powdered sugar
Carefully, turn the cake pan upside down onto the towel
Gently remove parchment paper and trim off crusty edges, if necessary
While the cake is still hot, gently roll the cake with the towel from the narrow end
Cool on a wire rack for at least 30 minutes
Gently unroll the cake
Spread cold custard all over the cake, leaving about an inch and a half one of the short ends (not the one you start rolling on)
Carefully roll the cake back up (starting with the side that has filling to the edge) and place cake on the serving dish, seam down
To make the ganache: Bring heavy cream to just before boiling, be careful not to let it actually boil or it will raise
Pour boiling cream over the chopped chocolate and let sit for about 2-3 minutes.
Stir until melted, then let it cool for a few minutes
Pour ganache over the top of the cake roll and gently spread it around

ingredients.raw: 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, or 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste
1 1/4 cups all-purpose flour, plus more for sprinkling
6 tablespoons (3/4 stick) unsalted butter, plus more for the pans
2 teaspoons vanilla extract, or 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste
4 ounces bittersweet chocolate, or 2/3 cup bittersweet chocolate chips

instructions.text: Fit a fine-mesh strainer over a medium bowl. Cut 2 tablespoons unsalted butter into small cubes.

Place 4 large egg yolks, 1/2 cup granulated sugar, 3 tablespoons cornstarch, and 1/4 teaspoon kosher salt in a medium saucepan. Whisk until no dry bits remain and the mixture is well combined. Add 1 3/4 cups whole milk and whisk to combine.

Cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, until the mixture begins to thicken, about 8 minutes. Once it thickens, continue to whisk, pausing every few seconds to check for bubbles. As soon as it begins to bubble, continue to whisk constantly for 1 minute. Remove the saucepan from the heat.

Add the butter and 2 teaspoons vanilla extract or 1 teaspoon vanilla bean paste. Whisk until the butter is melted. Scrape into the strainer and push through the strainer with a flexible spatula. Scrape the pastry cream on the underside of the strainer into the bowl. Discard the contents of the strainer. Press a sheet of plastic wrap directly onto the surface of the pastry cream to prevent a skin from forming. Refrigerate until cold, at least 3 hours and up to 3 days.
Place 3 large eggs in the bowl of a stand mixer (or large bowl if using an electric hand mixer). Let sit until room temperature.

Meanwhile, arrange a rack in the middle of the oven and heat the oven to 325ºF. Coat the sides and bottoms of 2 (9-inch) round cake pans with butter. Line the bottom of each pan with a parchment paper round. Sprinkle a little all-purpose flour over the sides and bottoms, and tilt and shake to distribute evenly, tapping out the excess.

Place 1 1/4 cups all-purpose flour and 2 teaspoons baking powder in a medium bowl and whisk to combine. Cut 6 tablespoons unsalted butter into small cubes and place in a microwave-safe measuring cup. Add 3/4 cup whole milk and microwave in 30-second intervals, stirring in between each, until the butter is melted, 60 to 90 seconds total. (Alternatively, heat the butter and milk in a small saucepan over medium heat.)
Add 2 teaspoons vanilla extract and 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, and stir to combine.

Add 1 1/4 cups granulated sugar to the bowl of eggs. Beat with the whisk attachment on high speed until thick and doubled in volume, about 6 minutes. The mixture should fall back on itself in a ribbon and be pale yellow in color. Reduce the mixer speed to low and pour in the milk mixture in a steady stream. Add the flour mixture, a few spoonfuls at a time, and beat, scraping down the sides of the bowl halfway through, until just combined, about 30 seconds total.

Evenly divide the batter between the pans (about 14 ounces or 2 3/4 cups each). Bake until the cakes are a light golden brown and until a tester inserted in the center of each cake comes out clean, 22 to 25 minutes.

Place the pans on a wire rack and let cool for 15 minutes. Run a small offset spatula or thin knife around the edges of each cake to loosen. Flip the cakes out of the pans onto the rack and remove the parchment paper. Gently flip right side up, and let cool completely, about 30 minutes.

Place one cake right-side up on a large flat serving plate or cake stand. Stir the pastry cream to loosen. Dollop onto the cake and spread into an even layer, going all the way to the edges. Place the second cake layer on top with the bottom facing up. Press down gently to adhere. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
MAKE THE CHOCOLATE GANACHE:
Coarsely chop 4 ounces bittersweet chocolate (or use 2/3 cup bittersweet chocolate chips) and place in a medium bowl. Add 1 tablespoon corn syrup and 1/8 teaspoon kosher salt.

Place 1/2 cup plus 1 tablespoon heavy cream in a small microwave-safe bowl (or measuring cup) and microwave in 30-second intervals until it begins to bubble, about 90 seconds total. (Alternatively, heat in a small saucepan over medium-high heat until bubbling around the edges.)

Immediately pour the cream over the chocolate. Let sit for 1 minute. Whisk until the chocolate is melted and the mixture is smooth. Pour the ganache over the chilled cake, letting it run down the sides of the cake. Refrigerate until the ganache is set, about 30 minutes.

ingredients.raw: 9 ounces bread (challah or French bread), cut into 1-inch cubes
1/4 cup dark rum (can also add in some butterscotch schnapps)
Pour very slowly, to prevent eggs from cooking
Can also prep the night before and leave in fridge overnight

instructions.text: For the Bread Pudding, grease an 8-inch square pan with butter, then set aside
Adjust oven rack to middle position and preheat oven to 350°F
Spread bread cubes onto a rimmed baking sheet and bake until edges of bread feels dry, about 10 minutes
Remove from oven and let pan cool on a wire rack
In a medium saucepan, whisk milk, cream, and sugar to combine
Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until scalding
Remove from heat and whisk in chocolate until melted and smooth
Let sit to cool slightly, about 10 minutes
In a large bowl, whisk egg yolks and egg to combine
Slowly whisk in warm milk mixture to combine
Whisk in vanilla
Add bread cubes and let soak, at least 30 minutes, gently stirring occasionally
Pour mixture into prepared pan and bake until just set, about 25 minutes
Let cool slightly before serving, about 15 minutes
To make the Brown Butter Rum Sauce, melt butter in saucepan over medium heat, swirling pan
Continue swirling pan and cooking butter until golden brown, about 2 minutes
Remove pan from heat and set aside
In a bowl, whisk egg yolks and sugar for several minutes until frothy
Gradually whisk in browned butter
In a saucepan, heat cream and milk over medium heat until bubbles form around edge
Slowly whisk hot cream mixture into egg mixture
Pour back into saucepan and heat over medium heat, stirring constantly, until sauce coats the back of a spoon, about 3 minutes
Remove from heat and whisk in chocolate until melted and smooth
Stir in rum
Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a serving bowl

recipes.title: Boxed Mix Chewy Brownies
notes: Brownies can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days

ingredients.raw: 2 large eggs plus 1 egg yolk, cold

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 325°F and line an 8x8-inch pan with foil and spray with nonstick cooking spray
In a microwave safe bowl, add the butter and sugar and microwave for about 1 minute, or until the butter is melted
Whisk in the eggs, egg yolk, and vanilla
Stir in the oil and cocoa powder
With a rubber spatula, stir in the flour, baking soda, cornstarch, and salt until combine
Stir in the chocolate chips
Spread the brownie batter evenly into the prepared pan
Place in the oven and bake for 30 minutes, or until the brownies are set and a cake tested inserted into the center has moist crumbs attached
Let cool completely before cutting and serving

recipes.title: Brownie in a Mug
notes: Can be divided into 2 mugs for 2 brownies or use 1 for a larger size brownie*

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons chocolate chips plus more for topping
1 ½ tablespoons vegetable oil, melted coconut oil or melted butter

instructions.text: Whisk together sugar, flour, cocoa powder and salt in a mug and stir until combined, and free of lumps
Stir in chocolate chips
Add oil, milk and vanilla, stir with a spoon or small rubber spatula until just combined
Sprinkle a few additional chocolate chips over the top and cook in the microwave for about 90 seconds (microwave times may vary)
Serve immediately with ice cream, whipped cream, powdered sugar, or berries

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (120g) King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour
3/4 cup (64g) unsweetened cocoa, Dutch-process or natural
1 teaspoon espresso powder, optional; for enhanced chocolate flavor
Half an 8-ounce package (113g) cream cheese, at room temperature
1 tablespoon King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour
The knife will draw the cream cheese filling into arcs
A toothpick inserted into the center will come out clean or with a few moist crumbs clinging to it; you shouldn't see any wet batter

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 375°F and lightly grease an 8x8-inch pan
Put all of the ingredients except the chocolate chips into a large bowl in the order in which they're written, then stir, and beat the mixture until it's smooth
Mix in the chocolate chips, then spoon the batter into the prepared pan
To make the cream cheese swirl, combine the cream cheese, sugar and flour in a small bowl, and mix until smooth
Add the egg yolk, mixing until smooth once again
Transfer the mixture to a disposable pastry bag, and cut just the very tip off the end
Place a small pool of the mixture in the center of the brownie batter
Draw circles around this pool about 1" apart, moving out from the center each time
Once the circles are drawn, take a table knife, wet the tip, and draw it from the center out towards the edge
Repeat this motion every 1-2 inches to fill in the rest of the spider web
Bake the brownies for about 30 minutes, or until they're just barely beginning to pull away from the sides of the pan
Remove the brownies from the oven; let them cool completely before cutting

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (80g) Dutch-process cocoa (or if using Natural cocoa add ½ teaspoon of baking soda)
12 tablespoons (1 ½ sticks, 170g) unsalted butter, room temperature
It'll become shiny-looking as you stir it
Again, adding the chips helps produce a shiny top crust

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F and lightly grease a 9x9-inch pan or line with parchment paper
In a medium-sized microwave-safe bowl, or in a saucepan set over low heat, melt the butter, then add the sugars and stir to combine
Continue to heat (or microwave) briefly, just until the mixture is hot (about 110°F to 120°F), but not bubbling
Crack the eggs into a bowl, and beat them at medium speed with the cocoa, salt, baking powder, and vanilla for about 1 minute, or until smooth
Add the hot butter/ sugar mixture to the egg/ cocoa mixture, stirring until smooth
Add the flour and chips, stirring until smooth
Spoon the batter into a lightly greased or lined 9" x 9" pan
Bake the brownies for 28 to 32 minutes, until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, or with just a few moist crumbs clinging to it
The brownies should feel set on the edges, and the center should look very moist, but not uncooked
Remove them from the oven and cool on a rack before cutting and serving

recipes.title: Glossy Fudge Brownies
notes: Can store in an airtight container with a sheet of wax paper between layers, for about 1 week at room temperature

ingredients.raw: 4 oz (112g) finely chopped dark chocolate, about 72%
The mixture will seem quite thin
Keep whipping the eggs until they're foamy, thick, and pale
The timing of this recipe is based on anodized aluminum and will vary significantly with glass, ceramic, or nonstick pans, materials that will also affect the brownies' consistency

instructions.text: Ingredients
Adjust oven rack to middle position and preheat to 350°F
Line a 8x8 inch anodized aluminum baking pan with foil or parchment paper to cover the bottom and sides of the pan, then grease lightly with pan spray
Brown the butter: In a 3-quart stainless steel sauce pan, melt butter over medium-low heat
When it has completely melted, increase heat to medium and simmer, stirring with a heat-resistant spatula while butter hisses and pops
Continue cooking and stirring, scraping up any brown bits that form inside the pan, until butter is golden yellow
Remove from heat and stir in dark chocolate
Making the Batter: Combine granulated sugar, brown sugar, salt, eggs, vanilla, and instant espresso (if using) in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the whisk attachment
Whip on medium-high until extremely thick and fluffy, about 8 minutes
Meanwhile, sift together flour and cocoa powder
When the foamed eggs are fluffy and thick, reduce speed to low and pour in the warm chocolate-butter
Once it is incorporated, add cocoa-flour mixture all at once and continue mixing until roughly combined
Finish with a flexible spatula, scraping and folding to ensure batter is well mixed from the bottom up
Add in chocolate chips and mix to combine
Pour into prepared pan and bake until brownies are glossy and just barely firm, about 30 minutes, or to an internal temperature of 205°F
Cool brownies to room temperature (at least 30 minutes) to allow their crumb to set before slicing
Slide a butter knife between the brownies and the short ends of the pan; gently tug on the foil, then lift to remove the brownies from the pan
Cut into 16 squares

ingredients.raw: 200 grams (7 ounces or 1 cup) granulated sugar
170 grams (6 ounces or 1 cup) light brown sugar
163 grams (5 ¾ ounces or 1 ¼ cups) natural cocoa powder (not Dutch process!)
70 grams (2 ½ ounces or scant 1/2 cup) all-purpose flour
This is essentially a sling that will allow for easy removal

instructions.text: Set oven to 300°F
Spray an 8-inch square baking pan with non-stick spray
Trim a piece of parchment paper so that it fits just inside the baking pan with 2-inch overhang on two sides and place it inside the pan
Beat the eggs with a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment at medium speed until fluffy and light yellow, 2-3 minutes
Sift together the granulated sugar, brown sugar, cocoa powder, flour and salt into a separate bowl
Reduce the mixer speed to low and slowly introduce the sugar mixture followed by the butter and vanilla
Mix until just smooth (add in chocolate chips now if using) and pour into the prepared pan
Bake for 15 minutes, then remove from the oven and cool for 15 minutes
Return the pan to the oven and bake until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the middle of the brownie reads 195°F, about 30 minutes
Cool in the pan for 30 minutes, then transfer the brownie to a cooling rack for 10 minutes before cutting into pieces using a plastic knife

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ teaspoons red gel food coloring
3/4 cup chocolate chips (milk chocolate, white chocolate, combination, etc.)
8 ounce (226g) brick of cream cheese, softened to room temperature

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line an 8-inch square baking pan with parchment paper, then set aside

To make the cheesecake swirl, use a handheld mixer on medium speed and beat the softened cream cheese, 1/4 cup sugar, egg yolk, and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla in a medium bowl until completely smooth, about 1 minute
To make the brownie layer, beat the 2 eggs together in a small bowl, then set aside
Melt the butter in a large microwave safe bowl in 30 second increments until melted
In this order, stir in 1 cup sugar, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, cocoa powder, espresso powder (if using), salt, food coloring, and vinegar
Whisk in the eggs
Using a spatula, fold in the flour until completely incorporated, but do not overmix
Fold in chocolate chips until well combined
Pour brownie batter into lined baking pan, leaving about 3-4 tablespoons of batter for top
Dollop spoonfuls of the cream cheese mixture on top of the prepared brownie batter
Cover with the last few tablespoons of brownie batter
Glide a knife through the layers as best you can, creating a swirl pattern, but be careful not to overblend
Bake brownies for 28-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted comes out clean
Allow the brownies to cool completely before cutting into squares
Cover brownies and store at room temp for 3 days or in refrigerator for up to 6 days

recipes.title: Flourless Healthy Brownies
notes: https://www.skinnytaste.com/amazing-flour-less-brownies/

ingredients.raw: 1 14-ounce canned low-sodium black beans, rinsed and drained
1 tablespoon unsweetened almond milk, or dairy, skim
1/2 teaspoon of freshly ground coffee or instant coffee

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F
Grease a nonstick 9 x 9-inch square baking pan with baking spray and line with parchment paper, leaving a 2-inch overhang on all sides
Blend the black beans, eggs, cocoa powder, sugar, oil, almond milk, balsamic, baking soda, baking powder and coffee in the blender until smooth and pour into a bowl
Fold in 1/2 cup chocolate chips until combined.
Pour the brownie batter into the prepared pan
Sprinkle the remaining chocolate chips evenly over the top of the brownies
Bake the brownies until a toothpick comes out clean, about 30-32 minutes
Allow the brownies to cool completely before slicing them into squares

recipes.title: Butterscotch Pudding Pie
notes: Can be made as one pie or in 8 ramekins*

ingredients.raw: Continue until all of the sugar is incorporated

instructions.text: Heat oven to 350°F
In a medium bowl, combine the cracker crumbs, 2 tablespoons granulated sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon salt
Melt 5 tablespoons butter and stir into the crumb mixture
Press the mixture into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch pie plate or into 8 ramekins (use a juice glass to press the crumbs down)
Bake until the edges of the crust are beginning to brown, 8-10 minutes
Melt the remaining 6 tablespoons butter in a small saucepan over medium heat
Once melted, swirl the pan until the butter begins to brown and smell nutty, 2-3 minutes more, then remove from heat
In a large bowl, combine the brown sugar, flour, cornstarch, and 1/2 teaspoon salt
Stir in the browned butter, milk, and egg yolks until fully incorporated
Place the large bowl over (but not in) a saucepan of simmering water and cook, whisking, until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 8 minutes
Remove from heat and whisk in the teaspoon of vanilla
Pour the pudding through a fine mesh sieve into an empty bowl to remove any clumps
Pour the pudding mixture into the prepared crust
Lay a piece of plastic wrap directly on the surface of the filling (this will prevent a skin from forming), then refrigerate until the meringue is ready
In a mixing bowl (preferably stainless steel or glass), beat the egg whites and a pinch of salt using the low speed of an electric mixer until they are frothy
Add the cream of tartar and increase the mixer speed to medium and continue beating until the whites are fluffy, with large bubbles forming around the edges
With the mixer running, add the sugar a few teaspoons at a time, mixing the sugar in completely before adding more
Continue beating until the peaks are firm but still glossy, then add the vanilla
Spoon the meringue onto the hot pie filling
If you are topping an unbaked pie filling, have it at room temperature (not cold) before you top it with the meringue
Spread the meringue to the crust edge to seal the filling in
Fluff it with the back of a spoon to make decorative peaks across the pie
Bake until the meringue is golden brown, 10-15 minutes
Let stand for at least 1 hour before serving

recipes.title: Carrot Cake Cupcakes
notes: Store leftover cupcakes in airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (200g) packed light or dark brown sugar
1/3 cup (80g) unsweetened applesauce, sour cream, or plain yogurt, at room temperature
1 ½ cups (200g) peeled and shredded carrots* (about 3 large carrots)
Optional add-in: 3/4 cup chopped walnuts or pecans; raisins or chocolate chips
8 ounces (224g) full-fat block cream cheese, softened to room temperature
1/4 cup (58g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
The batter will be slightly thick
For a loaf pan, bake for about 40 minutes, same oven temperature

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F, then line a 12-cup muffin pan with cupcake liners or a loaf pan with parchment paper and spray with non-stick spray
Whisk the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon and nutmeg together in a large bowl, then set aside
Whisk the oil, brown sugar, eggs, applesauce, and vanilla extract together until combined, and then whisk in the carrots
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and fold or whisk together until completely combined
Pour/ spoon batter into liners, filling only about 3/4 full to avoid spilling over the sides
Bake for 21-23 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean
Allow to cool completely before frosting
Meanwhile, make the frosting: In a large bowl using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle or whisk attachment, beat the cream cheese and butter together on high speed until smooth and creamy
Add confectioners’ sugar, vanilla extract, and salt
Beat on low speed for 30 seconds and then switch to high speed and beat for 2 minutes
Cover and refrigerate the frosting as the cupcakes finish cooling
Frost cooled cupcakes and enjoy

ingredients.raw: 6 ounces cream cheese, softened to room temperature
The more you stir brownie batter, the chewier the brownies will be!

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F
To make the brownies, combine the butter, sugar and cocoa powder in a medium microwave-safe bowl and heat on high for 30 seconds
Stop, stir the mixture, and then place it back in the microwave for another 30 seconds
Stir the brownie mixture gently to cool for about 3-4 minutes, and then stir in the salt and vanilla to further cool it
Once the brownie mixture feels warm (but not hot) to the touch, add the egg and stir until combined
Add the flour, and beat for at least 50 strokes
Add the chocolate chips and stir until combined
Line a 9x5x3” loaf pan with parchment paper, leaving extra on the ends to serve as handles
Spread the brownie mixture evenly in the bottom of the pan
Bake for 15 minutes, just until crust starts to form on top
While the brownies are baking, make the cheesecake filling: In a mixing bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth
Add in sugar and beat again
Add egg and vanilla extract and beat one more time
Add the chocolate chips and stir until combined
Spoon mixture on top of pre baked brownie bottom, smoothing to completely cover brownie layer
Reduce oven temp to 325°F
Return brownie and cheesecake mixture to oven and bake for 30-35 minutes, until center no longer wobbles and edges are just starting to turn golden
Remove from oven and let cool completely before refrigerating
Chill for at least 2 hours before slicing.
Use a long sharp knife, heated in a cup of hot water, then wiped dry for clean slices

ingredients.note: Can buy a premade Graham Cracker pie crust from store or just crush yourself To make for Passover, replace graham cracker crust with crumble macaroons

ingredients.raw: 2 (8-ounce) packages cream cheese, softened (room temperature - best to leave out for about 2 hours but can microwave if needed)
1 tablespoon orange or lemon zest (optional, but recommended)
Strawberry sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade) topped with fresh strawberries
Blueberry sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
Chocolate sauce (and add chocolate chips to cheesecake filling)
Make a thicker layer on the bottom than on the sides
To insure against leaks, place the pan on a cookie sheet that’s been lined with foil
Filling may look a little jiggly in the center, which is fine

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Prepare Graham Cracker crust first by combining graham cracker crumbs, powdered sugar, and salt then stir
Add melted butter and whisk/ mix until thoroughly combined
Pour crumbs into a 9” pie plate or Springform pan and press firmly into the bottom and up the sides of your pan, then set aside
Beat cream cheese and sugar at medium-low speed with an electric mixer until smooth
Mix in sour cream, vanilla and zest and eggs, one at a time, mixing on low speed just until combined
Pour cheesecake batter into the prepared pie crust
Transfer to oven and bake on 350°F for about 30 minutes, checking filling with thermometer about 1 inch away from the edge so that filling is at about 165-170°F
Remove cheesecake from oven and cool (leave in pie plate) on rack for a half hour
Cool in refrigerator for at least 2-3 hours, then top with sauce when ready to serve

recipes.title: Mini Cheesecakes
notes: Makes four 6-8-ounce ramekins, or fills 9-10 muffins tin sections*

ingredients.raw: 2/3 cup (65g) graham cracker crumbs (about 4-5 full sheet graham crackers)
One 8-ounce block cream cheese, softened to room temperature
1 ½ tablespoons (23g) sour cream, at room temperature
1 teaspoon fresh lemon or orange juice (optional, but recommended)
Strawberry sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade) topped with fresh strawberries
Blueberry sauce (store bought or see recipe for homemade)
Chocolate sauce (and add chocolate chips to cheesecake filling)
ingredients.note: Mixture will be sandy

ingredients.raw: If chilling for longer than 2 hours, loosely cover the cheesecakes

instructions.text: To make the crust:
To make the filling:
Bake until the edges are set and the centers only slightly jiggle, about 20 minutes
Allow cheesecakes to cool on a wire rack at room temperature for 30 minutes, then transfer to the refrigerator and chill for at least 2 hours or up to 1 day
Top with choice of topping

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ cups + 1 Tablespoon (196g) all-purpose flour (spoon & leveled)
1/2 cup (115g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
3/4 cup (150g) packed light or dark brown sugar
1 Tablespoon red food coloring (liquid or gel)
12 ounces (336g) block cream cheese, softened to room temperature
1/2 cup (90g) mini or regular semi-sweet chocolate chips
Beat in more food coloring if you’d like the dough to be redder
I say “scant” because otherwise you won’t have enough to make 22-24 mini cheesecakes
Try not to overbeat, which will cause cheesecake cracks
The cups are done in the oven when the cheesecake topping no longer jiggles when you shake the pan
Cover the cups with aluminum foil if the tops are getting too brown too soon

instructions.text: Ingredients
Preheat oven to 350°F, then line two 12-count muffin pans with cupcake liners
Make the red velvet cookie layer: toss the flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt together in a large bowl, then set aside
Using a handheld or stand mixer with a paddle attachment, beat the butter on high speed until creamy, about 1 minute
Scrape down the sides and the bottom of the bowl as needed
Switch the mixer to medium speed and beat in the brown sugar and granulated sugar until combined
Beat in egg, milk, and vanilla extract, scraping down sides and bottom of bowl as needed
Once mixed, add the food coloring and beat until combined
Turn the mixer off and pour the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients
Turn the mixer on low and slowly beat until a very soft dough is formed
Press 1 scant Tablespoon of cookie dough into the bottom of each cupcake liner
Bake each batch for 8 minutes to pre-bake the crust before layering the cheesecake on top
Make the cheesecake layer: using a handheld or stand mixer with a paddle attachment, beat the cream cheese on medium-high until completely smooth
Add the sour cream and sugar, beating on high until combined
Add the egg and vanilla and beat on medium until combined
Gently fold in the chocolate chips
Dollop 1 Tablespoon of cheesecake batter on top of the pre-baked cookie, spreading it to make sure it completely covers the cookie
Return the mini cheesecakes to the oven and continue to bake for about 20 more minutes, making sure the tops of the cheesecake does not get too brown
Allow to cool for 30 minutes on the counter, then in the refrigerator to set for another 1 ½ hours

ingredients.raw: 4 ounces (115g) cream cheese, at room temperature
1/3 cup (75g) crushed oreo cookies (extra for the base)
The edge of the cheesecake should be firm but make sure the center is jiggly
If it needs more time add in 5 second intervals until properly set

instructions.text: In a bowl, whisk together the cream cheese, sour cream,vanilla, sugar, egg and flour until light and fluffy
Mix in crushed oreos
In 3 microwavable mugs, sprinkle a layer of Oreo cookie crumbs in the bottom mug to create the cheesecake base
Pour 1/3 of the cheesecake mixture into each mug
Microwave each mug for 45 seconds and check cheesecake
Cool for 2 hours before serving

ingredients.raw: 1 package (10-12 ounces) white chocolate chips (I like Ghirardelli)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 325°F
In a small bowl, combine the graham cracker crumbs, sugar, and butter
Press mixture onto the bottom of a greased 9” springform pan, then set aside
In a large bowl, beat cream cheese and sugar until smooth
Beat in the sour cream, flour, and vanilla
Add eggs and beat on low speed just until combined
Fold in the chocolate chips, then pour over crust
In a microwave, melt raspberry jam and stir until smooth
Drizzle jam by teaspoonfuls over batter and cut through the batter with a knife to swirl
Place pan on a double thickness of heavy duty foil (about 18: square)
Securely wrap foil around pan and add 1” of hot water to larger pan
Bake for 80-85 minutes or until center is just set
Cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes
Carefully run a knife around the edge of pan to loosen
Cool 1 hour longer
Cover and refrigerate overnight, then remove sides of pan and serve

ingredients.raw: 1 ¾ cups all-purpose flour, plus more for pans
1 cup unsalted butter (2 sticks, room temperature)
1/2 tablespoon heavy whipping cream (may need more)
1/2 cup (4 oz.) semi-sweet chocolate melted and cooled
2 ½ cups powdered sugar sifted (spoon into measuring cup to measure)

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F
Butter two 8-inch x 2-inch round cake pans and line with parchment paper, then butter and flour the pans
Sift the flour, sugar, cocoa, baking soda, baking powder, and salt into the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle attachment and mix on low speed until combined
In another bowl, combine the buttermilk, oil, eggs, and vanilla
With the mixer on low speed, slowly add the wet ingredients to the dry
With mixer still on low, add the coffee and stir just to combine, scraping the bottom of the bowl with a rubber spatula
Pour the batter into the prepared pans and bake for 35-40 minutes, until a cake tester comes out clean
Cool in the pans for 30 minutes,then turn them out onto a cooling rack and cool completely
Place 1 layer, flat side up, on a flat plate or cake pedestal
With a knife or offset spatula, spread the top with frosting
Place the second layer on top, rounded side up, and spread the frosting evenly on the top and sides of the cake
In the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter on medium-high speed until light yellow and fluffy, about 3 minutes
Add in heavy cream, vanilla extract and salt and continue beating for 3 minutes
Mix in melted chocolate and espresso powder
Turn the mixer to low, gradually add the cocoa powder and powdered sugar then beat at medium speed, scraping down the bowl as necessary, until frosting is light, fluffy and whipped
Frost cake or cupcakes
Store leftover frosting in a covered container in the refrigerator (Let come to room temperature before using)

ingredients.raw: 1 cup less 1 tablespoon sweetened condensed milk

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 325°F and set two oven racks near the center of the oven
Line two baking sheets with parchment paper
In a medium bowl, mix together the coconut, sweetened condensed milk vanilla extract and chocolate chips, then set aside
In the bowl of an electric mixer, beat the egg whites and salt until stiff peaks form
Use a large rubber spatula to fold the egg whites into the coconut mixture
Using a mini ice cream scoop or two spoons, form heaping tablespoons of the mixture into mounds on the prepared baking sheets, spacing about 1 inch apart
Bake for 23-25 minutes, rotating the pans from top to bottom and front to back, until the tops and edges are golden
Let cool on the pans for a few minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely
If dipping the macaroons in chocolate, melt the chocolate in a microwave-safe bowl at medium power, stopping and stirring at 30 second intervals, until just smooth and creamy
Dip the bottoms of the macaroons in the chocolate, letting any excess drip back into the bowl, and return to the lined baking sheets
Place the macaroons in the refrigerator for about 10 minutes to allow the chocolate to set

recipes.title: Chocolate Chip Meringue Cookies
notes: Best enjoyed fresh on the day they are made but leftovers can be stored in an airtight container for several days
Take care when stacking the cookies; they are fragile

ingredients.raw: 3 large egg whites, at room temperature
3/4 cup high quality semisweet chocolate chips
