If it's not there yet, continue to whip the meringue on high speed at 30 second intervals and test again
This will anchor the parchment paper

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 250°F
Begin whipping egg whites in a stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment on low speed
As the whites start to look wet and frothy, after about 45 seconds, add cream of tartar and salt and allow it to dissolve with the mixer still on low speed, about 10 seconds
While the whites are still frothy, slowly begin to add the sugar in a steady stream with the mixer on low
Once you've added about 1/4 cup of the sugar, increase the speed to medium and continue to gradually add the sugar as the mixer runs
Once all the sugar has been added, increase the speed to high and whisk until the meringue is thick and glossy and holds firm peaks when you lift the whisk out of it, about 4 minutes
Beat in the vanilla extract
Once the meringue is at firm peak stage, spoon a tiny dot of it onto each of the four corners of a baking sheet and press the parchment paper liner down over it and do this for 2 sheets
Carefully fold the chocolate into the meringue with a rubber spatula, taking care not to deflate it
Use two soup spoons to drop 1.5-inch mounds of meringue onto the prepared baking sheets about 2 inches apart
Place the meringues in the oven and bake for 30 minutes
Turn the oven off and let the meringues cool in the oven for 30 minutes more
Let the meringues finish cooling on the baking sheets on the countertop for about 1 hour, or until the chocolate chips are no longer soft

ingredients.raw: 3 cups whole or 2 percent low-fat milk
3 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into 3 pieces
2 Tablespoons powdered sugar , or granulated sugar

instructions.text: For the crust: Preheat oven to 350°F
Add the whole Oreo cookies to a food processor and pulse into fine crumbs
Pour the crumbs into a bowl and stir in melted butter
Press mixture evenly and firmly into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch pie dish
Bake for 10-12 minutes, then set aside to cool
For the filling: Whisk sugar, cornstarch, cocoa, and salt together in large saucepan
Whisk in milk until incorporated, making sure to scrape corners of saucepan
Place saucepan over medium heat; cook, whisking constantly, until mixture is thickened and bubbling over entire surface, 8-10 minutes
Cook 30 seconds longer, then remove from heat
Add chocolate and butter and whisk until melted and fully incorporated
Whisk in vanilla, then pour filling into cooled pie crust
Press lightly greased parchment paper against surface of filling and let cool completely, about 1 hour
Refrigerate until filling is firmly set, at least 2 ½ hours or up to 24 hours
For the topping: Using stand mixer fitted with whisk attachment, whip cream and sugar on medium-low speed until foamy, about 1 minute
Add vanilla and increase speed to high and whip until stiff peaks form, 1 to 2 minutes
Spread whipped cream evenly over chilled pie and serve

recipes.title: Chocolate Lava Cake
notes: Can easily be halved*
Make Ahead & Freezing Instructions: You can prepare the batter through step 5, then cover tightly and refrigerate for up to 2 days
You can freeze the baked lava cakes for up to 3 months, but allow them to cool completely before freezing, then reheat in the microwave when ready to eat

ingredients.raw: 6 ounces bittersweet and semi sweet (half of each) good chocolate
Or spray half of a 12-count muffin pan and dust with flour or cocoa powder
ingredients.note: If baking in a muffin pan, the recipe will yield 6 cakes

ingredients.raw: Allow to come to room temperature before continuing with step 6

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 450°
Butter and dust lightly with flour or cocoa powder four 6-ounce ramekins, then tap out the excess flour, and set the ramekins on a baking sheet
Coarsely chop the chocolate
Place butter into a medium heat-proof bowl, then add chopped chocolate on top
Microwave on high in 10 second increments, stirring after each until completely smooth, then set aside
In a medium bowl, beat the eggs with the egg yolks, sugar and salt at high speed until thickened and pale
Fold the chocolate it into the egg mixture along with the flour and mix well
Spoon the batter into the prepared ramekins and bake for 12 minutes (8 minute if using muffin tins), or until the sides of the cakes are firm but the centers are soft
Let the cakes cool for 1 minute, then cover each with an inverted dessert plate
Carefully turn each one over, let stand for 10 seconds and then unmold, then serve immediately

recipes.title: Chocolate Souffle
notes: Soufflés begin to fall within minutes. You’ll still have that sponge-like texture underneath a crackly top, though it settles into a denser texture as time passes
Cover and store leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days

ingredients.raw: 4 Tablespoons (1/4 cup; 60g) unsalted butter, cut into 4 tablespoon size pieces
4 ounce (113g) semi-sweet chocolate bar, coarsely chopped
1 Tablespoon (15g) unsalted butter, extra soft (the softer it is, the easier it is to spread)
It’s best to do this in 3 separate additions, combining each addition completely before folding in more
No need to cover the batter unless you are refrigerating it for longer than 1 hour
Can be made up to 2 days in advance
Avoid opening/closing the oven too much

instructions.text: Melt the 4 tablespoons of butter and the chopped chocolate together in a double boiler or in a large heatproof bowl in the microwave in 20 second increments, stirring after each until completely melted and combined
Slightly cool for 3-5 minutes
Whisk egg yolks, vanilla extract, and salt into chocolate mixture, then set aside

In a completely clean residue-free medium glass or metal mixing bowl, using a handheld mixer or stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, beat the egg whites and cream of tartar together on high speed until soft peaks form, about 2 minutes
With the mixer running on high speed, slowly add the sugar in 3 additions about 1 tablespoon at a time, beating for 5 seconds before adding the next tablespoon
After all 3 tablespoons of sugar have been added, beat until stiff glossy peaks form, about 2 more minutes
Slowly and gently fold the egg whites into the chocolate mixture
Refrigerate the batter for 5-10 minutes as you preheat the oven and prepare the ramekins
Adjust the oven rack to the lower third position and preheat to 400°F

Prepare the ramekins: Brush four 6-ounce oven-safe straight sided ramekins with a generous coating of the softened butter, sprinkle in the granulated sugar, then shake/rotate each to ensure every spot of the ramekin has a sugar coating
Place ramekins on a baking sheet and discard excess sugar.
Spoon batter evenly into ramekins
Using a knife or icing spatula, smooth down the surface
Run a knife, icing spatula, or your thumb around the rim at the top, creating a “channel” between the batter and the rim of your pan
Place baking sheet with ramekins on top in the oven
Immediately reduce oven temperature to 375°F
Bake for 13-14 minutes or until the edges are set and the center just barely jiggles when you give the pan a light tap
Remove from the oven and serve immediately plain or with optional toppings

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ cups semi sweet chocolate chunks
Make sure you only brown the butter lightly - As soon as the butter starts to turn brown and smell nutty, take it off the heat to prevent any more liquid from escaping
Slowly add in the remaining flour a little bit at a time, stopping if the dough starts to get too dry

instructions.text: Brown the butter over medium heat, stirring constantly until the butter begins to foam and turns a golden brown, emitting a nutty aroma
Take butter off the heat and allow to cool to room temperature
In a medium bowl mix together the flour, salt and baking soda, then set aside
In a large mixing bowl combine the cooled brown butter, brown sugar, and white sugar and beat until mixed together
Add in the egg, egg yolk, and vanilla extract, and mix well
Mix half the dry ingredients into the wet until everything comes together
Fold in the chocolate and be careful not to over mix
If time allows, refrigerate the cookie dough for a half hour to overnight
When you are ready to bake the cookies, preheat the oven to 350°F and line a cookie sheet with parchment paper
Use a 1 ½ or 2 ounce cookie scoop to scoop the cookie dough out into balls, placing them 2 inches apart on the prepared sheet
Bake for 11 minutes, or until the edges are just golden brown and the centers have puffed up but are still gooey
Allow to cool before eating and enjoy

ingredients.raw: Best to use a wooden spoon

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Mix (not Whisk) together the cake mix, oil and eggs
Add chocolate chips
Bake for 10 minutes
Preheat oven to 375°F and line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper and set aside
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda and salt and set aside
Using a stand or electric mixer, beat the butter and both sugars on high speed until light and creamy, at least 3 minutes
Once combined, add the pudding mix, vanilla and eggs and beat on high for 2-3 minutes
Slowly add half of the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and turn the mixer on low to start so the flour doesn't get everywhere
Turn the mixer up to high and mix until combined
Add the rest of the dry ingredients and repeat until dough is just combined
Add chocolate chips and stir with a rubber spatula until incorporated
Drop cookies by 1 ½ tablespoons onto the prepared baking sheet
Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until slightly golden and just set on the top
Allow to cool on the baking sheet for two minutes then transfer to a baking rack to cool completely

ingredients.raw: 113g butter softened, but not melted (1/2 cup)
1 large egg cold (room temperature egg if you want your cookies to spread more)
2 cups extra large milk chocolate chips

instructions.text: Ingredients
Prepare cookie sheets with a piece of parchment paper
In a separate bowl, combine the flour, cornstarch, baking soda, baking powder, and salt.
Cream the butter, brown sugar, and granulated sugar on medium speed (with paddle attachment) for 2-3 minutes, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed
Add the egg and vanilla and mix until fully incorporated
Gradually add the flour mixture and mix until just combined
Add the chocolate chips and mix until evenly distributed
Scoop and measure the dough into 5 ounce balls and roll each into a ball
Place the cookies on the prepared sheet pans leaving 2-inches between each cookie
Chill the dough in the refrigerator for 30 minutes or overnight
Preheat your oven to 375°F
Just before baking, tear each ball in half and press back together with the jagged sides up
Bake for 12-16 minutes until golden brown

As soon as you pull out the cookies from the oven, using a larger cookie cutter or small bowl swoosh around your cookies to form the perfect round thick cookie, let the cookie rest on the baking sheet for 20 minutes to set fully before enjoying them

recipes.title: Dulce de Leche Stuffed Mexican Chocolate Cookies

ingredients.raw: 1/3 cup dark chocolate cocoa powder (I used Hershey’s special dark)
This will take a few minutes

instructions.text: Line a plate with wax paper and dollop on 12 heaping teaspoons of the dulce de leche
Place the plate in the freezer while you make the dough
In a bowl, whisk together the flour, cocoa powder, salt, baking soda and cinnamon, then set aside
Using an electric mixer, cream together the butter, sugar, and espresso powder until you reach a thick paste-like consistency
Mix in the vanilla and egg until combined
Finally, mix in the dry ingredients until well incorporated
Using a large 2-ounce cookie scoop, fill it about half way with the dough and press your thumb into the center
Place the chilled dulce de leche into the well and cover with more cookie dough
Once all cookies are complete, place them in the refrigerator for 30 minutes to firm up
Preheat the oven to 375°F and line a large cookie sheet with parchment paper
Bake 5 to 6 cookies at a time for 10-12 minutes, keeping the remaining cookie dough in the fridge while the first half of the batch bakes

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons milk, plus 1 more tablespoon if needed

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F and line baking sheets with parchment paper
Combine flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, nutmeg and salt in a medium bowl
Beat sugar and butter in large mixer bowl until well blended
Beat in pumpkin, egg and 1 teaspoon vanilla extract with sugar and butter until smooth
Gradually beat in flour mixture
Drop dough by rounded tablespoon onto prepared baking sheets
Bake for 12-14 minutes or until edges are firm
Cool on baking sheets for 2 minutes, then remove to wire racks to cool completely
Combine sifted powdered sugar, milk, tablespoon butter in a small bowl
Add vanilla extract and mix until smooth
Drizzle icing over cookies

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup unsalted butter, melted and cooled
Repeat this in 15 second increments until the chocolate is smooth and completely melted (roughly about 45 seconds)

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper, then set aside
In a large mixing bowl, combine the melted butter and sugars and mix well
Add the egg and egg yolk, vanilla extract and red food coloring and mix until smooth
In a separate bowl sift together the flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt to combine
Add the dry ingredients to the wet and mix together until combined and no streaks of flour remain
Add in the white chocolate chips a mix until just combined
Use a 1 ½ tablespoon cookie scoop to scoop cookies out and roll the balls with your hands so they are smooth, then place on the prepared cookie sheet an inch apart
Bake for 10 minutes or until the cookies have puffed up and crackled but are still a little gooey in the center
Transfer the cookies to a cooling rack to cool completely before assembling the cookie sandwiches
While the cookies cool, make the white chocolate frosting by chopping the white chocolate into small pieces and place in a microwave safe bowl
Microwave for 15 seconds and use a rubber spatula to stir the chocolate
In the bowl of a stand mixer fit with the paddle attachment, add the softened butter, powdered sugar, milk, vanilla extract, and melted white chocolate
Beat on low speed, gradually increasing to high speed
Mix for 1 minute or until light and fluffy
Place the frosting into a piping bag fitted with a medium tip
Pipe roughly 1 ½ tablespoons of frosting onto the bottom of half of the cookies, then sandwich with remaining cookies
Ingredients for the cookies
Preheat oven to 350°F
In a medium bowl, cream the butter and sugar
Add the eggs, ricotta cheese, and vanilla extract, and mix until well combined
In a separate medium bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, and baking soda
Add the flour mixture to the ricotta mixture and mix well
Refrigerate the dough for at least 30 minutes
Roll or scoop the dough into tablespoon-sized balls, and place on an ungreased cookie sheet about 2-inches apart
Bake for 10-15 minutes or until lightly browned
Allow cookies to cool on the baking sheet for 3 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely
In a medium bowl, whisk the milk, powdered sugar, and almond extract until smooth
Dip the tops of the cookies into the glaze and set upright back on the wire rack
Immediately top with sprinkles

recipes.title: Rugelach
notes: Store leftover rugelach in an airtight container at room temperature for several days

ingredients.raw: 6 ounces (170 grams) semisweet chocolate, finely chopped, or 1 cup (170 grams) mini chocolate chips
Don't overprocess; the dough should look crumbly, like cottage cheese.
If you don't have a cake pan, just guesstimate the size of the circle and cut it as smoothly as you can

instructions.text: If using a good processor, chop cold butter and cream cheese into smaller pieces
Put pieces into your food processor along with sour cream, vanilla, flour, salt, and sugar
Pulse together ingredients until a crumbly dough forms and begins to fall away from the sides of the processor
Let the butter and cream cheese come to room temperature
Using a stand mixer or hand mixer, cream the butter and cream cheese together with the sour cream and vanilla

In a separate bowl, sift together the flour, salt, and sugar, then slowly add the dry mixture to the wet mixture, mixing constantly, until dough holds together and begins to pull away from the sides of the bowl
Pour dough onto a lightly floured rolling surface and form into a rough ball shape
Divide the ball into four equal pieces and form those pieces into rough balls
Cover each ball with plastic and place in the refrigerator
Refrigerate dough balls for at least 1 ½ hours, for up to 48 hours
Pour nuts into a food processor along with the chocolate chips, berry preserves, and brown sugar, then pulse together until a thick, coarse paste forms, then set aside
Combine the cinnamon and sugar in a small bowl, then set aside
Beat your egg wash with water, then set aside
Preheat oven to 375°F
Lightly flour your rolling surface and rolling pin
Take one portion of the dough out the refrigerator (keep the rest of the dough cold until ready to use)
Roll out the dough to about 1/8 inch thickness
Lift the dough gently from the rolling surface (it may stick a bit) and re-flour your surface beneath the dough
Replace the dough onto the newly refloured surface
Use a round 9" cake pan as a guide to make a nice, smooth imprint of a circle in the dough
Cut the dough into a large circle, following the shape of the cake pan
Form the trimmed excess dough into a small ball
Wrap it in plastic and reserve in refrigerator, adding to ball with each batch that is made
Take 1/4 of the filling and place it in the center of the circle
Spread it thin across the surface of the dough, then gently press the filling down into the dough so it’s compact, leaving ¼ inch around the edge
Cut the circle into 8 or 12 equal triangles and cut a small notch in the wide end of each triangle
Roll each wedge up, beginning with the wide end and ending with the narrow end
Place the rolls point-side down onto the baking sheets, leaving an inch between the cookies, as they will expand slightly during baking
Brush the top of each cookie with egg wash, then sprinkle lightly with cinnamon sugar
Place cookies in the oven and let them back for about 25 minutes, or until golden brown
Roll out your next batch of cookies while this batch is baking
When the cookies are golden brown, remove from the oven and let them cool on a wire rack
When you're finished making cookies from the four dough balls, make a smooth ball from the leftover dough trimmings you've reserved and roll it out to make your fifth batch

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (115g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
Dough will be thick and sticky
The centers will look very soft.

instructions.text: Ingredients
Whisk the flour, baking powder, and salt together in a medium bowl, then set aside
In a large bowl using a hand mixer or a stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter and granulated sugar together on medium-high speed until creamed, about 1 minute
Add the egg and vanilla extract and beat on high speed until combined, about 1 minute
Scrape down the sides and up the bottom of the bowl and beat again as needed to combine
Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix on low until combined
Scoop large sections of dough (about 3 Tablespoons (2 oz) of dough each) and roll into balls
Place dough balls onto a large plate or lined baking sheet
Cover and chill cookie dough balls in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours (or up to 4 days)
Preheat oven to 350°F
Line baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats, then set aside
Arrange chilled cookie dough balls 3 inches apart on the baking sheets
Bake for 12-13 minutes or until lightly browned on the sides
Remove from the oven and allow cookies to cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely
Cookies stay fresh covered at room temperature for up to 1 week

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (240ml) cold heavy cream or heavy whipping cream
1 8-ounce block (225g) cream cheese, softened to room temperature
Optional for topping: homemade whipped cream
You should have about 2 cups (packed) crumbs, or 250g
The mixture will be thick and quite wet
Try to smash/break up any large chunks
Scrape down the sides and up the bottom of the bowl with a rubber spatula as needed
Make sure there are no large lumps of cream cheese
If there are lumps, keep beating until smooth
This takes several turns of your rubber spatula
Combine slowly as you don’t want to deflate all the air in the whipped cream
Use an offset spatula to smooth down the top.
The longer refrigerated, the better the pie will set up

instructions.text: Preheat to 350°F
In a food processor or blender, pulse 22 Oreos (including the cream filling) into a fine crumb
Pour crumbs into a large bowl
Add the melted butter and stir to combine
Pour the mixture into an ungreased 9-inch pie dish
With medium pressure using your hand, pat the crumbs down into the bottom and up the sides to make a compact, thick crust
Bake for 10 minutes, then allow to cool as you prepare the filling
To make the filling, use a hand mixer or a stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, and whip the cold heavy cream into stiff peaks on medium-high speed, about 4 minutes, then set aside
Using a hand mixer or a stand mixer fitted with a whisk or paddle attachment, beat the cream cheese on medium speed until perfectly smooth and creamy
Add the confectioners’ sugar and vanilla extract and beat on medium-high speed until combined
Using your mixer on low speed or a rubber spatula, fold the whipped cream and chopped Oreos into the cream cheese mixture until combined
Spread filling into cooled crust
Cover tightly with plastic wrap or aluminum foil and refrigerate for at least 6 hours and up to 2 days

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup hot coffee or hot water
1/2 cup (115g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
This recipe yields about 15 cupcakes, so line a second muffin pan with 3 cupcake liners or bake in batches
You should have enough batter for 15 cupcakes
Cupcakes must be completely cooled—and even chilled for 20–30 minutes in the refrigerator if they’re particularly sticky on top—before assembling
Add up to 1/4 cup more confectioners’ sugar if frosting is too thin or another 1 Tablespoon of cream if frosting is too thick
Add a pinch of salt if the frosting is too sweet
Spread on a thick layer, so it sets as a thick fudge-like topping
Pipe a looping swirl, or any design you prefer, on top of the ganache-covered cupcake
No need to chill ganache-topped cupcakes before doing this

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a 12-cup muffin pan with cupcake liners
To make the cupcakes, whisk the flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, baking powder, and salt together in a large bowl, then set aside
Whisk the oil, sugar, egg, vanilla, and buttermilk together until combined
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, add the hot coffee/water, and whisk until the batter is completely combined and thin
Pour/spoon the batter into the liners, filling only 2/3 full to avoid spilling over the sides
Bake for 20-22 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean
Allow cupcakes to cool in the pan for 10 minutes, and then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely
To make the chocolate ganache, place finely chopped chocolate in a medium heatproof bowl
Heat the cream in a small saucepan over medium heat until it begins to gently simmer
Pour over chocolate, then let it sit for 2–3 minutes to gently soften the chocolate
With a metal spoon or small rubber spatula, very slowly stir until chocolate has melted and mixture is smooth and thin
Once ganache mixture is smooth, transfer it to the refrigerator and let it chill for at least 30 minutes to thicken before spreading on cooled and filled cupcakes
To make the whipped vanilla buttercream filling, use a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, and beat butter on medium speed until creamy, about 2 minutes
Add confectioners’ sugar, heavy cream, and vanilla extract
Beat on low speed for 30 seconds, then increase to medium-high speed and beat for 3 full minutes
To fill the cupcakes, use a sharp knife and cut a circle into the center of the cooled cupcakes to create a little pocket about 1 inch deep
Spoon or pipe whipped buttercream inside each carved-out cupcake—use however much frosting will fit
Gently press the piece back on top of the filling
Top with ganache by spreading the thickened chocolate ganache on top of each cupcake with a knife or small icing spatula
Optional: Pipe icing swirl/loops on top: Fill a piping bag with the remaining vanilla buttercream

ingredients.raw: 3 cups (720ml) heavy cream or heavy whipping cream*
You’ll need hot water to pour into the baking sheet for the water bath
Keep those egg yolks moving so they don’t scramble
For a more accurate sign, they’re done when an instant read thermometer registers 170°F

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 325°F
Whisk the egg yolks and 1/2 cup (100g) of granulated sugar together, then set aside
At this point or before you temper the egg yolks in the next step, bring a small kettle or pot of water to a boil
Heat the heavy cream, espresso powder, and salt together in a medium saucepan over medium heat
As soon as it begins to simmer, remove from heat
Stir in the vanilla extract
Remove about 1/2 cup of warm heavy cream and, in a slow and steady stream, whisk into the egg yolks
In a slow and steady stream, pour and whisk egg yolk mixture into the warm heavy cream
Place ramekins in a large baking pan leaving one out of the pan
Divide custard between each ramekin, filling to the top
Pour enough hot water into the pan to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins
Place the last ramekin in the pan
Bake just until the creme brulee is set, but still trembling in the center, approximately 40-45 minutes
Remove pan from the oven and, using an oven mitt, remove the ramekins from the pan
Place on a wire rack to cool for at least 1 hour
Place in the refrigerator, loosely covered, and chill for at least 4 hours and up to 2 days before topping
Using the remaining granulated sugar, sprinkle a thin layer all over the surface of the chilled custards
Caramelize the sugar with a kitchen torch and serve about 5 minutes later to allow top to harden

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons granulated sugar plus 1 additional teaspoon
2-4 teaspoons turbinado sugar or Demerara sugar
Begin checking temperature about 5 minutes before recommended time
Tilt and tap ramekin for even coverage

instructions.text: Adjust oven rack to lower-middle position and heat oven to 300°F
Combine 1/2 cup cream, sugar, and salt in small saucepan
Bring mixture to boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally to ensure sugar dissolves
Meanwhile, place a kitchen towel on the bottom of the baking dish or roasting pan and arrange two 4- to 5-ounce ramekins (or shallow fluted dishes) on towel
Bring kettle or saucepan of water to boil over high heat
After cream has cooled slightly, stir in remaining 1/2 cup cream to further cool down
Whisk yolks in medium bowl until broken up and combined
Whisk in vanilla extract and about 1/4 cup cream mixture into yolks until loosened and combined
Repeat with another 1/4 cup cream
Add remaining cream and whisk until evenly colored and thoroughly combined
Strain through fine-mesh strainer into 2-cup measuring cup or pitcher (or clean medium bowl), then discard solids in strainer
Pour or ladle mixture into ramekins, dividing it evenly among them
Place baking dish with ramekins on oven rack and pour boiling water into dish, careful not to splash water into ramekins, until water reaches two thirds height of ramekins
Bake until centers of custards are just barely set and are no longer sloshy and digital instant-read thermometer inserted in centers registers 170-175°F, about 35-40 minutes
Transfer ramekins to wire rack and cool to room temperature, about 2 hours
Set ramekins on rimmed baking sheet, cover tightly with plastic wrap, and refrigerate until cold, at least 4 hours or up to 4 days
Uncover ramekins - if condensation has collected on custards, place paper towel on surface to soak up moisture
Sprinkle each with about 1 teaspoon sugar (1 ½ teaspoons for shallow fluted dishes)
Ignite torch and caramelize sugar
Refrigerate ramekins, uncovered, to re-chill, 30-45 minutes (but no longer), then serve

ingredients.raw: 1 cup coffee - cooled but not cold

instructions.text: Ingredients
Preheat oven to 350°F
Sift together all dry ingredients in a large bowl
In a separate smaller bowl whisk together all of the wet ingredients
Using a rubber spatula, slowly fold the dry ingredients into the wet
Grease and flour either a 9x13 baking dish, a bundt pan or 2 loaf pans
Bake for approximately 40 minutes (start checking at 35)
Can top with chocolate ganache or frosting

recipes.title: Apple Cider Donuts
notes: Donuts are best served immediately
Leftovers keep well covered tightly at room temperature for up to 2 days or in the refrigerator for up to 5 days

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar
Start checking at 20 minutes, 30 minutes, etc until you have 1/2 cup
For ease, use a large zipped-top bag, cut a corner off the bottom of the bag and pipe the batter into each donut cup, filling about halfway
To test, poke your finger into the top of the donut, and if the donut bounces back, they’re done
May want to use a brown lunch bag and put a few tablespoons of sugar in, drop a donut into the bag and give it a good shake to coat evenly

instructions.text: To reduce the apple cider, stirring occasionally, simmer the apple cider in a small saucepan over low heat until you’re left with about 1/2 cup, then set aside to cool for 10 minutes
Preheat oven to 350°F Spray donut pan with non-stick spray, then set aside
To make the donuts: Whisk the flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, apple pie spice, and salt together in a large bowl, then set aside
Whisk the melted butter, egg, brown sugar, granulated sugar, milk, and vanilla extract together
Pour the liquid ingredients into the dry ingredients, add the reduced apple cider, and whisk everything together until smooth and combined
Spoon the batter into the donut cavities
Bake for 10-11 minutes or until the edges and tops are lightly browned
Cool donuts for 2 minutes then transfer to a wire rack
Re-grease the pan and bake the remaining donut batter
To coat the donuts: Combine the granulated sugar, cinnamon, and apple pie spice together in a medium bowl
Once cool enough to handle, brush both sides of each donut with the melted butter, then dunk generously in the apple spice topping

recipes.title: Glazed Donuts
notes: Doughnuts are best enjoyed the same day
You can store in an airtight container at room temperature or in the refrigerator for 1-2 extra days

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (240ml) whole milk or buttermilk, warmed to about 110°F
6 tablespoons (86g) unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
4 cups (490g) all-purpose flour (spoon & leveled), plus more as needed
1/3 cup (80ml) heavy cream or whole milk
The mixture should be a little frothy on top after 5 minutes if using active yeast
Scrape down the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula as needed
If needed, add more flour, 1 Tablespoon at a time, until the dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl, but don’t add too much flour
You want a slightly sticky dough
If you do not own a mixer, you can mix this dough with a large wooden spoon or rubber spatula
If needed, punch down again to release any more air bubbles
If you can’t quite fit 12, re-roll the scraps and cut more
They will rise a bit as they rest

instructions.text: Whisk the warm milk, yeast, and sugar together in the bowl of your stand mixer fitted with a dough hook or paddle attachment, then cover and allow to sit for 5 minutes
Add the eggs, butter, vanilla, nutmeg, salt, and 2 cups (245g) flour
Beat on low speed for 1 minute
Add remaining flour and beat on medium speed until the dough comes together and pulls away from the sides of the bowl, about 2 minutes
Keep the dough in the mixer and knead for an additional 2 minutes or knead by hand on a lightly floured surface for 2 minutes
Lightly grease a large bowl with oil or nonstick spray
Place the dough in the bowl, turning it to coat all sides in the oil
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap, or a clean kitchen towel
Allow the dough to rise in a relatively warm environment for 1.5-2 hours or until double in size
When the dough is ready, punch it down to release the air
Remove dough from the bowl and turn it out onto a lightly floured surface
Using a rolling pin, roll the dough out until it is 1/2 inch thick, then using a 3-3.5 inch doughnut cutter, cut into approx 12 doughnuts
Line 1 or 2 baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats
Place doughnuts and doughnut holes on each
Loosely cover and allow to rest as you heat the oil
Place a cooling rack over another baking sheet
Pour oil into a large heavy-duty pot set over medium heat and heat oil to 375°F
Add 2-3 doughnuts at a time and cook for 1 minute on each side
Carefully remove with a metal spatula or metal slotted spoon
Place fried doughnuts onto prepared rack
Repeat with remaining doughnuts, then turn off heat
To glaze donuts:
To make frosting:

recipes.title: Sufganiyot (Jelly Donuts)
notes: Sufganiyot are best served fresh, as the powdered sugar will soak into the doughnut over time, and it will appear soggy
You can also freeze the sufganiyot

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ tablespoon instant yeast/ active dry yeast
2 cups (250 grams) unbleached all purpose flour, plus 1-2 cups more for dusting rolling surfaces
About 3/4 cup (5 ounces, 140 grams) fruit jelly or jam
ingredients.note: If using a berry jam, double the amount and strain of seeds and pulp before using (directions below) Pretty much just use as much as needed

ingredients.raw: 2 pints (32 fluid ounces) oil with high smoke point for frying
It should be gently stretched, so proceed carefully, as you can easily roll it too thin
Make sure you use plenty of flour on top and bottom, loosening the dough as you roll to keep it from sticking
If you do not have a thermometer, test the oil by placing a small piece of dough in it, and if it sizzles and floats, the oil is hot enough
Use a straw to tunnel into each donut, creating a narrow hollow hole in the top center of the pastry
Freeze them after the frying step, before filling
When ready to serve, defrost completely, fill with jelly and dust with powdered sugar before serving

instructions.text: Place flour, sugar, yeast, and salt into the bowl of the stand mixer, then mix
Add oil, eggs, water, and vanilla extract
Knead using a bread hook, mixing on low for 10 minutes, at the end of which it should be smooth but tacky
Move the dough to an oiled bowl, then cover with a towel or plastic wrap and let sit until double in size
Place the dough on a well floured surface and sprinkle the top with flour as well
Roll the dough out until it is about 1/2 inch thick
Cutout circles using a 2'' or 3" cookie cutter dipped in flour
Using a spatula, carefully remove the circles and place them on a floured tray
Take the remaining scraps and knead them together, then let sit for 5 minutes
Repeat steps until dough is used up
Sprinkle the circles with flour and cover with a towel
Set aside and let sit for 40 minutes in a warm place
Heat the oil to 320°F to 340°F
Place 3 or 4 sufganiyot in the pot one at a time
Let them simmer for 1 ½ minutes on each side until golden brown
Transfer cooked donuts to a wire rack or a paper towel-lined baking sheet to drain off oil
Using a squeeze bottle or a syringe filled with jelly, puncture a hole in the top of each donut and fill
Dust with sugar
Make the puff pastries, the pastry cream and chocolate ganache
Fit a pastry bag with a pastry tip and fill each with pastry cream that has been whisked smooth
Carefully fill the éclairs with pastry cream, piping it into the hole on the end without letting it seep out, but making sure there is pastry cream throughout the whole éclair
Dip the top of each eclair into a bowl with the chocolate ganache
Refrigerate to chill until ready to eat

recipes.title: Honey Cake
notes: Makes 3 loaves - but can easily be reduced to 1*
Fits in three loaf pans, two 9-inch square or round cake pans, one 9 or 10 inch tube or bundt cake pan, or one 9x13-inch sheet cake*

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (237 ml) brewed coffee or strong tea
This will ensure the cakes bake properly with the bottom baking faster than the cake interior and top
For angel and tube cake pans, this will take 60-75 minutes, loaf cakes, about 45-55 minutes, for sheet style cakes, baking time is 40-45 minutes

instructions.text: Preheat the oven to 350°F and generously grease the baking pan(s) with nonstick cooking spray, baking spray, or butter
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon (and other spices if using) and salt

Add the oil, 3/4 cup of the honey, sugar, brown sugar, eggs, vanilla, coffee or tea, orange juice, and rye or whiskey, if using, then mix thoroughly, until all ingredients are combined and no lumps remain
Pour the batter into the prepared pan(s), filling each about halfway
Drizzle the remaining 1/4 cup of honey over the batter
Place cake pan(s) on two baking sheets, stacked together
Bake until cake test is done, that is, it springs back when you gently touch the cake center
Let cool 15 minutes before removing from the baking dish
Slide a knife around the edges to help loosen the cake, if necessary

ingredients.note: 2 cups powdered sugar, sifted if possible 2 tablespoons milk, plus 1-2 more tablespoons if needed 1 tablespoon butter, softened 1 teaspoon vanilla

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Grease a loaf pan with cooking spray, lay down a layer of parchment paper, and grease parchment paper with cooking spray
In a medium sized bowl, mix together flour, baking powder, and salt, then set aside
In a larger bowl or stand mixer, combine softened butter and sugar and beat until light and fluffy
Add eggs, one at a time, and beat to combine
Add lemon zest, lemon juice, olive oil, sour cream, vanilla, milk, and poppy seeds
Continue beating mixture until fully combined
Slowly, about 1/2 cup at a time, add flour and beat to combine, then continue adding flour until all of it has been incorporated
Pour batter into greased loaf pan and bake for 55 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean
Let cool for at least 10 minutes
Serve with icing by either pouring across the whole loaf or drizzling on slices as you cut

ingredients.raw: 2 can Pillsbury Grands Southern Homestyle buttermilk biscuits (8 biscuits per can size)
Can use a loaf pan (8x4 inch) if making half the recipe

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 325°F
Grease a bundt pan generously with cooking spray so pan is completely coated
In a gallon size zip-lock bag combine sugar and cinnamon
Cut each biscuit into quarters
Add a few pieces of the biscuits to the cinnamon sugar mix and coat completely and continue doing so until all biscuit pieces are coated
Combine melted butter and brown sugar in a measuring cup/ bowl
Place biscuit quarters in pan and after each layer drizzle the butter sugar mixture over the pieces and repeat until all biscuits are in the pan
Can top with some of the leftover cinnamon sugar mix if desired
Place in the oven and cook for 30-40 minutes until golden brown
Preheat oven to 350°F
Cook egg noodles and drain
Coat 9x13-inch pan with the stick of butter then add the rest of the butter to the cooked noodles and put the noodles in the pan
In a separate bowl, mix cottage cheese, sour cream, eggs, milk and sugar together
Add cinnamon to taste
Add raisins, if desired
Pour mixture over noodles
To make the topping, mix crushed cornflakes, melted butter, and brown sugar together
Sprinkle topping mixture over noodles until covered
Bake for 45-60 minutes or until a knife comes out dry

recipes.title: Old Fashioned Pound Cake
notes: Can easily be halved*

ingredients.raw: 1 cup (2 sticks) butter at room temperature

instructions.text: Using a stand or electric mixer, beat the butter and sugar on high speed until light and creamy, at least 3 minutes
Add 1 egg at a time, beating after each egg
Add flour and whipping cream (do NOT whip) alternately and add vanilla
Pour the batter into a greased 10 cup Bundt pan (or use 2 loaf pans)
Start in a COLD oven at 325°F
Bake for 1 hour and 15 mins or more until done

recipes.title: Panna Cotta with Fresh Fruit Sauce
notes: Haven’t tried yet - Ina Garten’s recipe*
https://www.foodnetwork.com/recipes/ina-garten/panna-cotta-with-fresh-raspberry-sauce-12049031

ingredients.note: Raspberry sauce, blueberry sauce, strawberry sauce

instructions.text: In a small bowl, sprinkle the gelatin on 3 tablespoons of cold water
Stir and set aside for 10 minutes to allow the gelatin to dissolve
Meanwhile, in a large bowl, whisk together 1 ½ cups of the cream, the yogurt, vanilla extract, and vanilla seeds
Heat the remaining 1 ½ cups of cream and the sugar in a small saucepan and bring to a simmer over medium heat just until the sugar dissolves
Off the heat, stir the softened gelatin into the hot cream and stir until dissolved
Pour the hot cream and gelatin mixture into the cream and yogurt mixture and stir in the Grand Marnier
Pour into 7 or 8 serving glasses and refrigerate uncovered until cold
When the panna cottas are thoroughly chilled, cover them with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight
Before serving, spoon 2 tablespoons of the Fresh Sauce of your choice on each panna cotta and top with fresh fruit

recipes.title: Pastry Cream
notes: Good for cream pies and eclairs*
The pastry cream can be refrigerated for up to a week if it's tightly sealed

ingredients.raw: 480 grams (2 cups) whole milk (can use evaporated milk)
30 grams (3 tablespoons) unsalted butter, cubed and at room temperature
This thickening process should take between 1 and 3 minutes
There will be some odd little scrambled eggs bits in the sieve and that's what you want to exclude
Cream can be used hot, but I prefer to pipe it when it's down to room temperature

instructions.text: Combine the sugar and egg yolks in a medium mixing bowl and whisk until thickened, pale, and foam, about 3 minutes
Sift the cornstarch through a fine-mesh sieve into the egg mixture then whisk to combine
Set the bowl on a wet towel (to prevent scooting) and set near the cooktop
Position the sieve over a second mixing bowl and set aside
Combine the milk and salt in a 3-quart saucepan or saucier and bring to a bare simmer over medium heat, 3 to 5 minutes
Once hot, drizzle about a third of the milk into the sugar and egg mixture, all the while continuously whisking the eggs
Then reverse course and, still whisking, pour the nowwarmed egg mixture back into the pot with the milk
Move the pan back over medium heat and whisk briskly
As the mixture just begins to resist the whisk, drop the heat to low and keep whisking until the mixture turns thick and just begins to bubble

When the mixture just starts to bubble, you'll know it's as thick as it's going to get, so quickly remove the pan from the heat and pour the mixture through the sieve into the empty bowl, helping it through with a rubber spatula
Whisk the butter into the strained mixture a piece at a time, making sure each piece melts before introducing the next
Whisk in the vanilla
Cover the pastry cream with plastic wrap, making sure to get the wrap right down on the surface, thus preventing a skin from forming

recipes.title: Peanut Butter Bars
notes: Store in airtight container at room temperature

ingredients.raw: 4 4-ounce ghirardelli semi-sweet chocolate bars or 16 ounces chocolate chips
Mixture will come together into a “dough”

instructions.text: Line a 13x9-inch baking sheet with foil
In a large mixing bowl, beat 3/4 cup melted butter, powdered sugar, peanut butter and vanilla extract together
Using your hands, press into the bottom of the 13x9-inch dish
In another bowl, combine chocolate bars (broken into pieces) with 1 tablespoon butter, heat for 30 second intervals, stirring until chocolate is smooth, about 1 ½ minutes total
Spread evenly over top of peanut butter layer
Allow chocolate to set before cutting

ingredients.raw: Pie crust (Store bought or see recipe for homemade)
Egg wash: 1 large egg beaten with 1 tablespoon milk
2 medium apples, peeled and diced into small bite-size pieces (about 2 ½ cups diced)

instructions.text: Prepare the pie crust and chill
While the crust is chilling combine the diced apples, granulated sugar, butter, cinnamon, nutmeg, and allspice together in a medium saucepan or skillet over low-medium heat
While occasionally stirring, bring to a simmer and allow to simmer for 2 minutes
Remove from heat and allow to cool uncovered at room temperature for 30 minutes
Mix in the butterscotch chips after the mixture cools, if using
Combine all the blueberry pie filling ingredients
Preheat oven to 375°F and line two large baking sheets with parchment paper
Roll the pie crust to about 1/8" thick on a floured surface
Using a 3 1/2" cutter, cut out 20-24 circles, re-rolling the dough as necessary
Dampen the edges of half of the pastry circles with a bit of the egg wash
Place about 2 tablespoons filling in the center of each of the circles
Top each with unfilled pastry circle, and pinch the edges with fingers or a fork to seal
Brush the top of the pies with the remaining egg wash, and sprinkle with sparkling sugar
Use a knife to cut a couple of slits in the top of each to vent, then bake for 28-32 minutes or until golden brown on top and around edges, rotating pans halfway through

recipes.title: Baileys Chocolate Cream Pie
notes: Pie is best if eaten within 3-4 days

instructions.text: Grease an 8- or 9-inch pie pan and preheat the oven to 325°F
Combine the Oreo crumbs and melted butter in a medium bowl and mix until combined
Press the crumb mixture evenly into the bottom and up the sides of the pie pan, bake for 8-10 minutes, then set aside to cool.
To make the filling, add the cream cheese and sugar to a large mixer bowl and beat until well combined and smooth
Add in the Baileys and vanilla extract and beat until well combined and smooth
Add in the melted chocolate chips and cocoa powder and beat until well combined and smooth, then set mixture aside
In another large mixer bowl, add the heavy whipping cream and powdered sugar and whip on high speed until stiff peaks form
Add about half of the whipped cream to the Baileys mixture and gently fold together until combined
Add the remaining whipped cream and gently fold together until well combined
Spread about half of the filling evenly into the pie crust, then add the chopped Oreos in an even layer
Spread the remaining filling evenly over the chopped Oreos and refrigerate the pie until firm, 3-4 hours
To make the Baileys whipped cream, add the heavy whipping cream, powdered sugar and Baileys to a large mixer bowl and whip on high speed until stiff peaks form
Pipe the whipped cream around the outer edge of the pie, then finish off the pie with additional chopped Oreos and mini chocolate chips
Refrigerate the pie until ready to serve

ingredients.raw: 1 can (21 ounce) Premium Blueberry Fruit Filling
Pie crust (store bought or see recipe for homemade)

ingredientGroups.name: Streusel Topping (if only using bottom pie crust)

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar
1/3 (75g) cup unsalted butter, melted and slightly cooled
The crumble topping will be thick and crumbly

instructionGroups.name: Streusel Topping (if only using bottom pie crust)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 375°F
Gently press the pie crust into the bottom of a 9” pie plate
Fold the edges around the perimeter of the pan and crimp with fork
Mix all ingredients for filling together and pour onto crust
Cover pie with second crust, if using
If making a streusel topping - in a medium bowl, combine the brown sugar, cinnamon, and flour
Using a fork, stir in the butter
Sprinkle streusel topping on pie
Bake in oven for 35-40 minutes
Check on pie - if crust is browned but streusel isn’t, wrap tin foil around perimeter of plate, covering the crust and place back in oven for another 5-10 minutes until streusel browns
Take out of oven and let cool for 2 hours
Place fresh or frozen berries in a saucepan.
Whisk the cornstarch with the sugar, and pour over the berries.
Add the salt and lemon juice, stirring to combine.

Place the saucepan on a burner set to medium-high heat and cook, stirring, until the small amount of liquid in the bottom of the pan comes to a simmer. Reduce the heat to medium and continue to cook, stirring frequently, until the mixture starts to thicken, about 5 minutes
Transfer the cooked berries to a bowl and let cool to room temperature. It's fine to make the filling ahead of time, and refrigerate until you're ready to use it.
Preheat oven to 350°F
Fill pie plate 2/3 full with peeled, sliced apples
Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
In a small bowl, combine melted butter, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup flour, 1 egg, salt and pecans and/or raisins (if desired), then mix together and pour evenly over apples
Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes or until golden brown

recipes.title: Pie Crust
notes: If you only need one crust, freeze the other or divide measurements in half

ingredients.raw: 2 ½ cups (315g) all-purpose flour, plus more as needed (spoon & leveled)
1 cup (230g; 16 tablespoons) unsalted butter, frozen and grated
1/2 cup (120ml) ice water, plus more as needed (or ¼ cup each vodka and water)
Lift from bottom saran wrap and flip into pan

instructions.text: Place flour, sugar, and salt in a food processor; pulse 10 times
Add grated butter and process until mixture is crumbly
Add iced water (or vodka mixture) slowly through food chute, pulsing every few seconds until combined (do not form a ball)
Place pie dough on a lightly floured work surface
Using floured hands, fold the dough into itself until the flour is fully incorporated into the fats
Form it into a ball, then divide dough in half
Using your hands, flatten each half into a 1-inch thick disc
Wrap each disc tightly in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes and up to 5 days before using in a pie recipe
Place two overlapping lengths of plastic wrap on the work surface (big enough to roll out the dough)
Set the dough in the center and cover with two more sheets of plastic wrap layed out the same way
With rolling pin, roll dough into circle, about 12 inches in diameter
Remove the top sheets and invert the dough into a 9-inch pie plate
Gently press the dough into the bottom of the pie plate and remove the remaining wrap
Fold the edges around the perimeter of the pan and crimp with fork
Cover loosely with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 15 minutes
Remove pie pan from refrigerator, line crust with foil, and fill with pie weights or pennies
Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes (if making a full pie, don’t bake until you add filling)
Remove foil and weights, rotate plate, and bake for 5-10 additional minutes until crust is golden brown and crisp

ingredients.raw: 48 slices of frozen peaches or 7 fresh peaches
2 tubes of pillsbury 8-ounce original crescent rolls (8 in each tube)
1 ½ sticks of butter and more butter to coat the glass tray
Don’t cook this just keep it on stove until mixed

instructions.text: If using fresh peaches and peeling them:
Take approximately half a peach or three peach slices and roll it in the crescent slice and try to cover as much of the peach as possible
Coat bottom of pan (glass or ceramic - not metal!) with a little less than 1tablespoon of butter
Put them closely together in the pan
In a pot on the stove, melt the 1 ½ sticks of butter
Once melted, mix in sugar, vanilla, and cinnamon while still on stove
Pour mixture over crescent peach rolls
Pour 7-ounce sprite right in the middle of glass tray full of crescent peach rolls
Bake it at 350°F for 40 minutes

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons and 1/2 teaspoon of regular sugar
Use dough hook attachment for kitchenaid
If dough is too runny, add flour
use a big bowl or water may bubble over with baking soda
may have to reheat water in microwave for 1 min halfway through

instructions.text: In kitchen aid bowl, mix yeast, 1/2 teaspoon of sugar and warm water together, and let it sit for 10 minutes
Mix regular and brown sugar, melted butter, salt, and gradually mix in 2 cups of flour
Gradually mix and add another 3/4 cups of flour
Let that knead for about 5 minutes until ingredients combine
Butter bottom of a bowl and transfer dough to the bowl (buttered so dough won’t stick)
Cover it and let it sit for 30 minutes, preferably in an OFF oven (it's dark and warm)
When dough is ready, roll a handful out at a time and cut dough into one inch pieces
Boil water, move water to another bowl and add baking soda to bowl
Put pretzel bites in water for about 20-30 seconds and lay them out on parchment paper
Bake them at 425°F for about 8-10 minutes (watch carefully)
Make the frosting
To make cinnamon sugar mix sugar and cinnamon together in a bowl
Once pretzels are out of oven, dip them in some melted butter and coat in cinnamon sugar
Transfer to plate and enjoy with cream cheese dip

ingredients.raw: 1 packet active dry or instant yeast (2 ¼ teaspoons)
1 tablespoon brown sugar or granulated sugar
1 tablespoon unsalted butter, melted and slightly cool
3 ¾ -4 cups (460-500g) all-purpose flour (spoon & leveled), plus more for work surface
1 egg yolk beaten with 1 tablespoon water
Cinnamon Sugar: 3/4 cup of granulated sugar and 1 ½ teaspoons cinnamon
Mix with a dough hook attached to a stand mixer until dough is thick
If it is still sticky, add 1/4 – 1/2 cup more, as needed
Poke the dough with your finger – if it bounces back, it is ready to knead
Silicone baking mats are better for these
If using parchment paper, lightly spray with nonstick spray or grease with butter

instructions.text: Whisk the yeast into warm water and allow to sit for 1 minute
Whisk in salt, brown sugar, and melted butter
Slowly add 3 cups of flour, 1 cup at a time
Add 3/4 cup more flour until the dough is no longer sticky
Knead the dough for 4-5 minutes on low speed
Lightly grease a large bowl with oil and place dough in bowl, turning it to coat all sides
Cover with a clean towel or plastic wrap and allow to rest for 30 minutes - 1hour until doubled in size
Bring baking soda and 9 cups of water to a boil in a large pot
Preheat oven to 450°F and line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper or silicone baking mats, then set aside
Turn the dough out onto a lightly greased work surface, fold it over a few times to gently deflate it, then divide it into 8-10 pieces
Roll the dough into a 20-22 inch rope
Take the ends and draw them together so the dough forms a circle
Twist the ends, then bring them towards yourself and press them down into a pretzel shape, and repeat process for remaining pretzels
Drop 1-2 pretzels into the boiling water for 20-30 seconds (any more than that and your pretzels will have a metallic taste)
Using a slotted spatula, lift the pretzel out of the water and allow as much of the excess water to drip off, then place pretzel onto prepared baking sheet
Brush each pretzel with egg wash
Sprinkle each with coarse sea salt if not using cinnamon sugar
Bake for 10-12 minutes or until golden brown
If using cinnamon sugar: melt 2 tablespoons of butter
Brush the baked and warm pretzels with melted butter then sprinkle the tops with a mix of cinnamon and sugar

ingredients.raw: You will need to stop the mixer occasionally and scrape down the sides of the bowl and the paddle attachment
Before adding the last egg white, check the mixture for consistency: It should tear slightly as it falls from the beater, creating a“V” shape and the mixture may only need 1 egg white
Use a clean dampened finger to smooth any tips left from piping
For cream puffs, pipe into golfball-size shapes, 2 inches apart onto parchment lined sheet pans

instructions.text: Heat the oven to 425°F, adjust oven rack to the middle, and line two baking pans with parchment paper
Place water, butter, sugar, and salt in a 3-quart pot, set over high heat, and bring to a boil
As soon as it boils, add all of the flour at once and stir with a wooden spoon until the mixture starts to come together, about 1 minute
Decrease the heat to low and continue stirring until the mixture forms a ball and is no longer sticky, 3 to 4 minutes
Transfer the mixture to the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment and mix on low speed for 5 minutes to cool, or until there is no more steam rising
With the mixer still on low speed, add the eggs and egg whites, one at a time, mixing for about 30 seconds making sure each is completely incorporated before adding another
Transfer to a piping bag fitted with a plastic coupler and a round tip and pipe a little of the mixture into the four corners under the parchment paper to hold it in place on the sheet pans
Pipe the mixture into 2 ½-inch-long strips on the parchment, making 4 rows of 6
Place the sheet pan of eclairs in the oven and bake for 10 minutes
Reduce the oven temperature to 350°F and continue to bake until golden brown, 20-25 more minutes
Remove from the oven and immediately pierce one end of each eclair with a paring knife to release steam
Repeat with the remaining puff pastry
Let cool completely before filling

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon pumpkin pie spice (or 1/4 teaspoon each of ground nutmeg, ground ginger, ground cloves, and ground allspice
1/2 cup (100g) packed light or dark brown sugar
6 ounces (170g) block cream cheese, softened to room temperature
1/4 cup (1/2 stick; 60g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
We want an extremely nonstick surface for this cake roll
The cake will be warm as it just came out of the oven
I stick mine in the refrigerator for about 2 hours to speed it up
Flatten it out and spread frosting evenly on top, leaving about a 1/2 inch border around the cake
Make sure you’re rolling it tightly
Some frosting may spill out the sides, that’s ok!

instructions.text: Read the instructions through before beginning and make sure you are prepared for step 11 immediately when that cake comes out!
Preheat oven to 350°F and spray a 10×15 inch baking pan (jelly roll pan) with nonstick spray or grease with butter, so the parchment paper sticks
Line pan with parchment paper so the cake seamlessly releases in step 5 and spray or grease the parchment paper too
Whisk the flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, pumpkin pie spice, and salt together in a large bowl, then set aside
In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs, pumpkin, brown sugar, granulated sugar, and vanilla together until combined
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and whisk until combined
Spread batter evenly into prepared pan
Give the pan a shake to make sure the batter is level and reaches the corners
Bake for 17-18 minutes or until the cake springs back when you poke it with a finger
Meanwhile, lay a thin kitchen towel flat on the counter and sprinkle with 3/4 cup of confectioners’ sugar
Once the cake comes out of the oven, immediately invert it onto the towel
Peel off the parchment paper then, starting with the narrow end, begin rolling the cake up with the towel
Allow the cake to cool completely rolled up in the towel
Remove the cake roll from the refrigerator and allow it to sit on the counter for a few minutes to warm up as you prepare the frosting
To make the frosting: In a large bowl using a hand-held or stand mixer fitted with a paddle or whisk attachment, beat the cream cheese for 1 minute on high speed until completely smooth and creamy
Beat in the butter until combined
Add the confectioners’ sugar and vanilla and beat on medium-high speed until combined and creamy
Gently and slowly unroll the cake
Gently and slowly roll the cake back up, without the towel this time
Loosely cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 20 minutes before slicing and serving
Dust with more confectioners’ sugar, if desired

ingredients.raw: 2 ½ cups all purpose flour (I used King Arthur Unbleached AP Flour)
2 cups white chocolate chips (I used Ghirardelli)
When in doubt, take your brookies out early, as they will continue to cook as they cool

instructions.text: Ingredients
Line a large baking sheet with a silicon mat or parchment paper, then set aside
In a medium bowl, mix together the flour, cocoa, baking soda, powder, and salt
In a standing mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, cream the butter and sugars for 1-2 minutes, or until the sugar dissolves into the butter
Lower the speed to medium-low, and mix in the eggs, red food coloring, and vanilla
Gradually add in the flour mixture, beating until a little flour remains
Fold in the white chocolate chips with a rubber spatula
Divide the dough into 12 even pieces
Shape the dough roughly into a ball, but do not roll it
Place on the prepared baking sheet, spacing 2 inches apart
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or up to 12 hours before baking
Preheat oven to 375°F
Bake cookies for 17 minutes, until the brookies are soft to the touch
Cool on a wire rack, then serve

ingredients.raw: 1-2 ounce red food coloring more or less depending on how deep you want the color
1/2 cup plain hot coffee prepared (don't skip this ingredient)
Do not overbake as the cake will continue to cook as it cools
The warm cake will be very delicate

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 325°F
Generously grease and flour (2) 9-inch round cake pans, then set aside
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, baking powder, and salt, then set aside
In a large bowl, combine the sugar and vegetable oil
Mix in the eggs, buttermilk, vanilla, and red food coloring until combined
Combine the coffee with the cocoa powder and mix until smooth
Stir in the coffee/cocoa and white vinegar with the rest of the wet ingredients
Combine the wet ingredients with the dry ingredients a little at a time, mixing after each addition, just until combined
Pour the batter evenly into each pan
Bake in the middle rack for 30-40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out with moist crumbs clinging to it
Let pans cool on a cooling rack until the pans are warm to the touch
Slide a knife or offset spatula around the inside of the pans to loosen cakes from the pan
Gently remove the cakes from the pan and let them finish cooling
While the cakes are cooling make the frosting: In a large bowl cream together cream cheese and butter
Mix in vanilla extract
Gradually mix in powdered sugar
Let frosting set in the fridge for a few hours to firm up if needed
Frost the cake with cream cheese frosting when the cakes have cooled completely

recipes.title: Red Velvet Cake Bites
notes: Easy to split in half*

ingredients.raw: May want to add about 1/4 teaspoon canola oil to thin chocolate

instructions.text: Bake red velvet cake according to box instructions and let it cool
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper
Once cooled, break up cake into a crumbs
Add cream cheese, frosting and chocolate chips to cake and mix with a spoon or fork
Scoop tablespoons of mixture and roll into balls
Melt chocolate
Dip each ball into melted chocolate and place on baking sheet
While preparing the white chocolate to drizzle, place bites in refrigerator
Remove from refrigerator and drizzle each ball with white chocolate
Place back in refrigerator to harden

recipes.title: Salt and Buttered Popcorn - big bowl

ingredients.raw: 1 stick and a half of butter

instructions.text: Use peanut oil to barely cover the bottom of the pot
Stir it with one kernel until it pops to make sure it’s hot enough
Make the rest of the popcorn
Melt butter in a separate bowl
Drizzle 1/3 of the butter onto popcorn
Add salt
Take a knife and dig from bottom of bowl to bring dry popcorn to the top
Turn bowl 90° and do the same process
Repeat process 3 times

ingredients.raw: Make sure it is enough to fill the bottom of the pan, add more as necessary
Better to have enough on the bottom of the pan than to have it perfectly split

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish with cooking spray, line with parchment paper, then spray paper
In large bowl or in stand mixer, cream butter and sugar until light in color, starting at a low speed to combine, then increasing the speed to medium high to whip
Add eggs, one at a time, and vanilla and beat well
Combine flour, graham cracker crumbs, baking powder and salt in bowl and whisk to mix
Add flour mixture to wet mixture and beat until blended
Press about half of the dough into prepared pan
Bake for 15 minutes
Arrange chocolate bars over baked layer to fit (may have to break some up)
Sprinkle marshmallows over chocolate
Drop/scatter remaining dough over marshmallows
Bake 15-20 minutes until top is lightly browned
Cool completely and cut into bars

recipes.title: S'mores Brownie Pie
notes: Can cover and store leftover pie at room temperature for up to 3 days or in the refrigerator for up to 1 week
You can reheat slices for a few seconds in the microwave if desired

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ cups (180g) graham cracker crumbs (about 12 full-sheet graham crackers)
2 Tablespoons (30ml) vegetable oil or melted coconut oil
2 large eggs + 1 egg yolk, at room temperature
3/4 cup (62g) unsweetened natural or dutch-process cocoa powder
24–25 large marshmallows (1/2 of a standard package, or use mini marshmallows)
The mixture will be thick, coarse, and sandy
Do not pack down with heavy force because that leads to a hard crust
Simply pat down until the mixture is no longer crumby/crumbly
Batter will continue to thicken the longer it sits, so immediately spoon and spread evenly into crust
Do not walk away from the oven because toasting can happen fast and the marshmallows and crust can quickly burn
For extra neat slices, run a very sharp knife under hot water and wipe dry between slices

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F
Make the crust: Mix the graham cracker crumbs and granulated sugar together in a medium bowl, and then stir in the melted butter
Pour the mixture into an ungreased 9-inch deep dish pie dish
With medium pressure using your hand, pat the crumbs down into the bottom and up the sides to make a compact crust
Make the brownie batter immediately before spreading into crust: Slice the butter into Tablespoon-size pieces and melt in the microwave in a large heatproof bowl or in a small saucepan on the stove
If using stove, transfer melted butter to a large bowl
Whisk the oil and granulated sugar into the melted butter
Let it sit for 3-5 minutes to slightly cool
Whisk in 2 whole eggs, 1 egg yolk, and vanilla extract until combined
Add the cocoa powder, flour, and salt and whisk until combined
Fold in the chocolate chips
Bake for 30 minutes
Remove from the oven and arrange marshmallows on top, pressing each very lightly down into the filling to help them stick
Return pie to the oven and continue to bake for an additional 3 minutes to toast the marshmallows
If the marshmallows aren’t toasted to your liking after this time, turn on the broiler for 30-60 seconds and remove the pie as soon as the marshmallows look toasted
Remove pie from the oven and cool for at least 45 minutes on a wire rack
For best presentation and neatest slices, allow to cool for closer to 2 hours or cool completely
Slice strawberries and mix in a bowl with sugar and orange juice
Let it sit for at least a half hour
Slice a piece of cake, layer strawberries, pudding, more strawberries, and whipped cream

recipes.title: Strawberry Mousse
notes: Mousse will keep up to 48 hours

ingredients.raw: 2 pounds strawberries, hulled (6 ½ cups total)
1/2 cup cream (4 ounces) cream cheese, at room temperature, cut into 8 pieces
Berries and whipped cream to garnish, if desired

instructions.text: Reserve 3 strawberries to use as garnish
Pulse remaining strawberries in food processor in two batches until most pieces are between 1/4-1/2 inch thick (approximately 6-10 pulses)
Transfer the berries to a bowl and toss with 1/4 cup sugar and salt, then cover and let stand for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally
Place heavy cream in bowl of the stand mixer and refrigerate until ready to use
Strain strawberries through a fine-mesh strainer set in a small saucepan, rendering a minimum of 2/3 cup juice, then reserve juice, and do not wash bowl
Return strained strawberries to food processor and process until smooth 15-20 seconds
Strain puree through a fine-mesh strainer into a now empty bowl, pressing on solids to remove seeds and pulp until you have 1 ⅔ cup of puree
Place Chambord or raspberry liqueur in a small bowl and sprinkle gelatin over liqueur and let sit until gelatin softens about 5 minutes
Meanwhile, cook reserved juice over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until reduced to 3 tablespoons, about 10 minutes (syrup will be thick), then remove from heat
Pour gelatin mixture over hot syrup and whisk to dissolve
Add softened cream cheese and whisk vigorously until smooth
Strain mixture through fine-mesh strainer into a bowl, pressing on solids to remove any remaining lumps
Whisk in the 1 ⅔ cups strawberry puree until combined.
Using the stand mixer fitted with the whisk, whip chilled cream and remaining 1/4 cup of sugar until the cream holds peaks
Add berry mixture and mix on low until no white streaks remain, 10-15 seconds
Portion into dessert dishes and chill for at least 4 hours
Garnish with whipped cream and strawberry halves

ingredients.raw: 3/4 cup (170g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
Don’t worry too much about it being perfectly smooth, because it will soon be covered in frosting
Take them out when edges are golden brown, but center of the bar is still gooey
Add more confectioners’ sugar if frosting is too thin or more cream if frosting is too thick
Add a pinch of salt if frosting is too sweet - 1/8 - 1/4 teaspoon of salt

instructions.text: Make the cookies: Preheat the oven to 350°F
Line an 8x8-inch baking pan with parchment paper and set aside
Using a stand mixer fixed with the paddle attachment, beat the butter and sugar together until just combined
Add in the egg, milk, vanilla, and almond extract and beat well
Scrape down the sides of the bowl and add the salt and flour, beating for about 1 minute
Transfer the cookie dough to your prepared pan, spreading all the way to the edges and smoothing out the top
Bake the bars for 20-30 minutes
Make the frosting: With a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter on medium speed until creamy, about 2 minutes
Add confectioners’ sugar, cream, and vanilla extract with the mixer running on low
Increase to high speed and beat for 3 full minutes
Beat in food coloring, if using
Once cookie bars have cooled, frost the entire bar, then cut into slices and serve

ingredients.raw: 1/4 cup natural cocoa powder plus additional for dusting towel (25g+ additional)
1 tablespoon (15ml) strong brewed coffee *or* 1 teaspoon espresso powder
4 large eggs whites and yolks separated, room temperature

ingredientGroups.name: Filling (can use any filling of choice - whipped cream, buttercream frosting, etc)

ingredients.raw: 1 cup unsalted butter softened but not melty (226g)
1/2 cup heavy cream or heavy whipping cream
1 teaspoon light corn syrup (optional for glossy shine)
Be gentle, deflate your egg whites as little as possible, but the mixture should look smooth
This will keep the cake from sticking to it when you unroll and will prevent cracks
Be careful not to burn it!
Do not let it come to a rapid boil– that’s too hot
Feel free to spoon up any dripped ganache and spoon over the cake again

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 350°F and lightly spray a 10x15-inch jelly roll pan with baking spray, then line with parchment paper and spray the paper, then set aside
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, baking powder, salt
In a separate bowl, combine egg yolks, 1/2 cup (100g) granulated sugar, brown sugar, melted butter, coffee and vanilla extract
Whisk vigorously until mixture is lightened in color, about 1-2 minutes
Add flour mixture to the bowl and stir together until well-combined
In another large, completely clean, dry, and grease-free bowl, beat egg whites to soft peaks
Gradually, about 1 tablespoon at a time, add remaining 1/4 cup (50g) granulated sugar until you have stiff peaks
Briefly stir chocolate mixture in case it has thickened, then gently fold in egg whites until completely combined
Spread batter evenly into prepared pan and bake on the center rack of your oven for 15-18 minutes or until the cake springs back when very lightly touched
While cake is baking, lay a thin kitchen towel flat on the counter and place a piece of parchment paper on top
Generously and thoroughly dust the paper with about 2 tablespoons of cocoa powder
When the cake has finished baking, allow it to rest for 2 minutes then quickly and carefully invert onto prepared parchment paper lined towel
Carefully peel off parchment paper from bottom of cake
Now very gently, starting with one of the shorter ends of the cake, roll it into the towel to form a log
Very carefully transfer to a cooling rack and allow to cool completely
While cooling, prepare your filling.
To make the cream filling, combine sugar, flour, and salt in a medium-sized saucepan and whisk well to combine and remove any lumps from the flour
Cook for about 2 minutes over medium heat to toast the flour
Slowly add in your milk, whisk to combine, and bring your heat to medium-high
Bring the mixture to a simmer, whisk continuously until it is thick and pudding-like
Once it's simmering, cook for one minute more

Remove from heat and pour into a heat-proof container and cover the surface with a piece of plastic wrap, wax paper, or parchment paper pressed directly against the surface (to prevent a skin from forming)
Allow to cool completely to room temperature
Once flour mixture has cooled, use an electric mixer or stand mixer to beat butter on high speed until it is light and fluffy, about 3 minutes

Reduce mixer speed to medium and gradually add flour mixture, one heaping spoonful at a time, waiting until each spoonful is incorporated before adding the next and beating until all ingredients are well-combined
Scrape sides and bottom of bowl with a spatula, then stir in vanilla extract
Gradually increase speed to medium-high and whip frosting until smooth, light, and airy
Once the swiss roll has cooled, very carefully unroll it in the towel
Don’t worry about any small cracks, you can patch them together with your icing
Evenly spread filling over the roll then very carefully re-roll the log
Transfer to a wax-paper lined serving platter and place in the fridge while you prepare the chocolate glaze
To make topping, place chopped chocolate and corn syrup, if using, in a medium bowl
Heat the cream in a small saucepan until it begins to gently simmer
Pour over chocolate, then let it sit for 2-3 minutes to gently soften the chocolate
Slowly stir until completely combined and chocolate has melted
Pour ganache all over cake roll
Refrigerate for at least 30-60 minutes before slicing and serving

recipes.title: Vanilla Buttercream Frosting
notes: Makes enough for a 2-layer cake plus some*

ingredients.raw: 1 ¼ cups (2 ½ sticks) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
May not need to use all the heavy cream, as you don’t want the frosting to be too thin
Add more confectioners’ sugar if frosting is too thin, more cream if frosting is too thick, or a pinch more of salt if frosting is way too sweet

instructions.text: In a large bowl using a hand-held mixer or stand mixer fitted with a whisk or paddle attachment, beat the butter on medium speed until creamy, about 2 minutes
Add vanilla extract and salt with mixer running on low
Slowly and in increments, add confectioners’ sugar and cream, a little bit at a time with the mixer still on low, and repeat this process until all sugar is added
Increase to high speed and beat for 3 full minutes

ingredients.note: 390 grams flour plus 50 grams cornstarch

ingredients.raw: 1 ½ cups (3 sticks) unsalted butter, plus more for greasing softened to room temperature
3 large eggs plus 2 additional egg whites, at room temperature
1 ½ cups buttermilk, at room temperature*
Zest from half an orange or ⅛ teaspoon almond extract (optional)
Scrape down the sides and up the bottom of the bowl with a rubber spatula as needed
Mixture will look curdled as a result of the egg liquid and solid butter combining
Scrape down the sides and up the bottom of the bowl as needed
You may need to whisk it all by hand at the end to make sure there are no lumps at the bottom of the bowl
The batter will be slightly thick
Allow cakes to cool completely in the pans set on a wire rack before frosting and assembling

instructions.text: Before starting, make sure items are at room temperature
Preheat oven to 350°F
Grease three 9-inch or two 8-inch cake pans with butter, line with parchment paper, then grease the parchment paper and sides of the pan
Whisk the cake flour, salt, baking powder, and baking soda together, then set aside
Using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a paddle or whisk attachment, beat the butter and sugar together on high speed until smooth and creamy, about 3 minutes
Beat in the eggs, egg whites, orange or almond and vanilla extract on high speed until combined, for about 2 minutes
With the mixer on low speed, add the dry ingredients just until combine
With the mixer still running on low, pour in the buttermilk and mix just until combined
Pour batter evenly into cake pans and weigh them to ensure accuracy, if desired
Bake for about 23-26 minutes for 9-inch cakes and 28-30 minutes for 8-inch cakes or until the cakes are baked through

ingredients.raw: 1 ¾ cups (420ml) whole milk or almond milk
2 Tablespoons (28g) unsalted butter, softened to room temperature
You can skip this step if you don't notice any lumps in your pudding

instructions.text: Combine the milk, heavy cream, sugar, and salt in a medium saucepan over medium heat
Whisk until all the sugar has dissolved, then bring to a gentle simmer, whisking occasionally
Working quickly as the milk mixture begins to simmer, whisk the egg yolks and cornstarch together in a medium heatproof bowl until thick and smooth
Very slowly stream in 1 cup (240ml) of the simmering milk to slightly warm the egg yolk mixture
Then, in a very slow stream, whisk the egg yolk mixture into the pan
While continuously whisking, cook until the mixture is thick and big bubbles begin bursting at the surface, about 1 minute
Remove the pan from heat and whisk in the vanilla extract and butter
Place a fine mesh strainer over a large heatproof bowl, then pour the mixture through the strainer and into the bowl
Transfer the pudding into a large bowl or into individual serving bowls
Place a piece of plastic wrap directly on top of the warm pudding to prevent a “skin” from forming on top
Refrigerate for several hours or until chilled

recipes.title: Whoopie Pie
notes: Whoopie pies can be refrigerated for up to 3 days

ingredients.raw: 1 cup packed (7 ounces) light brown sugar
Filling can be refrigerated for up to 2 days

instructions.text: Adjust oven racks to upper-middle and lower-middle positions and heat oven to 350°
Line two baking sheets with parchment paper
Whisk flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt together in a bowl, then set aside
